title: fix: switch gRPC to shared linking and exclude unsecure variants
body:
## Summary

- Switch gRPC to shared linking (`grpc:shared=True`) to prevent `libmilvus_core.so` and `libmilvus-common.so` from each embedding a full gRPC runtime, which causes metric duplicate registration crash on Linux
- Set `grpc:secure=True` to exclude `grpc_unsecure` and `grpc++_unsecure` from `CONAN_LIBS`, preventing both secure and unsecure gRPC variants from being loaded simultaneously

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48457

## Test plan

- [x] Built VDC image with fix, verified `libmilvus_core.so` no longer links `grpc_unsecure`
- [x] Verified fixed image starts normally on Linux (no SIGABRT)
- [x] Verified old image without fix crashes with `Metric name grpc.lb.pick_first.disconnections has already been registered`


🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: switch gRPC to shared linking and exclude unsecure variants

issue: #48457

Switch gRPC to shared linking (grpc:shared=True) to prevent multiple
shared libraries (libmilvus_core.so, libmilvus-common.so) from each
embedding a full gRPC runtime, which causes metric duplicate registration
crash on Linux.

Set grpc:secure=True to exclude grpc_unsecure and grpc++_unsecure from
CONAN_LIBS, preventing both secure and unsecure gRPC variants from being
loaded simultaneously which also causes duplicate metric registration.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

chore: trigger CI rebuild

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: add LSAN suppression for gRPC global singleton leak

When gRPC is dynamically linked (libgrpc.so.44), its global
AuditLoggerRegistry singleton—allocated via `static T* = new T()`
and intentionally never destroyed—triggers LeakSanitizer false
positives. This happens because LSAN cannot scan the .bss segment
of uninstrumented shared libraries to find reachable pointers,
unlike static linking where the pointer lives in the main binary's
.bss and is recognized as reachable.

This is a known gRPC design choice (grpc/grpc#40389, grpc/grpc#24488)
to avoid the C++ static destruction order fiasco.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: feat: [ExternalTable Part5] Enable loading and querying external collections
body:
## Summary

- Implement virtual primary key (VirtualPK) for external collections without user-defined PK
- Add ExternalSegmentCandidate for PK oracle to support segment pruning on external segments
- Support loading external segments via manifest-based sealed segment loader
- Add ManifestGroupTranslator with external field name fallback for column mapping
- Add lance-table and vortex format support with HTTP scheme fix for MinIO endpoints
- Fix extfs boolean property type mismatch by disabling extfs.* bool properties
- Add lance and vortex format E2E tests with full query/search coverage
- Add Python data generation scripts and CI Docker Python environment

issue: #45881

design doc: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus-design-docs/blob/main/design_docs/20260105-external_table.md

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests pass (pkoracle, segments, delegator, datanode/external)
- [x] E2E tests pass (13/13): TestCreateExternal*, TestRefreshExternal*, TestExternalCollectionLanceFormat, TestExternalCollectionVortexFormat
- [x] `make milvus` compiles successfully
- [x] `make lint-fix` passes with no auto-fixes
- [ ] CI checks pass
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
feat: [ExternalTable Part5] Enable loading, querying and indexing external collections

issue: #45881

- Implement virtual primary key (VirtualPK) for external collections without user-defined PK
- Add ExternalSegmentCandidate for PK oracle to support segment pruning on external segments
- Support loading external segments via manifest-based sealed segment loader
- Add ManifestGroupTranslator with external field name fallback for column mapping
- Add lance-table and vortex format support with HTTP scheme fix for MinIO endpoints
- Support index building and loading for external table fields (StorageVersion=V3,
  schema-based IsExternalCollection detection, external_field() name in manifest)
- Eagerly update job state when refresh task reaches terminal state to eliminate
  race window in HasActiveJob
- Enable lazy loading for external collection column groups
- Fix macOS C++ logging with __attribute__((weak)) for goZapLogExt
- Add Python data generation scripts and CI Docker Python environment
- Add comprehensive unit tests and E2E tests with scalar+vector index verification

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: Guard vector normalization to external fields only in ManifestGroupTranslator

issue: #47974

NormalizeVectorArraysToFixedSizeBinary() was applied to all collections
including normal ones. Normal collections with nullable vector fields store
vectors as BINARY (not FIXED_SIZE_BINARY), causing assertion failures and
SIGABRT during load_collection. This broke all NGRAM index tests in CI.

Adding is_external_field() check ensures normalization only runs for external
collection fields where Parquet stores vectors as list-of-float.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

test: add unit tests and fix review findings for external table Part5

issue: #47974

- Fix BatchPkExist nil pkCandidate panic (add nil guard consistent with MayPkExist)
- Fix EnsureMaterialized missing offset mask in VirtualPKChunkedColumn
- Extract ComputeVirtualPK private helper to centralize 4 inlined call sites
- Extract detectVirtualPKCollisions helper and add tests (5 sub-tests)
- Extract extractSegmentIDFromVirtualPK helper in pkoracle to avoid duplication
- Add newTestBaseSegment helper to reduce test fragility
- Add tests for external collection delta log skip and IsExternalCollection
- Add tests for injectVirtualPKForExternalCollection (4 sub-tests)
- Add tests for GetColumnNamesFromSchema external collection logic
- Add tests for separateLoadInfoV2 external field handling (4 tests)
- Add tests for baseSegment pkCandidate wrapper methods
- Split Dockerfile pip install into required and optional packages

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: normalize external vector data in index build path

The index build reads external collection data via GetFieldDatasFromManifest
which directly passes Parquet's List<float> arrays to FillFieldData. However,
FillFieldData expects FixedSizeBinary for vector fields. Add normalization
(List/FixedSizeList → FixedSizeBinary) in GetFieldDatasFromManifest for
external vector fields, matching the existing normalization in
ManifestGroupTranslator::TranslateFromStorageV3.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: restore packed.StatsResolver for bloom filter and BM25 stats loading

The previous code replaced packed.StatsResolver with direct Statslogs field
access (filterPKStatsBinlogs/filterBM25Stats), which broke bloom filter loading
when storage v2 is enabled (useLoonFFI=true). With storage v2, stats are stored
in the manifest rather than in loadInfo.Statslogs, so the direct access returns
empty paths and bloom filters are never loaded.

This caused delete and upsert operations to silently fail — deletes were
acknowledged but not applied during query, and upserts created duplicate rows
instead of replacing existing ones.

Restore packed.NewStatsResolverFromLoadInfo() which correctly handles both
manifest-based (storage v2) and legacy (Statslogs) paths for:
- BloomFilterPaths (3 call sites)
- BloomFilterMemorySize (1 call site)
- BM25StatsPaths (2 call sites)

Also restore loadBloomFilter to use DeserializeBloomFilterStats which handles
compound stats type detection internally via path inspection.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: fix: handle full-size format in GetDataIterator for nullable GROUP BY fields [2.6]
body:
pr: #48219

## Summary
- Cherry-pick of #48219 to the 2.6 branch
- Fixes `GetDataIterator` to handle both full-size and compact nullable data formats, which caused incorrect GROUP BY results (returning more results than the specified `group_size`)

## Related Issue
https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/46734

## Test plan
- [x] Existing unit tests updated (`TestGetDataIterator`, `TestTaskSearch_reduceGroupBySearchResultData`)
- [ ] Verify `test_hybrid_search_group_size` and `test_search_pagination_group_size` pass in 2.6 nightly CI
author: NicoYuan1986

commits.message:
fix: handle full-size format in GetDataIterator for nullable GROUP BY fields (#48219)

## Summary
- `GetDataIterator` only handled compact format (data array contains
only valid entries) but C++ segcore produces full-size format
(Resize+Set, data array same length as ValidData, zero-filled at null
positions) for numeric GROUP BY field values (INT8/16/32/64, BOOL,
TIMESTAMPTZ)
- This caused wrong group-by values after null positions during search
reduce, leading to incomplete/incorrect group search results in
distributed mode (multiple queryNodes)
- Fix: auto-detect format by comparing scalar data array length with
ValidData length. Full-size → direct indexing; compact → mapped indexing
(original logic preserved)
- Promote two group_by test cases from L2 to L0 so they run in CI to
catch regressions

## Root Cause
In `ReduceSearchResultData` (queryNode level) and
`reduceSearchResultDataWithGroupBy` (proxy level), `GetDataIterator` is
called on `GroupByFieldValue`. The old code assumed compact format (only
valid entries in data array) and built index mappings accordingly. But
for numeric types, C++ segcore uses `Resize+Set` (full-size format), so
the data array has the same length as ValidData with zeros at null
positions. The wrong mapping caused values after null positions to shift
left, producing incorrect group-by keys and broken grouping logic.

## Files Changed
- `pkg/util/typeutil/schema.go`: Fix `GetDataIterator` to auto-detect
format + add `getScalarDataLen` helper
- `pkg/util/typeutil/schema_test.go`: Add 4 test cases covering
int64/varchar × compact/full-size combinations
- `tests/python_client/.../test_milvus_client_search_group_by.py`:
Promote `test_search_group_by_supported_scalars` and
`test_search_group_by_non_exit_field_on_dynamic_enabled_collection` from
L2 to L0

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/46734

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests pass: `go test -tags dynamic,test -gcflags="all=-N -l"
./util/typeutil/... -run TestGetDataIterator` (6 sub-tests, all pass)
- [ ] CI e2e tests (L0) pass in distributed mode — validates the fix
end-to-end

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Signed-off-by: nico <cheng.yuan@zilliz.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix: remove dangling TraceContext pointer storage in SealedIndexTranslator (#48494)
body:
SealedIndexTranslator stored a TraceContext member containing raw traceID/spanID pointers originating from Go memory. These pointers dangle when get_cells() is called during async warmup or cache re-population, causing heap-buffer-overflow detected by ASAN.

Remove the TraceContext member and use an empty TraceContext in get_cells() instead, since the original request's trace context is meaningless for deferred index loading.

issue: #48494
author: chyezh

commits.message:
fix: add global OPENTELEMETRY_STL_VERSION to fix nostd::shared_ptr ABI mismatch

milvus-common defines OPENTELEMETRY_STL_VERSION=2017 (PUBLIC), which
makes nostd::shared_ptr an alias for std::shared_ptr (16 bytes). However,
Milvus core OBJECT libraries (segcore, index, query, etc.) never receive
this define because CMake TARGET_OBJECTS does not propagate transitive
compile definitions from link targets.

Without the define, these translation units use the custom nostd::shared_ptr
implementation with a 32-byte PlacementBuffer — a completely different
memory layout. This ODR violation causes heap-buffer-overflow detected by
ASAN when OTel spans are created/copied across the ABI boundary (e.g.
during async cache warmup in SealedIndexTranslator::get_cells).

Fix by adding add_compile_definitions(OPENTELEMETRY_STL_VERSION=2017) at
the top-level CMakeLists.txt so all translation units use the same layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: chyezh <chyezh@outlook.com>

author: lyyyuna

reviews.body:
## Review Comments

**1. Missing trailing newline**

`test_milvus_client_insert.py` is missing a trailing newline at end of file (diff shows `\ No newline at end of file`). Please add one.

**2. `test_insert_row_data` and `test_insert_single_row` can be merged**

These two tests have identical logic — the only difference is `nb`. Suggest merging with parametrize:

```python
@pytest.mark.tags(CaseLabel.L0)
@pytest.mark.parametrize("nb", [1, default_nb])
def test_insert_row_data(self, nb):
```

`nb=1` is still covered as a boundary case, and CI can distinguish failures via `test_insert_row_data[1]` / `test_insert_row_data[3000]`.

**3. Extra fields in `test_insert_row_data` go into dynamic field, not schema fields**

The collection is created via `self.create_collection(client, collection_name, default_dim)`, which only defines `pk + vector` (+ dynamic field enabled). However, the inserted rows include `default_float_field_name` and `default_string_field_name` — these will be stored in the dynamic field rather than as schema-defined fields.

- If the intent is to test schema field insertion → create the collection with an explicit schema that includes these fields.
- If the intent is to test dynamic field insertion → update the test name and docstring to make this clear.

title: fix: validate clustering key type in addCollectionFieldTask
body:
## What this PR does

`addCollectionFieldTask.PreExecute()` was missing the `IsClusteringKeyType()` validation that was added to `createCollectionTask.validateClusteringKey()` in PR #48184.

This allowed users to add fields with unsupported data types (JSON, Bool, Array, etc.) as clustering keys via the `AddCollectionField` (schema evolution) API. During clustering compaction, `NewScalarFieldValue()` panics on these types, crashing the DataNode.

## Root cause

PR #48184 fixed the type check only in the collection-creation path. The schema-evolution path (`addCollectionFieldTask`) was not updated with the same guard.

## Fix

Add `typeutil.IsClusteringKeyType()` check before the duplicate clustering-key loop in `addCollectionFieldTask.PreExecute()`, consistent with `createCollectionTask.validateClusteringKey()`.

```go
if t.fieldSchema.GetIsClusteringKey() {
    if !typeutil.IsClusteringKeyType(t.fieldSchema.GetDataType()) {
        return merr.WrapErrParameterInvalidMsg(...)  // ← added
    }
    for _, f := range t.oldSchema.Fields { ... }    // existing duplicate check
}
```

## Tests

- Added test cases for JSON, Bool, and Array fields with `IsClusteringKey=true` — all must return `ErrParameterInvalid`
- Added positive test case: valid Int64 clustering key must be accepted
- Fixed pre-existing "more ClusteringKey field" test to use explicit `DataType_Int64` so it exercises the duplicate-key branch (not the new type check)

issue: #48285
author: bigsheeper

commits.message:
fix: validate clustering key type in addCollectionFieldTask

PR #48184 added IsClusteringKeyType() validation only in
createCollectionTask.validateClusteringKey(). The same check was
missing from addCollectionFieldTask.PreExecute(), allowing JSON,
Bool, and Array fields to be added as clustering keys via schema
evolution (AddCollectionField API).

Without this check, a user could add an unsupported type as a
clustering key, which would later panic in NewScalarFieldValue()
during clustering compaction, crashing the DataNode.

Fix: add IsClusteringKeyType() check before the duplicate
clustering-key check, consistent with createCollectionTask.

issue: #48285

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Yihao Dai <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>

  DataType=Int64 and Nullable=true so the test exercises the
  duplicate-key check branch (previously DataType_None triggered
  the new type check instead)
- Add positive test: valid Int64 clustering key must be accepted
  without error

title: enhance: [2.6] speed up MixCoord recovery by parallelizing startup phases
body:
## Summary

Cherry-pick of #47784 to the 2.6 branch.

issue: #47783
pr: #47784

Optimize standalone MixCoord recovery to reduce startup time:

1. **Parallelize DataCoord and QueryCoord startup**: use errgroup to run DC and QC init+start concurrently since they only depend on RootCoord being ready.

2. **Parallelize DataCoord sub-meta loading**: load all sub-metas (indexMeta, analyzeMeta, partitionStatsMeta, compactionTaskMeta, statsTaskMeta) concurrently via errgroup instead of sequentially.

3. **Parallelize DataCoord sub-meta and reloadFromKV**: run reloadFromKV (ListSegments, ListChannelCheckpoint) as a goroutine alongside sub-meta loading since they operate on independent data structures.

4. **Parallelize indexMeta reload**: run ListIndexes and ListSegmentIndexes concurrently with their memory updates in the same goroutines.

5. **Batch delete TargetManager targets**: replace per-collection RemoveCollectionTarget calls with a single RemoveCollectionTargets (RemoveWithPrefix) call after in-memory recovery.

6. **Batch etcd loading for ListCollections**: replace sequential per-key etcd reads with a single LoadWithPrefix call in RootCoord's initMetaTable.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>
author: sijie-ni-0214

commits.message:
enhance: speed up MixCoord recovery by parallelizing startup phases

Optimize standalone MixCoord recovery to reduce startup time:

1. Parallelize DataCoord and QueryCoord startup: use errgroup to run
   DC and QC init+start concurrently since they only depend on
   RootCoord being ready.

2. Parallelize DataCoord sub-meta loading: load all 6 sub-metas
   (indexMeta, analyzeMeta, partitionStatsMeta, compactionTaskMeta,
   statsTaskMeta, snapshotMeta) concurrently via errgroup instead of
   sequentially.

3. Parallelize DataCoord sub-meta and reloadFromKV: run reloadFromKV
   (ListSegments, ListChannelCheckpoint) as a 7th goroutine alongside
   sub-meta loading since they operate on independent data structures.

4. Parallelize indexMeta reload: run ListIndexes and ListSegmentIndexes
   concurrently with their memory updates in the same goroutines, as
   they write to independent data structures (m.indexes vs
   m.segmentIndexes/m.segmentBuildInfo).

5. Batch delete TargetManager targets: replace per-collection
   RemoveCollectionTarget calls with a single RemoveCollectionTargets
   (RemoveWithPrefix) call after in-memory recovery.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

enhance: speed up indexMeta recovery by loading segment indexes per collection

ListSegmentIndexes previously scanned all segment indexes with a single
prefix walk. Add a collectionID parameter and load per-collection in
parallel using conc.Pool, matching the pattern already used by
ListSegments.

Extract showCollectionIDs helper from meta.reloadFromKV so both
reloadFromKV and newIndexMeta can share the collection ID list.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

enhance: async Prometheus metrics update during indexMeta recovery

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

fix: add Maybe() to ListSegmentIndexes mock in import_checker_test

After ListSegmentIndexes was changed to per-collection calls, it won't
be invoked when there are no collections. Add Maybe() to prevent mock
expectation failures.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

fix: resolve data race in parallel meta loading and fix mock expectations

1. Replace errgroup.WithContext with plain errgroup.Group in newMeta and
   indexMeta.reloadFromKV since the derived context is unused. This
   avoids a data race between context.propagateCancel modifying the
   parent context and testify mock reading context via reflection.

2. Add ListFileResource and ListSegments mock expectations with Maybe()
   to error test cases in TestReloadFromKV. With parallel loading,
   reloadFromKV goroutine may call these methods even when a sub-meta
   loading goroutine fails.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

fix: close balancer in TestBalancer_WithRecoveryLag to prevent data race

The test was missing b.Close(), causing its background goroutine to
continue reading the global resource.r variable while the next test
(TestBalancer_DynamicChannelFromProvider) called resource.InitForTest()
to write to the same variable, triggering a data race detected by
the race detector in CI.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

enhance: conditionally skip taskStats LRU insert during indexMeta recovery

Skip the LRU write only when the LRU is already full and the index task
is finished. Unfinished tasks are always inserted so they remain visible
for monitoring.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

title: fix: clear stale sessions in sessionDiscoverer.initDiscover on retry
body:
When etcd watch breaks (compaction/network) and initDiscover retries, sessions deleted during the watch gap persisted forever because initDiscover only added entries to peerSessions without clearing old ones. This caused the resolver to permanently report dead nodes as alive, blocking the streaming balancer indefinitely.

Fix: reset peerSessions map before re-populating from etcd Get response.

issue: #48564
pr: #48566
author: chyezh

commits.message:
fix: clear stale sessions in sessionDiscoverer.initDiscover on retry (#48564)

When etcd watch breaks (compaction/network) and initDiscover retries,
sessions deleted during the watch gap persisted forever because
initDiscover only added entries to peerSessions without clearing old
ones. This caused the resolver to permanently report dead nodes as
alive, blocking the streaming balancer indefinitely.

Fix: reset peerSessions map before re-populating from etcd Get response.

issue: #48564

- Pipeline-based query reduction at QN/Delegator/Proxy levels

title: fix: reject compaction requests without params instead of falling back to defaults
body:
Remove legacy compatibility code that silently generated default v3 compaction params when the request from an old datacoord had empty params. This caused datanode to produce v2 segments unexpectedly. Now ParseParamsFromJSON returns an error on empty input, forcing the caller to provide valid compaction params.

Remove legacy compatibility code that silently generated default v3
compaction params when the request from an old datacoord had empty
params. This caused datanode to produce v2 segments unexpectedly.
Now ParseParamsFromJSON returns an error on empty input, forcing
the caller to provide valid compaction params.

title: fix: fix swallowed errors and missing error handling in multiple modules
body:
## Summary
- **validate_util.go:653**: `return nil` → `return err` in TimestamptzData branch of `FillWithDefaultValue`. All other branches correctly return `err`; this was a copy-paste bug that silently swallows errors when filling default values for timestamptz fields.
- **print_binlog.go:62**: `return nil` → `return err` when `mmap.Open` fails. All other error paths in the same function correctly return `err`.
- **delta_data.go:228**: Add `default` case in `DeleteLog.UnmarshalJSON()` switch to return error on unsupported PK types, preventing nil pointer panic on line 230.
- **segment_writer.go:436**: Check and log error from `newBinlogWriter()` in `clear()` instead of ignoring with `_`. If this fails, `w.writer` becomes nil causing panic on next use.
- **insert_node.go:48**: Fix wrong format specifier `%d` → `%v` for error type. `%d` produces garbled output like `%!d(*errors.errorString=...)`.

## Test plan
- [ ] Existing unit tests pass (no behavioral change for success paths)
- [ ] Each fix is a minimal one-line change with clear before/after comparison

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/39893

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: liliu-z

commits.message:
fix: fix swallowed errors and missing error handling in multiple modules

- validate_util.go: return err instead of nil in TimestamptzData branch of
  FillWithDefaultValue, which silently swallowed fillWithDefaultValueImpl errors
- print_binlog.go: return err instead of nil when mmap.Open fails
- delta_data.go: add default case in UnmarshalJSON switch to prevent nil pointer
  panic on unsupported primary key types
- segment_writer.go: check and log error from newBinlogWriter in clear() instead
  of ignoring it, which could lead to nil pointer panic
- insert_node.go: fix wrong format specifier %d to %v for error type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: [loon] support V3 storage segment GC with basePath-based deletion
body:
Related to #44956

V3 (loon/manifest) segments store all data under a single basePath (insert_log/{coll}/{part}/{seg}/) with _data/, _metadata/, _stats/, _delta/ subdirectories. The existing GC only handled V1/V2 per-file binlog paths, leaving V3 segment files leaked after drop.

- recycleDroppedSegments: add V3 branch that uses RemoveWithPrefix(basePath) to recursively delete the entire segment directory, instead of removing individual log files which are incomplete for V3 (stats/delta/metadata are embedded in manifest, not tracked in proto fields)
- recycleUnusedBinlogFiles: skip V3 segment files during orphan scan, as V3 orphan file lifecycle is managed by loon (milvus-storage)
- Add parseV3SegmentID helper to extract segmentID from variable-depth V3 paths that fail ParseSegmentIDByBinlog's fixed-depth format
- Use StorageVersion == StorageV3 as the canonical V3 check
author: congqixia

commits.message:
enhance: [loon] support V3 storage segment GC with basePath-based deletion

Related to #44956

V3 (loon/manifest) segments store all data under a single basePath
(insert_log/{coll}/{part}/{seg}/) with _data/, _metadata/, _stats/,
_delta/ subdirectories. The existing GC only handled V1/V2 per-file
binlog paths, leaving V3 segment files leaked after drop.

- recycleDroppedSegments: add V3 branch that uses RemoveWithPrefix(basePath)
to recursively delete the entire segment directory, instead of removing
individual log files which are incomplete for V3 (stats/delta/metadata
are embedded in manifest, not tracked in proto fields)
- recycleUnusedBinlogFiles: skip V3 segment files during orphan scan,
as V3 orphan file lifecycle is managed by loon (milvus-storage)
- Add parseV3SegmentID helper to extract segmentID from variable-depth
V3 paths that fail ParseSegmentIDByBinlog's fixed-depth format
- Use StorageVersion == StorageV3 as the canonical V3 check

Signed-off-by: Congqi Xia <congqi.xia@zilliz.com>

title: fix: add nil check in CloseFFIReader to prevent heap-use-after-free on double close
body:
Guard against double-close in both C++ and Go layers of FFIPackedReader. The C++ CloseFFIReader now returns early when given a nullptr, and the Go Close() method nil-checks before calling into C and nils out the pointer after close. This prevents ASAN heap-use-after-free crashes when Close() or Release() is called more than once.

issue: milvus-io/milvus-storage#456
author: congqixia

commits.message:
fix: add nil check in CloseFFIReader to prevent heap-use-after-free on double close

Guard against double-close in both C++ and Go layers of FFIPackedReader.
The C++ CloseFFIReader now returns early when given a nullptr, and the Go
Close() method nil-checks before calling into C and nils out the pointer
after close. This prevents ASAN heap-use-after-free crashes when Close()
or Release() is called more than once.

issue: milvus-io/milvus-storage#456

Signed-off-by: Congqi Xia <congqi.xia@zilliz.com>

title: fix: clone arrow string/binary values to prevent dangling references
body:
issue: #48297

Arrow array Value() returns references to underlying buffers. When
records are released (especially CGo-backed packed readers), these
references become dangling pointers causing use-after-free.

- NewVarCharPrimaryKey now always clones input string via
  strings.Clone, making all callers safe by default
- Direct VarCharPrimaryKey struct inits from arrow arrays replaced
  with constructor call (segment_writer, stats_collector)
- Clone string map key in binlog delta reader
- Copy sparse vector binary value in payload reader
- Fix clustering compactor shouldCopy=false to true since values
  are buffered in SerializeWriterImpl beyond record lifetime
- Clone arrow string values in parquet struct field reader
- Remove redundant strings.Clone in compaction/common.go since
  NewVarCharPrimaryKey now clones internally

Signed-off-by: Ted Xu <ted.xu@zilliz.com>
author: tedxu

commits.message:
fix: clone arrow string/binary values to prevent dangling references

Arrow array Value() returns references to underlying buffers. When
records are released (especially CGo-backed packed readers), these
references become dangling pointers causing use-after-free.

- NewVarCharPrimaryKey now always clones input string
- Direct VarCharPrimaryKey struct inits from arrow arrays replaced
  with constructor call
- Clone string map key in binlog delta reader
- Copy sparse vector binary value in payload reader
- Fix clustering compactor shouldCopy=false to true since values
  are buffered in SerializeWriterImpl beyond record lifetime

title: enhance: optimize LIKE pattern matching with SmartPatternMatcher replacing regex
body: issue: #47307
author: xiaofan-luan

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request significantly enhances LIKE pattern matching performance by introducing `SmartPatternMatcher` and integrating the `RE2` library. The `SmartPatternMatcher` provides an optimized, non-regex approach for standard LIKE patterns, while `RE2` offers a faster regex engine where complex patterns necessitate it. The refactoring is comprehensive, touching various index types and expression evaluation paths, and includes a consistent renaming of `*RegexQuery` methods to `*PatternQuery` for clarity. Extensive new test cases have been added to ensure correctness and validate the performance benefits of the new implementation, including thorough checks for edge cases and escaped characters. Overall, this is a well-executed and impactful optimization.

commits.message:
enhance: optimize LIKE pattern matching with LikePatternMatcher and fix escaping bugs

title: fix: upgrade Go to 1.25.8 for CVE-2025-68121, CVE-2026-27142, CVE-2026-25679
body:
issue: #48574

## Summary
- Upgrade Go from 1.24.12 to 1.25.8 across all go.mod files and Dockerfiles
- Fixes CVE-2025-68121 (CRITICAL), CVE-2026-27142 (HIGH), CVE-2026-25679 (HIGH) in Go stdlib
- All three CVEs affect the Go standard library and are resolved by upgrading to Go 1.25.8

## Changes
- Updated `go` directive in go.mod files (root, pkg/, client/, tests/go_client/, examples/)
- Updated Go download URLs in Dockerfiles (build/docker/builder/)
- Updated `toolchain` directives where present

title: enhance: add CRC32C checksum and TLS support for object storage
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48360 https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48359

Add CRC32C checksum validation for MinIO/S3 PutObject requests and enhance TLS configuration for object storage connections.

- Add minio.ssl.useCRC32C config option to enable CRC32C checksum on PutObject requests for data integrity validation
- Pass TLS minimum version to GoogleHttpClientFactory and add TlsHttpClientFactory for non-GCP storage when TLS is configured
- Add use_crc32c_checksum field to StorageConfig proto and propagate through CGO layers
- Bump milvus-storage dependency
author: jiaqizho

commits.message:
enhance: add CRC32C checksum and TLS support for object storage

Add CRC32C checksum validation for MinIO/S3 PutObject requests and enhance TLS configuration for object storage connections.

- Add minio.ssl.useCRC32C config option to enable CRC32C checksum on PutObject requests for data integrity validation
- Pass TLS minimum version to GoogleHttpClientFactory and add TlsHttpClientFactory for non-GCP storage when TLS is configured
- Add use_crc32c_checksum field to StorageConfig proto and propagate through CGO layers
- Bump milvus-storage dependency

Signed-off-by: jiaqizho <jiaqi.zhou@zilliz.com>

title: feat: implement data salvage for force failover
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47598

design doc: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus-design-docs/blob/main/design_docs/20260205-data_salvage_for_force_failover.md

## Summary

Implement data salvage feature that enables zero data loss after force failover by capturing and exposing the salvage checkpoint, along with a DumpMessages API to retrieve unsynchronized messages.

### SalvageCheckpoint Capture & Persistence
- Add `salvageCheckpoint` field to `ReplicateManager`
- Capture last synced position on force promote before clearing secondary state
- Persist to separate etcd key via `StreamingNodeCataLog`
- Load salvage checkpoint on WAL recovery

### API Exposure
- Return `salvage_checkpoint` in `GetReplicateInfoResponse`
- Add `GetSalvageCheckpoint` to `HandlerClient` and `ReplicateService`

### DumpMessages Streaming API
- Implement `DumpMessages` RPC for streaming WAL messages
- Support `start_timetick` and `end_timetick` filters
- Filter out system messages (TimeTick, CreateSegment, Flush, RollbackTxn)
- Use oneof response for status or message

### Tests
- Unit tests for salvage checkpoint capture
- Unit tests for `shouldDumpMessage` filter
- Integration tests for `GetReplicateInfo` and `DumpMessages`

## Test plan
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] Manual test with force failover scenario

proto pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus-proto/pull/568

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
author: bigsheeper

commits.message:
feat: Support force promote for primary-secondary failover

Add force_promote flag to UpdateReplicateConfiguration API for disaster recovery.

Changes:

- Add ForcePromote field to UpdateReplicateConfigurationRequest

- Refactor UpdateReplicateConfiguration to accept request object instead of separate params

- Add WithForcePromote() method to ReplicateConfigurationBuilder

- Implement force promote validation and handling in assignment service

- Add integration tests for force promote scenarios

Force promote allows a secondary cluster to immediately become standalone primary

when the original primary is unavailable, enabling active-passive failover.

Signed-off-by: bigsheeper <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>

feat(salvage): implement data salvage feature for force failover

This PR implements the data salvage feature that enables zero data loss
after force failover by capturing and exposing the salvage checkpoint.

SalvageCheckpoint Capture:
- Add salvageCheckpoint field to ReplicateManager
- Capture last synced position on force promote before clearing secondary state
- Add GetSalvageCheckpoint() method to access captured checkpoint

SalvageCheckpoint Persistence:
- Add SaveSalvageCheckpoint/GetSalvageCheckpoint to StreamingNodeCataLog
- Persist to separate etcd key on force promote
- Load salvage checkpoint on WAL recovery

API Exposure:
- Return salvage checkpoint in GetReplicateInfoResponse
- Add GetSalvageCheckpoint to HandlerClient and ReplicateService

DumpMessages Streaming API:
- Implement DumpMessages RPC for streaming WAL messages
- Support start_timetick and end_timetick filters
- Filter out system messages (TimeTick, CreateSegment, Flush, RollbackTxn)
- Use oneof response for status or message

Tests:
- Add unit tests for salvage checkpoint capture
- Add unit tests for shouldDumpMessage filter
- Add integration tests for GetReplicateInfo and DumpMessages

Signed-off-by: bigsheeper <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>

fix: resolve deadlock and GracefulClose race in data salvage

- Move persistSalvageCheckpoint out of the r.mu-held call path in
  updateCheckpoint to avoid potential deadlock when etcd is unavailable
  during force promote. The salvage checkpoint is now stored in a new
  pendingSalvageCheckpoint field and persisted by the background task.
- Add isDirty() check for pendingSalvageCheckpoint so the background
  task wakes up and flushes it even when there is no dirty snapshot.
- In RollbackAllInFlightTransactions, signal m.closed after clearing all
  sessions to unblock GracefulClose if it is already waiting, matching
  the existing pattern in CleanupTxnUntil.

title: fix: add generic LoadPlugin[T] to serialize plugin.Open() calls
body:
Replace three independent plugin.Open() + Lookup() + type-assert sequences with a single centralized LoadPlugin[T](path, symbol) generic function that holds a mutex around plugin.Open(). This prevents the concurrent plugin.Open() panic ("plugin has empty pluginpath") and eliminates the footgun of forgetting to acquire the mutex at new call sites.

Migrated call sites:
- hookutil.initHook() (MilvusHook + MilvusExtension)
- hookutil.initCipher() (CipherPlugin) — the unprotected site
- QueryNode.initHook() (QueryNodePlugin)

The old LockHookInit/UnlockHookInit API is removed — the mutex is now an internal detail of LoadPlugin.

See also: #48335
author: XuanYang-cn

commits.message:
fix: add generic LoadPlugin[T] to serialize plugin.Open() calls

Replace three independent plugin.Open() + Lookup() + type-assert
sequences with a single centralized LoadPlugin[T](path, symbol)
generic function that holds a mutex around plugin.Open(). This
prevents the concurrent plugin.Open() panic ("plugin has empty
pluginpath") and eliminates the footgun of forgetting to acquire
the mutex at new call sites.

Migrated call sites:
- hookutil.initHook() (MilvusHook + MilvusExtension)
- hookutil.initCipher() (CipherPlugin) — the unprotected site
- QueryNode.initHook() (QueryNodePlugin)

The old LockHookInit/UnlockHookInit API is removed — the mutex
is now an internal detail of LoadPlugin.

See also: #48335

title: fix: filter deleted indexes in GetIndexedSegments to prevent stale segment loading
body:
## Summary
- Filter out deleted index entries from `targetIndices` in `GetIndexedSegments`, fixing a mismatch where `len(targetIndices)` included deleted entries but the counting loop skipped them
- After drop+create index cycles, this caused all segments to appear unindexed, forcing `GetQueryVChanPositions` to load stale pre-compaction segments instead of the optimized one

issue: #48557

Signed-off-by: yangxuan <xuan.yang@zilliz.com>
author: XuanYang-cn

commits.message:
fix: filter deleted indexes in GetIndexedSegments to prevent stale segment loading

GetIndexedSegments included deleted index entries in targetIndices but
skipped them in the counting loop, causing indexedFields to never equal
len(targetIndices) when deleted entries existed. This made every segment
appear unindexed after drop+create index cycles, forcing
GetQueryVChanPositions to fall back to loading stale pre-compaction
segments instead of the optimized one.

See also: #48557

Signed-off-by: yangxuan <xuan.yang@zilliz.com>

title: fix: close parquet reader and underlying reader on NewReader error paths
body:
Fix resource leak in parquet reader `NewReader`: close parquet reader and underlying reader on error paths.

issue: #48532
author: sunby

commits.message:
fix: close parquet reader on NewReader error paths

In NewReader, when file.NewParquetReader succeeds but subsequent
operations (EstimateReadCountPerBatch, NewFileReader, or
CreateFieldReaders) fail, the parquet reader is not closed, leaking
file descriptors and memory.

Fix by calling r.Close() on each error path after the parquet reader
is created. Arrow's file.Reader.Close cascades to close the underlying
retryableReader. For the NewParquetReader failure path, close
retryableReader directly since the parquet reader doesn't exist yet.

Signed-off-by: sunby <sunbingyi1992@gmail.com>

title: fix: unify raw data lifecycle management via LoadDiff to prevent SIGSEGV on column groups
body:
Remove direct ManualEvictCache calls from LoadVecIndex/LoadScalarIndex and move raw data lifecycle management entirely into the LoadDiff + ApplyLoadDiff pipeline. This fixes unsafe cache eviction on multi-field column groups that could cause SIGSEGV in GroupChunk::GetChunk, and eliminates data vacuum windows during index transitions.

Key changes:
- Remove ManualEvictCache from LoadVecIndex and LoadScalarIndex
- Add IsInMultiFieldColumnGroup() guard to DropFieldData
- Add PK field protection to DropFieldData
- ComputeDiffIndexes: mark field_data_to_drop when new index has raw data
- ComputeDiffReloadFields: rebuild field data from storage (binlogs/column_groups) instead of just warmup when index loses raw data
- ApplyLoadDiff: reorder to load field data BEFORE dropping indexes

issue: #47738
author: congqixia

commits.message:
fix: unify raw data lifecycle management via LoadDiff to prevent SIGSEGV on column groups

Remove direct ManualEvictCache calls from LoadVecIndex/LoadScalarIndex and
move raw data lifecycle management entirely into the LoadDiff + ApplyLoadDiff
pipeline. This fixes unsafe cache eviction on multi-field column groups that
could cause SIGSEGV in GroupChunk::GetChunk, and eliminates data vacuum
windows during index transitions.

Key changes:
- Remove ManualEvictCache from LoadVecIndex and LoadScalarIndex
- Add IsInMultiFieldColumnGroup() guard to DropFieldData
- Add PK field protection to DropFieldData
- ComputeDiffIndexes: mark field_data_to_drop when new index has raw data
- ComputeDiffReloadFields: rebuild field data from storage (binlogs/column_groups)
  instead of just warmup when index loses raw data
- ApplyLoadDiff: reorder to load field data BEFORE dropping indexes

title: enhance: use bulk copy for nullable vector payload reading
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48088

The nullable vector reading code was duplicated across 5 functions (BinaryVector, Float16Vector, BFloat16Vector, FloatVector, Int8Vector), all doing the same per-element Value(i) loop to extract valid entries.

This pulls that logic into a shared readNullableVectorData helper and switches to bulk-copying via ValueBytes(). This works because our PayloadWriter uses BinaryBuilder.AppendNull() which produces zero-length null entries in the Arrow Binary values buffer, so ValueBytes() naturally gives us only the valid data packed together. A length assertion is added just in case this invariant ever breaks.

Benchmarked with 100k rows at 50% null rate, seeing about 10-15% speedup across the board (only benchmarked the affected read functions).
author: jiaqizho

reviews.body: NIT: add some unittest for new helper function

commits.message:
enhance: use bulk copy for nullable vector payload reading

The nullable vector reading code was duplicated across 5 functions (BinaryVector, Float16Vector, BFloat16Vector, FloatVector, Int8Vector), all doing the same per-element Value(i) loop to extract valid entries.

This pulls that logic into a shared readNullableVectorData helper and switches to bulk-copying via ValueBytes(). This works because our PayloadWriter uses BinaryBuilder.AppendNull() which produces zero-length null entries in the Arrow Binary values buffer, so ValueBytes() naturally gives us only the valid data packed together. A length assertion is added just in case this invariant ever breaks.

Benchmarked with 100k rows at 50% null rate, seeing about 10-15% speedup across the board (only benchmarked the affected read functions).

Signed-off-by: jiaqizho <jiaqi.zhou@zilliz.com>

title: fix: [GoSDK][2.6] align timestamptz field type and data format with server(#47304)
body:
Cherry-pick from master
pr: #47304
Related to #47303

The Go SDK had two issues with Timestamptz field type:

1. FieldTypeTimestamptz was incorrectly defined as 15, but server expects 26
2. Timestamptz data was serialized as int64 via TimestamptzData, but server expects ISO 8601 strings (RFC3339Nano format) via StringData

Changes:
- Update FieldTypeTimestamptz value from 15 to 26
- Modify ColumnTimestamptz to store data as RFC3339Nano strings internally
- Change NewColumnTimestamptz to accept []time.Time and convert to ISO strings
- Add ColumnTimestampTzIsoString for direct ISO string input
- Update FieldDataColumn to parse Timestamptz from StringData
- Update values2Scalars to handle Timestamptz as string type
- Add NewNullableColumnTimestamptz for nullable time.Time input
- Update NewNullableColumnTimestamptzIsoString for nullable ISO string input
- Add corresponding unit tests

---------
author: congqixia

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request effectively addresses the misalignment of the `Timestamptz` field type and data format with the server's expectations. The changes correctly update the `FieldTypeTimestamptz` value, modify column handling to store `Timestamptz` data as ISO 8601 strings, and introduce new constructors for flexibility. The addition of comprehensive unit tests ensures the correctness of these new functionalities. Overall, the changes are well-implemented and directly resolve the stated issues.

The Go SDK had two issues with Timestamptz field type:

1. FieldTypeTimestamptz was incorrectly defined as 15, but server
expects 26
2. Timestamptz data was serialized as int64 via TimestamptzData, but
server expects ISO 8601 strings (RFC3339Nano format) via StringData

Changes:
- Update FieldTypeTimestamptz value from 15 to 26
- Modify ColumnTimestamptz to store data as RFC3339Nano strings
internally
- Change NewColumnTimestamptz to accept []time.Time and convert to ISO
strings
- Add ColumnTimestampTzIsoString for direct ISO string input
- Update FieldDataColumn to parse Timestamptz from StringData
- Update values2Scalars to handle Timestamptz as string type
- Add NewNullableColumnTimestamptz for nullable time.Time input
- Update NewNullableColumnTimestamptzIsoString for nullable ISO string
input
- Add corresponding unit tests

body:

The Go SDK had two issues with Timestamptz field type:

1. FieldTypeTimestamptz was incorrectly defined as 15, but server expects 26
2. Timestamptz data was serialized as int64 via TimestamptzData, but server expects ISO 8601 strings (RFC3339Nano format) via StringData

Changes:

author: congqixia

reviews.body:
## Code Review

The pull request successfully addresses the alignment of the `Timestamptz` field type and data format with the server's expectations. The changes correctly update the `FieldTypeTimestamptz` value, modify `ColumnTimestamptz` to store data as RFC3339Nano strings, and introduce `ColumnTimestampTzIsoString` for direct ISO string input. The parsing and serialization logic in `FieldDataColumn` and `values2Scalars` have been updated accordingly. Comprehensive unit tests have been added to cover these new functionalities and conversions, ensuring correctness. The change in `mock_milvus_server_test.go` appears to be unrelated to the `timestamptz` fix and might be an unintended inclusion or part of a separate change.

commits.message:
fix: align Go SDK timestamptz field type and data format with server

Related to #47303

The Go SDK had two issues with Timestamptz field type:

1. FieldTypeTimestamptz was incorrectly defined as 15, but server expects 26
2. Timestamptz data was serialized as int64 via TimestamptzData, but server
   expects ISO 8601 strings (RFC3339Nano format) via StringData

Changes:
- Update FieldTypeTimestamptz value from 15 to 26
- Modify ColumnTimestamptz to store data as RFC3339Nano strings internally
- Change NewColumnTimestamptz to accept []time.Time and convert to ISO strings
- Add ColumnTimestampTzIsoString for direct ISO string input
- Update FieldDataColumn to parse Timestamptz from StringData
- Update values2Scalars to handle Timestamptz as string type
- Add NewNullableColumnTimestamptz for nullable time.Time input
- Update NewNullableColumnTimestamptzIsoString for nullable ISO string input
- Add corresponding unit tests

Signed-off-by: Congqi Xia <congqi.xia@zilliz.com>

Bump go-api as well

Signed-off-by: Congqi Xia <congqi.xia@zilliz.com>

Fix NPE & Worker holds func

title: fix: filter git tags with --match 'v*' to avoid pkg/ submodule tags in MILVUS_VERSION
body:
## Summary

Cherry-pick of #48418 to 2.6 branch.

- Add `--match 'v*'` to `git describe --exact-match --tags` in `MILVUS_VERSION` calculation
- Prevents `pkg/v2.6.13` (Go submodule tag) from being picked instead of `v2.6.13` on release builds
- Without this fix, `GetVersion` returns `pkg/v2.6.13` instead of `2.6.13`

pr: #48418

issue: #47823

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify on a tagged commit with both `v*` and `pkg/v*` tags: `git describe --exact-match --tags --match 'v*'` returns only the root tag
- [ ] Dev builds unaffected (no exact tag match, falls through to branch-date-commit format)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: yanliang567

commits.message:
fix: filter git tags with --match 'v*' to avoid pkg/ submodule tags in MILVUS_VERSION

`git describe --exact-match --tags` may return `pkg/v2.6.13` (Go submodule tag)
instead of `v2.6.13` when both tags exist on the same commit. The subsequent
`sed 's/^v//'` does not strip the `pkg/` prefix, causing GetVersion to return
`pkg/v2.6.13` instead of `2.6.13` on release builds.

Add `--match 'v*'` to only match root module tags starting with `v`.

issue: #47823

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: yanliang567 <82361606+yanliang567@users.noreply.github.com>

title: fix: filter git tags with --match 'v*' to avoid pkg/ submodule tags in MILVUS_VERSION
body:
## Summary

- Add `--match 'v*'` to `git describe --exact-match --tags` in `MILVUS_VERSION` calculation
- Prevents `pkg/v2.6.13` (Go submodule tag) from being picked instead of `v2.6.13` on release builds
- Without this fix, `GetVersion` returns `pkg/v2.6.13` instead of `2.6.13` because `sed 's/^v//'` doesn't strip the `pkg/` prefix

### Root Cause

The milvus repo uses Go multi-module: `pkg/` has its own `go.mod` with tags like `pkg/v2.6.13`. When both `v2.6.13` and `pkg/v2.6.13` exist on the same commit, `git describe --exact-match --tags` may return the `pkg/` prefixed tag.

issue: #47823

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify on a tagged commit with both `v*` and `pkg/v*` tags: `git describe --exact-match --tags --match 'v*'` returns only the root tag
- [ ] Dev builds unaffected (no exact tag match, falls through to branch-date-commit format)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: yanliang567

commits.message:
fix: filter git tags with --match 'v*' to avoid pkg/ submodule tags in MILVUS_VERSION

`git describe --exact-match --tags` may return `pkg/v2.6.13` (Go submodule tag)
instead of `v2.6.13` when both tags exist on the same commit. The subsequent
`sed 's/^v//'` does not strip the `pkg/` prefix, causing GetVersion to return
`pkg/v2.6.13` instead of `2.6.13` on release builds.

Add `--match 'v*'` to only match root module tags starting with `v`.

issue: #47823

title: fix: update expected error for test_search_no_connection after pymilvus rc162
body:
## Summary
- Update `test_search_no_connection` expected error to match pymilvus 2.7.0rc162 behavior

## Root Cause
After pymilvus upgrade to rc162 (#48424), `MilvusClient.close()` sets the internal gRPC handler to `None`. When `search()` is called after `close()`:
- **Before rc162**: `MilvusException(code=1, "should create connection first")`
- **After rc162**: `AttributeError("'NoneType' object has no attribute 'search'")` → wrapped as `err_code=-1`

This was not caught by PR #48424's CI because:
1. The test was refactored from ORM to MilvusClient v2 style in PR #48347 (merged after #48424's CI ran)
2. PR #48424's CI ran the old ORM version which uses `remove_connection()` (different code path, still returns err_code=1)
3. Only after both PRs merged to master did the combination break in hourly CI

## Test plan
- [x] `test_search_no_connection` passes locally against Milvus 10.104.14.152 with pymilvus 2.7.0rc162

issue: #48048

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: fix: [2.6] clamp targetVecIndexVersion to cluster maximum to prevent QN load failure during rolling upgrade
body:
## Summary

During rolling upgrade, `targetVecIndexVersion` can exceed old QueryNodes'
`maximum_version`. The `max(merged, target)` operation bypasses the
`GetCurrentIndexEngineVersion()` MIN safety mechanism, producing index
versions that old QNs cannot load.

- QN now reports `MaximumIndexVersion` via session
- DataCoord computes cluster-wide max via `GetMaximumIndexEngineVersion()`
- New `ResolveVecIndexVersion()` clamps target to `[cluster_min, cluster_max]`
- `task_index.go` and `compaction_trigger.go` use the clamped version

issue: #48470
pr: #48249
author: foxspy

reviews.body: Review comments from code analysis — 2 high, 1 medium, 3 low.

commits.message:
fix: [2.6] clamp targetVecIndexVersion to cluster maximum to prevent QN load failure during rolling upgrade

During rolling upgrade, targetVecIndexVersion can exceed old QNs'
maximum_version. The max() operation bypasses the MIN safety mechanism,
causing index builds with versions that old QNs cannot load.

Fix: QN reports MaximumIndexVersion via session; DataCoord computes
cluster-wide max and clamps the resolved version to [min, max] via
ResolveVecIndexVersion().

issue: #48470

Signed-off-by: xianliang.li <xianliang.li@zilliz.com>

title: fix: allocate new buildIDs for copy segments to prevent segmentBuildInfo corruption
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47658

## Summary

When restoring snapshots via copy segment, the source buildIDs were reused directly, causing 1:1 map corruption in DataCoord's `segmentBuildInfo`. This PR:

- Extends `CopySegmentTarget` proto with `new_build_ids` mapping (`map<int64, int64>`)
- Allocates fresh buildIDs in DataCoord and passes them to DataNode
- DataNode replaces buildIDs in both index file paths and metadata
- Fixes indexID mismatch in `syncVectorScalarIndexes` by resolving target collection's indexID via fieldID
- Shifts GC protection from indexID-based (`GetSnapshotByIndex`) to buildID-based (`GetSnapshotByBuildID`) for precise file-level protection
- Updates snapshot user guide to reflect GC protection mechanism change

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests updated for `copy_segment_meta`, `snapshot_meta`, `copy_segment_task`
- [x] E2E test added: snapshot restore → drop → restore again flow (`TestSnapshotRestoreDropRestoreAgain`)
- [ ] CI: ut-go, integration-test, e2e pass

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: allocate new buildIDs for copy segments to prevent segmentBuildInfo corruption

issue: #47658

When restoring snapshots via copy segment, the source buildIDs were
reused directly, causing 1:1 map corruption in DataCoord's
segmentBuildInfo. This change:
1. Extends CopySegmentTarget proto with new_build_ids mapping
2. Allocates fresh buildIDs in DataCoord and passes them to DataNode
3. DataNode replaces buildIDs in both index paths and metadata
4. Fixes indexID mismatch in syncVectorScalarIndexes
5. Persists SegmentIDs/IndexIDs in SaveSnapshot metadata

Also adds e2e test for snapshot restore-drop-restore-again flow.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: support multiple JSON path indexes per field in snapshot restore

issue: #47658

CopySegmentResult.index_infos used fieldID as map key, causing only the
last index to survive when a JSON field had multiple path indexes
(e.g., metadata["category"] and metadata["price"]). This change:
1. Adds index_name to VectorScalarIndexInfo proto for source-target matching
2. Changes index_infos map key from fieldID to buildID (globally unique)
3. DataCoord matches indexes by indexName instead of fieldID
4. Fixes AddSegmentIndex unconditionally overriding IndexState to Unissued,
   so copied indexes keep Finished state and avoid redundant rebuilds
5. Extracts allocNewBuildID helper to deduplicate alloc loops (review feedback)
6. Adds unit tests for syncVectorScalarIndexes and E2E test with filter queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: align import with DDL/DCL pattern and remove cross-RPC broadcast
body:
issue: #43897

This refactoring moves import handling from proxy-initiated cross-RPC broadcast to coordinator-internal broadcast, aligning with the standard DDL/DCL message handling pattern used throughout Milvus.

- Add import_callbacks.go: validation, broadcast, and ack callback handling
- Modify ImportV2() to support dual mode via DataTimestamp heuristic:
  * DataTimestamp=0: Proxy call (validate → broadcast → return jobID)
  * DataTimestamp>0: Ack callback (create import job)
- Simplify initMessageCallback() to use RegisterImportCallbacks()

- Change task_import.go to call DataCoord.ImportV2() instead of WAL broadcast
- Set DataTimestamp=0 to indicate proxy call (critical for heuristic)
- Remove unused imports: strconv, streaming, message, funcutil

- Deprecate streaming/broadcast.go Append() method with panic
- Deprecate streamingcoord Broadcast() gRPC handler with error
- Prevent components from bypassing coordinator validation

- Remove CheckCallback invocation from broadcast_manager.go
- Remove CheckCallback registration infrastructure
- Deprecate check_message_callback.go and related test file
- Move all validation logic before broadcast (in DataCoord)

Added comprehensive test coverage (658 lines):
- import_refactoring_test.go (269 lines): DataTimestamp heuristic, flow paths
- import_callbacks_test.go (105 lines): validation logic, error cases
- task_import_refactoring_test.go (284 lines): proxy call verification, DataTimestamp=0 check

E2E testing infrastructure:
- Created test scripts for milvus-cluster-manage integration
- Tests cover basic import, partition import, data consistency

Before: Proxy → RPC Broadcast → StreamingCoord → CheckCallback → DataCoord
After:  Proxy → DataCoord → Local Broadcast → DataCoord (ack callback)

Benefits:
- Validation centralized in coordinator (standard pattern)
- No cross-component RPC broadcast
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Easier to maintain and test

Added docs/import_refactoring_complete.md with:
- Complete flow diagrams
- Design decisions and rationale
- Testing strategy and verification steps
- Migration notes
author: chyezh

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a significant and well-executed refactoring of the import process. By moving the import handling from a proxy-initiated cross-RPC broadcast to a coordinator-internal broadcast, it aligns with the standard DDL/DCL message handling pattern, which improves the architecture's clarity and maintainability. The introduction of a dual-mode `ImportV2` function based on `DataTimestamp` is a clever solution to handle both initial proxy requests and subsequent ack callbacks. The changes are supported by a comprehensive set of new tests.

My review includes a high-severity comment on a potential regression in the replication validation logic and a medium-severity suggestion to improve the clarity of a new unit test. Overall, this is a solid contribution that enhances the robustness of the import functionality.

commits.message:
refactor: align import with DDL/DCL pattern and remove cross-RPC broadcast

This refactoring moves import handling from proxy-initiated cross-RPC broadcast
to coordinator-internal broadcast, aligning with the standard DDL/DCL message
handling pattern used throughout Milvus.

- Add import_callbacks.go: validation, broadcast, and ack callback handling
- Modify ImportV2() to support dual mode via DataTimestamp heuristic:
  * DataTimestamp=0: Proxy call (validate → broadcast → return jobID)
  * DataTimestamp>0: Ack callback (create import job)
- Simplify initMessageCallback() to use RegisterImportCallbacks()

- Change task_import.go to call DataCoord.ImportV2() instead of WAL broadcast
- Set DataTimestamp=0 to indicate proxy call (critical for heuristic)
- Remove unused imports: strconv, streaming, message, funcutil

- Deprecate streaming/broadcast.go Append() method with panic
- Deprecate streamingcoord Broadcast() gRPC handler with error
- Prevent components from bypassing coordinator validation

- Remove CheckCallback invocation from broadcast_manager.go
- Remove CheckCallback registration infrastructure
- Deprecate check_message_callback.go and related test file
- Move all validation logic before broadcast (in DataCoord)

Added comprehensive test coverage (658 lines):
- import_refactoring_test.go (269 lines): DataTimestamp heuristic, flow paths
- import_callbacks_test.go (105 lines): validation logic, error cases
- task_import_refactoring_test.go (284 lines): proxy call verification, DataTimestamp=0 check

E2E testing infrastructure:
- Created test scripts for milvus-cluster-manage integration
- Tests cover basic import, partition import, data consistency

Before: Proxy → RPC Broadcast → StreamingCoord → CheckCallback → DataCoord
After:  Proxy → DataCoord → Local Broadcast → DataCoord (ack callback)

Benefits:
- Validation centralized in coordinator (standard pattern)
- No cross-component RPC broadcast
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Easier to maintain and test

configurable parameter `common.threadCoreCoefficient.maxThreadsSize`.
The default value remains 16 for backward compatibility. The limit
is only effective when greater than 0.

pools, so all pools can be dynamically resized at runtime via config
changes.

title: enhance: speed up MixCoord recovery by parallelizing startup phases
body:
## Summary

Optimize standalone MixCoord recovery to reduce startup time by parallelizing independent operations:

- **Parallelize DataCoord & QueryCoord startup**: both only depend on RootCoord being ready, run them concurrently via errgroup
- **Parallelize DataCoord sub-meta loading**: load all 6 sub-metas (indexMeta, analyzeMeta, partitionStatsMeta, compactionTaskMeta, statsTaskMeta, snapshotMeta) concurrently alongside reloadFromKV
- **Parallelize indexMeta reload**: run ListIndexes and ListSegmentIndexes concurrently since they update independent data structures (m.indexes vs m.segmentIndexes/m.segmentBuildInfo)
- **Batch delete TargetManager targets**: replace per-collection RemoveCollectionTarget calls with single RemoveWithPrefix
- **Batch etcd loading for ListCollections**: replace sequential per-key reads with single LoadWithPrefix in RootCoord's initMetaTable

issue: #47783

## Test Plan

- [x] Unit tests pass for all changed packages (index_meta, meta, kv_catalog, target_manager)
- [x] Race detector clean (`go test -race`)
- [x] Coverage: core functions (reloadFromKV, newIndexMeta, RemoveCollectionTargets) at 100%; batch load functions at 81-93%
author: sijie-ni-0214

commits.message:
enhance: speed up MixCoord recovery by parallelizing startup phases

Optimize standalone MixCoord recovery to reduce startup time:

1. Parallelize DataCoord and QueryCoord startup: use errgroup to run
   DC and QC init+start concurrently since they only depend on
   RootCoord being ready.

2. Parallelize DataCoord sub-meta loading: load all 6 sub-metas
   (indexMeta, analyzeMeta, partitionStatsMeta, compactionTaskMeta,
   statsTaskMeta, snapshotMeta) concurrently via errgroup instead of
   sequentially.

3. Parallelize DataCoord sub-meta and reloadFromKV: run reloadFromKV
   (ListSegments, ListChannelCheckpoint) as a 7th goroutine alongside
   sub-meta loading since they operate on independent data structures.

4. Parallelize indexMeta reload: run ListIndexes and ListSegmentIndexes
   concurrently with their memory updates in the same goroutines, as
   they write to independent data structures (m.indexes vs
   m.segmentIndexes/m.segmentBuildInfo).

5. Batch delete TargetManager targets: replace per-collection
   RemoveCollectionTarget calls with a single RemoveCollectionTargets
   (RemoveWithPrefix) call after in-memory recovery.

title: enhance: [2.6] use differentiate load priorities by scenario
body:
pr: #47410
issue: #43040

Implement intelligent LoadPriority assignment based on operation scenarios,
allowing QueryNodes to prioritize critical operations over background tasks.

**Background:**
Previously, all segment load operations used the same priority, which could
cause issues when urgent operations (like search requests) competed with background tasks (like balance) for resources.

**Solution:**
Centralize LoadPriority decisions in Checkers, with each Checker understanding
its operational context and assigning appropriate priorities:

| Scenario | LoadPriority | Rationale |
|------------------------|------------------------|------------------------------------| 
| Recovery | HIGH | Restore data availability ASAP | 
| User Load / Refresh | replica.LoadPriority() | Respect user's configured priority |
| Stopping Balance | HIGH | Drain nodes quickly for shutdown | 
| Normal Balance | LOW | Background optimization | 
| Manual Balance | LOW | User maintenance, not urgent | 
| Handoff | LOW | Growing→sealed, can wait |
| Index Update | LOW | Background maintenance |
| Stats Update | LOW | Background maintenance |

**Implementation:**

1. SegmentChecker: Determine priority by collection state
   - Check if segment exists in currentTarget (recovery vs new segment)
   - Check `collection.IsRefreshed()` to distinguish handoff from import

2. BalanceChecker: Add `isStoppingBalance` flag
   - Stopping balance uses HIGH to accelerate node draining
   - Normal balance uses LOW to avoid interfering with user operations

3. IndexChecker: Always use LOW for index/stats updates

4. Manual Balance: Manual balance uses LOW directly

title: enhance: [2.6] make DefaultValueChunk multi-cells to save memory usage
body:
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/46000
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/47481
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/45999

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
- Core invariant: DefaultValueChunkTranslator assumes a constant per-value byte size (value_size()) for a field and therefore models default-value storage as multiple deterministic, fixed-size cells (num_cells(), cell_id_of(uid) == uid). A single contiguous primary_buffer_ is built (optionally persisted to mmap) and sliced per-cell; this invariant enables safe sharing of the same underlying bytes across multiple Chunk views.
- Removed/simplified logic: per-field direct file_path writes and duplicated make_chunk write paths were collapsed into a buffer-first flow (ChunkBuffer + create_chunk_buffer + make_chunk_from_buffer). This removes redundant per-field mmap/write code and allows multiple Chunk instances to reuse a shared ChunkBuffer/ChunkMmapGuard instead of performing independent writes.
- Why no data loss or regression: byte layout, alignment, padding and per-row content are preserved because create_chunk_buffer implements the same alignment/padding and writes identical bytes previously produced by the direct make_chunk path; make_chunk_from_buffer constructs Chunk views over those same bytes. DefaultValueChunkTranslator uses deterministic math (total_rows_, num_rows_until_chunk_, primary_cell_rows_) to slice rows—no rows are dropped, reordered, or altered. Concrete code paths: callers that used create_group_chunk → per-field make_chunk now use create_group_chunk → create_chunk_buffer → make_chunk_from_buffer; DefaultValueChunkTranslator’s get_cells(), estimated_byte_size_of_cell(), and value_size() produce the same observable outputs as before for single-cell cases and correct per-cell outputs for multi-cell cases.
- Enhancement / scope: adds multi-cell default-value chunks and optional mmap-backed persistence plus shared-memory ChunkBuffer support. Changes touch DefaultValueChunkTranslator (multi-cell behavior, value_size(), primary_buffer_, mmap_dir_path_), ChunkWriter (ChunkBuffer, create_chunk_buffer, make_chunk_from_buffer), and
ChunkedSegmentSealedImpl (per-field mmap decision and mmap_dir_path propagation); comprehensive tests (mmap/non-mmap, nullable, multi/single-cell) validate correctness.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------
author: sparknack

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request enhances `DefaultValueChunk` to support multiple cells, aiming to reduce memory usage, and introduces mmap-backed persistence. The core changes are in `DefaultValueChunkTranslator`, which now splits default value fields into smaller, 64KB cells. A `ChunkBuffer` abstraction has been introduced to allow sharing of the underlying memory buffer across multiple `Chunk` instances, which is a key part of the memory optimization. The changes are well-tested with new unit tests covering various data types, mmap functionality, and edge cases.

I've identified a couple of issues. There's an inconsistency in how `create_group_chunk` is called, where the new `mmap_populate` parameter is not always passed. Additionally, the calculation for `value_size` for JSON and Array types in `DefaultValueChunkTranslator` appears to be incorrect, which could undermine the memory-saving benefits for those types. I've also suggested a minor refactoring in the new tests to improve maintainability.

commits.message:
enhance: [2.6] make DefaultValueChunk multi-cells to save memory usage

pr: #46000
pr: #47481
issue: #45999

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
- Core invariant: DefaultValueChunkTranslator assumes a constant
per-value byte size (value_size()) for a field and therefore models
default-value storage as multiple deterministic, fixed-size cells
(num_cells(), cell_id_of(uid) == uid). A single contiguous
primary_buffer_ is built (optionally persisted to mmap) and sliced
per-cell; this invariant enables safe sharing of the same underlying
bytes across multiple Chunk views.
- Removed/simplified logic: per-field direct file_path writes and
duplicated make_chunk write paths were collapsed into a buffer-first
flow (ChunkBuffer + create_chunk_buffer + make_chunk_from_buffer). This
removes redundant per-field mmap/write code and allows multiple Chunk
instances to reuse a shared ChunkBuffer/ChunkMmapGuard instead of
performing independent writes.
- Why no data loss or regression: byte layout, alignment, padding and
per-row content are preserved because create_chunk_buffer implements the
same alignment/padding and writes identical bytes previously produced by
the direct make_chunk path; make_chunk_from_buffer constructs Chunk
views over those same bytes. DefaultValueChunkTranslator uses
deterministic math (total_rows_, num_rows_until_chunk_,
primary_cell_rows_) to slice rows—no rows are dropped, reordered, or
altered. Concrete code paths: callers that used create_group_chunk →
per-field make_chunk now use create_group_chunk → create_chunk_buffer →
make_chunk_from_buffer; DefaultValueChunkTranslator’s get_cells(),
estimated_byte_size_of_cell(), and value_size() produce the same
observable outputs as before for single-cell cases and correct per-cell
outputs for multi-cell cases.
- Enhancement / scope: adds multi-cell default-value chunks and optional
mmap-backed persistence plus shared-memory ChunkBuffer support. Changes
touch DefaultValueChunkTranslator (multi-cell behavior, value_size(),
primary_buffer_, mmap_dir_path_), ChunkWriter (ChunkBuffer,
create_chunk_buffer, make_chunk_from_buffer), and
ChunkedSegmentSealedImpl (per-field mmap decision and mmap_dir_path
propagation); comprehensive tests (mmap/non-mmap, nullable,
multi/single-cell) validate correctness.

title: feat: add scalar index version management with MaxVersion support
body:
Introduce full scalar index version management parity with vector indexes:
- Add MaximumScalarIndexEngineVersion constant and MaximumIndexVersion to session
- Add targetScalarIndexVersion and forceRebuildScalarSegmentIndex config params
- Add GetMaximum*Version() and Resolve*IndexVersion() to IndexEngineVersionManager
- Propagate MaximumIndexVersion from knowhere C++ to Go layer via session
- Add scalar forceRebuild path in compaction trigger
- Fix DataNode scalar version clamp to use Maximum instead of Current
- Fix sort_compaction and stats task to use request-carried version
- Use resolved (clamped) target version in compaction trigger to prevent infinite rebuild loops

fix: clamp scalar index version in stats task and sort compaction, add lower bound validation

- Stats task and sort compaction now call getCurrentScalarIndexVersion() /
  ClampScalarIndexVersion() before passing version to C++ CreateIndex,
  matching the behavior of normal index build path (task_index.go).
- ResolveScalarIndexVersion() now validates lower bound against
  MinimalScalarIndexEngineVersion, preventing negative version numbers
  from being sent to the builder.

fix: clamp Resolve*IndexVersion() to [clusterMinimal, clusterMaximum] safe range

ResolveVecIndexVersion() only had upper-bound clamp but no lower-bound,
allowing force rebuild to produce indexes below cluster minimal that some
QueryNodes cannot build/search.

ResolveScalarIndexVersion() used hardcoded constant (0) as lower bound
instead of the cluster-aggregated GetMinimalScalarIndexEngineVersion().

Extract clampVersion() helper and apply both bounds in both Resolve methods.
Update tests to verify lower-bound clamping including multi-node scenarios.

title: enhance: enable OpenSSL FIPS mode for Milvus
body:
Layer 2 (OpenSSL FIPS) changes:
- Add programmatic FIPS activation via OSSL_LIB_CTX_load_config in boring_enabled.go (gated by //go:build boringcrypto)
- Add openssl-fips.cnf with fips + default providers and default_properties = fips=yes
- Use absolute .include path for fipsmodule.cnf — OpenSSL resolves relative .include from the process working directory, not the config file's directory, causing silent FIPS provider load failure
- Add RAND_bytes probe after config load to verify the FIPS provider is truly functional (EVP_default_properties_is_fips_enabled only checks the property string, not whether the provider loaded)
- Dockerfiles: add openssl fipsinstall + OPENSSL_MODULES env var
- Log OpenSSL FIPS status from C++ via EVP_default_properties_is_fips_enabled

Layer 1 (Go BoringCrypto) changes:
- Add GOEXPERIMENT=boringcrypto build flag (conditional on MILVUS_FIPS_ENABLED=ON)
- Add boringEnabled() build-tagged functions for startup logging

s2n-tls upgrade:
- Override s2n 1.4.1 (from aws-c-io) to 1.6.0 in conanfile.py. s2n 1.4.1 only detects FIPS via the legacy OPENSSL_FIPS define (not set by OpenSSL 3.x). s2n 1.6.0 adds EVP_default_properties_is_fips_enabled() detection so s2n enters FIPS mode and uses RAND_bytes() through the FIPS provider.

See also: #48202, #48301
pr: #48331
author: XuanYang-cn

commits.message:
enhance: enable OpenSSL FIPS mode for Milvus

Layer 2 (OpenSSL FIPS) changes:
- Add programmatic FIPS activation via OSSL_LIB_CTX_load_config in
  boring_enabled.go (gated by //go:build boringcrypto)
- Add openssl-fips.cnf with fips + default providers and
  default_properties = fips=yes
- Dockerfiles: add openssl fipsinstall + OPENSSL_MODULES env var
- Log OpenSSL FIPS status from C++ via EVP_default_properties_is_fips_enabled

Layer 1 (Go BoringCrypto) changes:
- Add GOEXPERIMENT=boringcrypto build flag (conditional on MILVUS_FIPS_ENABLED=ON)
- Add boringEnabled() build-tagged functions for startup logging

s2n-tls upgrade:
- Override s2n 1.4.1 (from aws-c-io) to 1.6.0 in conanfile.py.
  s2n 1.4.1 only detects FIPS via the legacy OPENSSL_FIPS define
  (not set by OpenSSL 3.x). s2n 1.6.0 adds
  EVP_default_properties_is_fips_enabled() detection so s2n enters
  FIPS mode and uses RAND_bytes() through the FIPS provider.

enhance: add CRC32C checksum and TLS support for object storage (#9)

Add CRC32C checksum validation for MinIO/S3 PutObject requests and

See also: #48202, #48301

title: fix: resolve flaky TestBalancer_DynamicChannelFromProvider test
body:
## Summary
- Fix flaky `TestBalancer_DynamicChannelFromProvider` and `TestBalancer_DynamicChannelProviderClosed` tests in `internal/streamingcoord/server/balancer`
- Root cause: `TestBalancer` leaked `WALBalancerExpectedInitialStreamingNodeNum=3` via `SwapTempValue` without restoring, causing subsequent tests to hang waiting for 3 streaming nodes when mocks only provided 2
- Secondary cause: shared embedded etcd state with stale proxy sessions caused `SaveVersion` mock expectation failures
- Added 30s timeout protection to `WatchChannelAssignments` calls that previously used `context.Background()` with no deadline

## Test plan
- [x] Reproduced failure locally (1/1 = 100% failure rate on unmodified code)
- [x] Verified fix: `TestBalancer_DynamicChannelFromProvider` 10/10 passes
- [x] Verified fix: `TestBalancer_DynamicChannelProviderClosed` 10/10 passes
- [x] Both tests combined 10/10 passes

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/39893

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: liliu-z

commits.message:
fix: resolve flaky TestBalancer_DynamicChannelFromProvider test

The test failed intermittently because:
1. TestBalancer set WALBalancerExpectedInitialStreamingNodeNum to "3"
   via SwapTempValue but never restored it, leaking to subsequent tests
2. Shared embedded etcd state caused SaveVersion mock expectation to
   fail when stale proxy sessions existed from prior test runs
3. WatchChannelAssignments used context.Background() with no timeout

Fix by restoring the param in TestBalancer via defer, explicitly setting
it to "0" in affected tests, marking SaveVersion as Maybe(), and adding
30s timeouts to WatchChannelAssignments calls.

title: fix: resolve flaky mixcoord client tests with context deadline race
body:
## Summary
- Fix 39 flaky "test ctx done" cases in `internal/distributed/mixcoord/client/client_test.go`
- Root cause: `ReCall` mock directly called `f(mockmix)` without checking if context was expired, causing a race where the mock returned the previous test case's error instead of `context.DeadlineExceeded`
- Fix: add `ctx.Err()` check before invoking `f()` in all 42 `ReCall` mock setups, matching real gRPC client behavior

## Test plan
- [x] `Test_DropVirtualChannel` 10/10 passes
- [x] All tests in `internal/distributed/mixcoord/client/` pass

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/39893

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: liliu-z

commits.message:
fix: resolve flaky mixcoord client tests with context deadline race

The ReCall mock in client_test.go directly called f(mockmix) without
checking if the context was already expired. When the "test ctx done"
cases created a 10ms timeout and slept 20ms, a race existed: the mock
could execute and return the previous test case's mock error instead
of context.DeadlineExceeded.

Fix by checking ctx.Err() before invoking f() in all 42 ReCall mock
setups, matching the real gRPC client behavior of checking context
before making calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
