reviews.body:
## Test Review: PR #48271

Overall: Good direction — `_wait_until_search_count` is a solid abstraction, and the flaky fix approach is correct. A few issues to address.

### Major

**1. Search retry logic inconsistency — `_wait_until_search_count` exists but isn't reused**

The PR adds `_wait_until_search_count` (good!), but `test_search_filter_expired_entity_data` and `test_search_without_filter_expired_entity_data` still use inline `for` loops with `time.sleep(2)` instead of the new helper. These should be unified to use `_wait_until_search_count` for consistency and maintainability.

**2. Inline retry has incomplete assertion after timeout**

In both `test_search_filter_expired_entity_data` and `test_search_without_filter_expired_entity_data`:

```python
for _ in range(15):
    res = self.search(...)
    if len(res[0]) > 0 and all(hit['id'] >= 100 for hit in res[0]):
        break
    time.sleep(2)
assert len(res[0]) > 0
```

If all 15 retries fail (loop exits normally without `break`), `res` holds the last result. The subsequent `assert len(res[0]) > 0` only checks non-empty — **it does NOT verify `all(hit['id'] >= 100)`**. So if search returns expired data after 30s, this assert passes → false positive.

Should be:
```python
assert len(res[0]) > 0 and all(hit['id'] >= 100 for hit in res[0])
```

Or better yet, just use `_wait_until_search_count` with a filter parameter.

**3. `_wait_until_search_count` timeout calculation has off-by-one**

```python
for _ in range(timeout // interval):  # timeout=30, interval=2 → 15 iterations
    ...
    time.sleep(interval)
# one final search + assert
```

This works for the default values, but edge cases like `timeout=5, interval=3` → `range(1)` → only 1 loop iteration + 1 final = waits only 3s. Consider `range(timeout // interval + 1)` or using elapsed time for accuracy.

### Minor

**4. `test_milvus_client_ttl_default` retry increased from 3×1s to 15×2s**

Max wait goes from 3s to 30s (10x increase). This works, but it sugg

commits.message:
test: add upsert TTL overwrite test and remove skipped alter test

Issue: #47482

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Eric Hou <eric.hou@zilliz.com>

test: fix flaky TTL test cases by adding search retry logic

Search and query take different code paths in Milvus, and TTL filtering
can propagate at different speeds between them. Several TTL tests used
query-based polling (_wait_until_count) to confirm TTL state, then
immediately asserted on search results without retry, causing flaky
failures.

- Add _wait_until_search_count helper that polls search results
- Fix test_upsert_overwrite_null_ttl_with_short_ttl: use polling for
  all search assertions (TTL expiry, release/reload, compaction)
- Fix test_milvus_client_ttl_default: increase retry from 3x1s to 15x2s
  for search after new insert post-TTL expiry
- Fix test_search_filter_expired_entity_data: add retry loop for search
  checking expired entities are filtered
- Fix test_search_without_filter_expired_entity_data: add retry loop for
  search without filter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: replace more immediate TTL assertions with polling helpers

- test_insert_with_expired_entity_ttl: use _wait_until_count for

- test_entity_ttl_switch_ttl_field: use _wait_until_count after

- test_insert_new_data_after_entity_ttl_expiry: use

- test_entity_ttl_with_partitions: add per-partition polling after
  TTL expiry with CONSISTENCY_STRONG

title: enhance: replace per-row std::set copy with bitmask in ContainsAll
body:
issue: #48160

Replace the O(n) heap-allocating std::set copy per row in ExecArrayContainsAll, ExecJsonContainsAll, and
ExecJsonContainsAllByStats with a ContainsAllMatcher that builds a value->bit-index hash map once per sub-batch and uses a uint64_t bitmask per row (zero heap alloc). For >64 targets, falls back to a vector<uint64_t> dynamic bitset.

Also fix pre-existing bug in ChunkVector.h where std::atomic<int64_t> was passed directly to fmt::format (which does not support std::atomic). Use .load() to extract the value first.

Benchmark:
| Scenario                              | SetCopy  | Bitmask  | Speedup |
|---------------------------------------|---------:|---------:|--------:|
| int64, 3 targets                      | 162.6 ms | 92.6 ms  | 1.8x    |
| int64, 10 targets                     | 520.0 ms | 252.6 ms | 2.1x    |
| int64, 30 targets                     | 726.9 ms | 155.1 ms | 4.7x    |
| int64, 5 targets early exit           | 147.6 ms | 12.9 ms  | 11.5x   |
| int64, small range (many matches)     | 218.9 ms | 83.6 ms  | 2.6x    |
| int64, 80 targets (large bitset)      | 883.6 ms | 111.4 ms | 7.9x    |
| int64, Zipf skew                      | 222.4 ms | 158.1 ms | 1.4x    |
| string, 3 targets (pessimistic)       | 195.4 ms | 158.5 ms | 1.2x    |
| string, 10 targets (pessimistic)      | 558.3 ms | 359.5 ms | 1.6x    |
| JSON, 3 targets                       | 176.9 ms | 90.1 ms  | 2.0x    |
| JSON, 10 targets + 10% double         | 521.6 ms | 239.5 ms | 2.2x    |
| JSON, 5 targets + errors              | 351.9 ms | 197.8 ms | 1.8x    |
| JSON, 20 targets + 15% error          | 499.2 ms | 223.2 ms | 2.2x    |
author: lyang24

reviews.body:
thanks for the contribution! 

we should see further improvements after addressing the comments!

commits.message:
enhance: replace per-row std::set copy with bitmask in ContainsAll

Replace the O(n) heap-allocating std::set copy per row in
ExecArrayContainsAll, ExecJsonContainsAll, and
ExecJsonContainsAllByStats with a ContainsAllMatcher that builds a
value->bit-index hash map once per sub-batch and uses a uint64_t
bitmask per row (zero heap alloc). For >64 targets, falls back to a
vector<uint64_t> dynamic bitset.

Also fix pre-existing bug in ChunkVector.h where std::atomic<int64_t>
was passed directly to fmt::format (which does not support
std::atomic). Use .load() to extract the value first.

Benchmark (1M rows, array len 10-30):
- 3 targets: 1.8x faster
- 10 targets: 2.1x faster
- 30 targets: 4.7x faster
- early exit (array shorter than targets): 11.5x faster

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: lyang24 <lanqingy93@gmail.com>

enhance: address review feedback — hoist matcher/vector, add state API

- Move ContainsAllMatcher outside execute_sub_batch lambda (built once,
  not per chunk)
- Hoist vector<uint64_t> allocation outside per-row loop, reset with
  std::fill (20% faster on >64 targets path)
- Add ContainsAllBranchState with reset()/mark()/all_found() API per
  maintainer suggestion — benchmarks show no regression vs direct loop

title: test: fix flaky test_hybrid_search_as_search on ARM
body:
## What does this PR do?

Fix a flaky test in `test_milvus_client_hybrid_search_v2.py`.

## Why?

`test_hybrid_search_as_search` asserts that hybrid search (single field + WeightedRanker) returns the same ordered result list as a regular search. When two results have tied scores, tie-breaking order is non-deterministic across architectures and runs due to floating-point differences — this causes spurious failures on ARM e2e CI.

Comparing ID sets instead of ordered lists preserves the intent of the test (same result candidates returned) while being robust to ordering of ties.
author: xiaofan-luan

commits.message:
test: fix flaky test_hybrid_search_as_search on ARM

        When scores are tied, tie-breaking order is non-deterministic across
        architectures (x86 vs ARM) and between runs due to floating-point
        differences. Compare result ID sets instead of ordered lists to avoid
        spurious failures on ARM e2e CI.

Signed-off-by: xiaofanluan <xiaofan.luan@zilliz.com>

consistent tokenization and condition-based polling (30 retries, 1s
interval) to wait for growing segment BM25 index readiness.

body:

 - add util.GetPath/GetPrefixPath and replace path.Join in etcd/tikv key composition
  - normalize metastore prefix builders and Walk/Load/Remove prefix usage with trailing /
  - fix suffix snapshot prefix/root handling when root is empty

author: xiaofan-luan

reviews.body:
### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `2afd08dd44`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

commits.message:
fix: replace path.Join with trailing-slash-preserving GetPath for KV operations

path.Join strips trailing slashes, which causes prefix-based queries in
etcd/TiKV to match unintended keys (e.g., "user_content" matching
"user_content_marketing"). Introduce util.GetPath that preserves trailing
slashes, and add explicit trailing "/" to prefix-based operations across
all KV catalogs to ensure correct namespace isolation.

Also removes unnecessary HandleTenantForEtcdKey calls since the tenant
parameter was always DefaultTenant (empty string), simplifying the code.

author: FeilongHou

reviews.body:
## Test Review: PR #48217

Overall: Good test additions covering dynamic fields and non-nullable default values in partial update. A few issues to address.

### Major

**1. Fixture bypasses test framework wrappers**

`prepare_default_value_collection` (new class fixture) uses raw client API calls directly:
```python
schema = client.create_schema(...)        # should be self.create_schema(client, ...)
index_params = client.prepare_index_params()  # should be self.prepare_index_params(client)
client.create_collection(...)              # should be self.create_collection(client, ...)
client.insert(...)                         # should be self.insert(client, ...)
```

All other tests in this file use the `TestMilvusClientV2Base` wrappers (`self.create_schema`, `self.create_collection`, `self.insert`, etc.) which provide consistent logging, error handling, and resource tracking. The fixture should follow the same pattern.

**2. Shared collection tests have implicit ordering dependency**

`TestPartialUpdateNotNullableDefault` uses a shared collection where each test mutates data at specific PKs. While distinct PK ranges avoid direct conflicts, **pytest does not guarantee test execution order within a class by default**. If tests ever run in a different order (e.g., via `-p no:randomly` removed, or shuffle plugin), the sequential assertions in `test_partial_update_default_multiple_fields_one_by_one` could break since it verifies initial defaults on id=10 that another test might have modified.

Recommendation: Add a brief note in the class docstring that tests MUST run in the default declaration order, or better yet, add an explicit assertion at the start of each test that verifies the precondition (you already do this for some tests with "Verify default" comments — good).

**3. `test_partial_update_dynamic_different_keys_per_row` skips verification of id=1**

Step 3 upserts 4 rows (id=0, 1, 1000, 1001) but only verifies ids [0, 1000, 1001]. Id=1 was updated with `"name": "m

commits.message:
fix: preserve $meta during partial update after schema evolution (#47717)

When a dynamic field in $meta becomes a static column via schema evolution,
partial update (upsert with partial_update=True) fails because
verifyDynamicFieldData rejects the key in $meta that now matches a
static field name.

Fix: skip verifyDynamicFieldData validation for partial update in
insertPreExecute. The merged $meta from queryPreExecute may legitimately
contain keys matching static field names — this is historical data that
should be preserved. The static field name check only applies to
user-provided dynamic data in non-partial-update flows.

Issue: #47717

matching static field names, causing data loss and int64 precision
corruption) with checkDynamicFieldDataForPartialUpdate that validates

evolution, $meta legitimately contains keys matching newly added static
columns — this data must be preserved, not deleted.

title: fix: compaction forward delete replay blocks tsafe advancement
body:
Problem:
During compaction, loadStreamDelete() holds deleteMut.RLock for 30-60+ seconds while replaying historical WAL deletes via bloom filter checking. This blocks ProcessDelete() (needs WLock), which in turn blocks UpdateTSafe() (called after ProcessDelete in DeleteNode.Operate()). Strong consistency queries timeout waiting for tsafe.

Blocking chain:
  loadStreamDelete holds deleteMut.RLock (30-60s)
    → ProcessDelete blocked on deleteMut.Lock
      → DeleteNode.Operate blocked
        → UpdateTSafe never called → tsafe frozen → query timeout

Solution:
Split loadStreamDelete into a 3-phase snapshot-and-release pattern to minimize lock hold time:

- Phase 1 (RLock, microseconds): snapshot delete buffer entries, copy the slice, release lock immediately.
- Phase 2 (no lock, seconds): perform expensive BF check and forward deletes from snapshot. WAL pipeline is fully unblocked.
- Phase 3 (RLock, milliseconds): catch up new entries added during Phase 2 using index-based slicing, flush the forwarder, then atomically add segment to distribution.

Additional changes:
- Extract processDeleteRecords helper for BF check + forward logic.
- Move loadBM25Stats before loadStreamDelete so stats are ready before segment becomes visible. Add rollback via UnregisterSealed on failure.
- Use one BufferForwarder per segment shared across Phase 2 and Phase 3, flushed once after all records are processed.

issue: #47882
pr: #47947
author: chyezh

reviews.body: `segment.Delete` cannot be called concurrently and stream delete must be applied in order. current impl break this constraint in phase3. we shall prevent this or replace segment data structure to remove this limit

commits.message:
fix: compaction forward delete replay blocks tsafe advancement (#47947)

Problem:
During compaction, loadStreamDelete() holds deleteMut.RLock for 30-60+
seconds while replaying historical WAL deletes via bloom filter
checking. This blocks ProcessDelete() (needs WLock), which in turn
blocks UpdateTSafe() (called after ProcessDelete in
DeleteNode.Operate()). Strong consistency queries timeout waiting for
tsafe.

Blocking chain:
  loadStreamDelete holds deleteMut.RLock (30-60s)
    → ProcessDelete blocked on deleteMut.Lock
      → DeleteNode.Operate blocked
        → UpdateTSafe never called → tsafe frozen → query timeout

Solution:
Split loadStreamDelete into a 3-phase snapshot-and-release pattern to
minimize lock hold time:

- Phase 1 (RLock, microseconds): snapshot delete buffer entries, copy
the slice, release lock immediately.
- Phase 2 (no lock, seconds): perform expensive BF check and forward
deletes from snapshot. WAL pipeline is fully unblocked.
- Phase 3 (RLock, milliseconds): catch up new entries added during Phase
2 using index-based slicing, flush the forwarder, then atomically add
segment to distribution.

Additional changes:
- Extract processDeleteRecords helper for BF check + forward logic.
- Move loadBM25Stats before loadStreamDelete so stats are ready before
segment becomes visible. Add rollback via UnregisterSealed on failure.
- Use one BufferForwarder per segment shared across Phase 2 and Phase 3,
flushed once after all records are processed.

issue: #47882

Signed-off-by: chyezh <chyezh@outlook.com>

title: fix: search without filter returns expired data when entity TTL is enabled
body:
When a collection has ttl_field configured, search() without a filter expression still returns expired entities. This is because entity-level TTL filtering is injected in CompileExpressions, which is only called when a FilterBitsNode exists in the plan. Search without filter builds the plan as MvccNode -> VectorSearchNode with no FilterBitsNode, so TTL filtering is completely skipped.

Add an AlwaysTrueExpr FilterBitsNode when no user filter is present but entity-level TTL is enabled, so CompileExpressions is invoked and merges the TTL filter expression at execution time.

issue: #47977
author: xiaocai2333

commits.message:
fix: search without filter returns expired data when entity TTL is enabled

When a collection has ttl_field configured, search() without a filter
expression still returns expired entities. This is because entity-level
TTL filtering is injected in CompileExpressions, which is only called
when a FilterBitsNode exists in the plan. Search without filter builds
the plan as MvccNode -> VectorSearchNode with no FilterBitsNode, so
TTL filtering is completely skipped.

Add an AlwaysTrueExpr FilterBitsNode when no user filter is present but
entity-level TTL is enabled, so CompileExpressions is invoked and merges
the TTL filter expression at execution time.

issue: #47977

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: test: Remove hardcoded metric_type from chaos test search params
body:
## Summary
- Remove `metric_type` from `DEFAULT_SEARCH_PARAM` and `DEFAULT_INT8_SEARCH_PARAM` in chaos test constants, letting the server auto-infer metric type from the index
- Fixes chaos test failures (broken since build #24014) where `AddVectorFieldChecker` creates COSINE-indexed vector fields but all search checkers hardcode `metric_type: L2`

## Root Cause
In chaos tests, `AddVectorFieldChecker` dynamically adds nullable FLOAT_VECTOR fields with **COSINE** HNSW index. After etcd pod-failure recovery, `SearchChecker`, `HybridSearchChecker`, and `NullVectorSearchChecker` re-initialize and discover these new fields. When they search on them using hardcoded `metric_type: L2`, the server returns:
- `metric type not match: invalid parameter[expected=COSINE][actual=L2]`
- `hybrid_search` 0% success rate (iterates ALL vector fields)
- `search`/`null_vector_search` ~28% success rate

Reference failure: [chaos-test-cron #24056](https://qa-jenkins.milvus.io/blue/organizations/jenkins/chaos-test-cron/detail/chaos-test-cron/24056/pipeline/)

## Test plan
- [x] Verified on Milvus master (`master-20260313-f163e94`): search without `metric_type` correctly auto-detects L2 and COSINE from index
- [x] Verified hybrid search across mixed metric types (L2 + COSINE) works without specifying `metric_type`
- [x] Backwards compatible: explicit `metric_type` in search params still works

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: yanliang567

commits.message:
test: Remove hardcoded metric_type from chaos test search params

Remove metric_type from DEFAULT_SEARCH_PARAM and DEFAULT_INT8_SEARCH_PARAM
so the server auto-infers it from the index. This fixes chaos test failures
where AddVectorFieldChecker creates COSINE-indexed fields but SearchChecker,
HybridSearchChecker and NullVectorSearchChecker hardcode L2 in search params,
causing metric type mismatch errors after etcd pod-failure recovery.

title: enhance: replace proto.Clone with shallow copy for query request fanout
body:
## Summary

Replace expensive `proto.Clone` with shallow copy for `QueryRequest` and `GetStatisticsRequest` when fanning out to worker nodes.

issue: #48074

### Key Improvements

- Add `shallowCopyRetrieveRequest()` function mirroring the search path pattern
- Modify `modifyQueryRequest()` to use shallow copy instead of proto.Clone
- Optimize inline lambda in `GetStatistics`

### Benchmark Results

| Scenario | proto.Clone | Shallow Copy | Speedup |
|----------|-------------|--------------|---------|
| 1KB payload | 396 ns/op | 0.3 ns/op | ~1300x |
| 64KB payload | 11,698 ns/op | 0.3 ns/op | ~38,000x |
| 10 workers | 4,306 ns/op | 2.6 ns/op | ~1,650x |

### Changes

- `internal/querynodev2/delegator/delegator.go`: Add shallow copy function, update query/stats paths
- `internal/querynodev2/delegator/shallow_copy_benchmark_test.go`: Add benchmark tests

### Test Plan

- [x] Benchmark tests verify performance improvement
- [x] Existing unit tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bigsheeper

commits.message:
enhance: replace proto.Clone with shallow copy for query request fanout

Replace expensive proto.Clone with shallow copy for QueryRequest and
GetStatisticsRequest when fanning out to worker nodes.

proto.Clone uses reflection-based deep copy which is expensive,
especially for requests with large SerializedExprPlan (up to 64KB).
Shallow copy shares read-only slice data while creating new Base
objects for mutable TargetID field.

Benchmark results show ~1000x+ performance improvement:
- 1KB payload: 396 ns/op → 0.3 ns/op
- 64KB payload: 11,698 ns/op → 0.3 ns/op
- 10 workers: 4,306 ns/op → 2.6 ns/op

title: fix: prevent node panic when unsupported types used as ClusteringKey
body:
## Summary

Fixes #47540

- **Bug 1 (MixCoord panic):** `compactionInspector.analyzeScheduler` was never initialized in production code. When FloatVector is used as ClusteringKey, the `doAnalyze()` path calls `analyzeScheduler.Enqueue()` on a nil pointer → panic. Fixed by passing `analyzeScheduler` to `newCompactionInspector`.
- **Bug 2 (DataNode round-robin panic):** `validateClusteringKey()` had no field type whitelist, so JSON/Bool/Array passed schema validation. During clustering compaction, `NewScalarFieldValue()` panics on unsupported types. Fixed by adding `IsClusteringKeyType()` check to reject unsupported types at collection creation time.

## Test plan

- [x] `TestIsClusteringKeyType` — verifies supported/unsupported type classification
- [x] `TestClusteringKey` — new sub-tests for JSON, Bool, Array as ClusteringKey (all rejected)
- [ ] Existing `TestClusteringKey` sub-tests (normal, multiple keys, vector key) still pass
- [ ] `TestCompactionPlanHandler*` tests pass with updated `newCompactionInspector` signature

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: xiaocai2333

commits.message:
fix: prevent node panic when unsupported types used as ClusteringKey (#47540)

Fix two bugs that cause node panics during clustering compaction:

1. FloatVector as ClusteringKey causes MixCoord nil pointer panic because
   compactionInspector.analyzeScheduler was never initialized in production
   code. Add analyzeScheduler parameter to newCompactionInspector.

2. JSON/Bool/Array as ClusteringKey causes DataNode panic because schema
   validation didn't check field type whitelist. Add IsClusteringKeyType()
   to reject unsupported types at collection creation time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Cai Zhang <cai.zhang@zilliz.com>

title: fix: pickNode should deduct task slots instead of zeroing available slots
body:
## Summary
- **Bug**: `pickNode` always set `AvailableSlots=0` for the selected node, limiting the scheduler to at most one task per node per scheduling cycle regardless of actual capacity
- **Fix**: Deduct only the required slot count (`AvailableSlots -= taskSlot`) when a node has sufficient capacity; zero-out only as a fallback when no node has enough slots
- **Tests**: Updated `TestGlobalScheduler_pickNode` to verify proper slot deduction across multiple consecutive picks and fallback behavior

issue: #48169

## Test plan
- [x] Updated `TestGlobalScheduler_pickNode` to cover:
  - Normal pick: verifies `AvailableSlots` decremented by `taskSlot` (not zeroed)
  - Multiple consecutive picks from same node: verifies cumulative deduction
  - Fallback path: when `taskSlot > AvailableSlots`, picks max-available node and zeros it
  - No available slots: returns `NullNodeID`
- [ ] CI: ut-go passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: xiaocai2333

commits.message:
fix: pickNode should deduct task slots instead of zeroing available slots

The old pickNode always set AvailableSlots=0 for the selected node,
limiting the scheduler to at most one task per node per cycle. Now it
deducts only the required slot count when capacity is sufficient, and
falls back to zero-out only when no node has enough capacity.

issue: #48169

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Cai Zhang <cai.zhang@zilliz.com>

title: fix: Use AlwaysTrueExpr instead of ValueExpr in combineOrInWithNotEqual
body:
combineOrInWithNotEqual used `newBoolConstExpr(true) (a ValueExpr)` when detecting tautologies like `(a IN S) OR (a != d) where d ∈ S`. This ValueExpr was not recognized by foldBinary's `IsAlwaysTrueExpr` check, causing it to pass through to the C++ execution engine. The C++ side compiled it into `PhyValueExpr` which returns a ConstantVector<bool>, but FilterBitsNode expects a ColumnVector, resulting in an assertion error.

Fix by using `newAlwaysTrueExpr()` so the expression is properly recognized and handled throughout the pipeline.

issue: #47997
author: xiaocai2333

commits.message:
fix: emit AlwaysTrueExpr instead of ValueExpr in combineOrInWithNotEqual

combineOrInWithNotEqual used newBoolConstExpr(true) (a ValueExpr) when
detecting tautologies like (a IN S) OR (a != d) where d ∈ S. This
ValueExpr was not recognized by foldBinary's IsAlwaysTrueExpr check,
causing it to pass through to the C++ execution engine. The C++ side
compiled it into PhyValueExpr which returns a ConstantVector<bool>,
but FilterBitsNode expects a ColumnVector, resulting in an assertion
error.

Fix by using newAlwaysTrueExpr() so the expression is properly
recognized and handled throughout the pipeline.

issue: #47997

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Cai Zhang <cai.zhang@zilliz.com>

title: fix: skip TLS hostname verification for internal proxy-to-proxy forwarding during upgrade
body:
The forward service's gRPC client used an empty tls.Config{}, causing TLS handshake failures when the resolver-provided ServerName ("proxy") didn't match the server certificate's SAN. Since this is a temporary internal connection only used during 2.5→2.6 rolling upgrades, skip certificate verification to avoid the mismatch.

issue: #48176

- Remove two O(N) temporary slices (`keys []uint32`, `values []int32`) in `BM25Stats.Deserialize`
- Read key/value directly into scalar variables and write to the map in one pass

enhance: reduce memory allocations in BM25Stats.Deserialize

title: fix: [2.6] set ValidData on user-provided $meta before partial update merge
body:
## Summary

Cherry-pick of #48084 to 2.6 branch.

When partial upsert includes user-provided dynamic fields and the batch mixes existing rows (update) with new rows (insert), the `$meta` field lacks `ValidData`, causing `FillWithDefaultValue` to fail with `the length of valid_data of field($meta) is wrong`.

Fix: initialize all-true `ValidData` on user-provided `$meta` in `PreExecute` before `queryPreExecute` runs.

pr: #48084
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47957
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: set ValidData on user-provided $meta before partial update merge

issue: #47957

When partial upsert includes user-provided dynamic fields and the batch
mixes existing rows (update) with new rows (insert), the $meta field
lacks ValidData. During queryPreExecute merge, the update path sets
ValidData on dstField (length=existRows), but the insert path's
AppendFieldDataByColumn skips ValidData propagation when src has none,
leaving ValidData length < total rows. FillWithDefaultValue then fails
with "length of valid_data is wrong".

Fix: initialize all-true ValidData on user-provided $meta in PreExecute
before queryPreExecute runs, consistent with autoGenDynamicFieldData.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: reduce memory allocations in BM25Stats.Deserialize
body:
enhance: reduce transient memory allocations in BM25Stats.Deserialize

issue: #48176

## Summary

- Remove two O(N) temporary slices (`keys []uint32`, `values []int32`) in `BM25Stats.Deserialize`
- Read key/value directly into scalar variables and write to the map in one pass
- No behavioral change; eliminates `2 × dim × 4` bytes of short-lived allocation per call

## Background

`Deserialize` is called from `FetchStats()` inside `SyncDistribution()` on streamingnode delegators. With large BM25 collections (119M rows, 119 sealed segments), each sync that activates segments triggers these redundant allocations, causing transient heap spikes observed in production monitoring.

## Test plan

- [ ] Existing `TestBM25Stats` unit tests pass
- [ ] Manual verification: serialize → deserialize round-trip produces identical results
author: xiaofan-luan

commits.message:
enhance: reduce memory allocations in BM25Stats.Deserialize

The previous implementation allocated two temporary slices (keys []uint32
and values []int32) before writing into the map. Since key and value are
consumed immediately, the slices are unnecessary.

Replace the two O(N) temporary allocations with a single pair of scalar
variables, reducing GC pressure during BM25 IDF oracle sync on
streamingnode delegators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: xiaofanluan <xiaofan.luan@zilliz.com>

title: fix: generate ManifestPath for StorageV3 growing segments at creation time
body:
Related to #48120

When pchannel migrates between streaming nodes, growing segments lose their ManifestPath because it was only generated locally in the write buffer metacache, never persisted to DataCoord meta. The new node gets an empty ManifestPath from GetSegmentInfo, causing "unexpected end of JSON input" errors on sync.

Generate ManifestPath in openNewSegmentWithGivenSegmentID when StorageVersion is V3, so segments are born with a valid manifest path in DataCoord meta.

Also fix typo: UnmarshalManfestPath → UnmarshalManifestPath across codebase.
author: congqixia

commits.message:
fix: generate ManifestPath for StorageV3 growing segments at creation time

Related to #48120

When pchannel migrates between streaming nodes, growing segments lose their
ManifestPath because it was only generated locally in the write buffer metacache,
never persisted to DataCoord meta. The new node gets an empty ManifestPath from
GetSegmentInfo, causing "unexpected end of JSON input" errors on sync.

Generate ManifestPath in openNewSegmentWithGivenSegmentID when StorageVersion is
V3, so segments are born with a valid manifest path in DataCoord meta.

Also fix typo: UnmarshalManfestPath → UnmarshalManifestPath across codebase.

Signed-off-by: Congqi Xia <congqi.xia@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: [2.6] add big topk optimization property
body:
issue: #48011 
pr: #47848 

  ## Summary
  - Add a new collection-level property `bigtopk_optimization.enabled` that allows collections to use significantly higher TopK limits (up to 1M by default vs 16384) for search and query operations
  - When enabled, auto-index creation selects an IVF-based index type (configurable via `autoIndex.params.bigTopK.build`, default IVF_SQ8) instead of the default HNSW, which is better suited for large TopK retrieval scenarios
  - Introduce dedicated quota parameters (`quotaAndLimits.limits.bigTopK` and `quotaAndLimits.limits.bigMaxQueryResultWindow`) to control the relaxed limits independently
  - The property can be set at collection creation or via alter collection, but changing it requires dropping any existing vector index first
- This PR also fix the partitionkey isolation alter bug when non related properties could affect isolation's vector index check
author: chasingegg

title: fix: fix flaky TestOnEvent and TestCachedConfig in pkg/config
body:
## Summary

Fixes #48186
PR: #48187

`TestOnEvent` and `TestCachedConfig` in `pkg/config/manager_test.go` intermittently fail on CI due to two issues:

1. **`assert.NoError` inside `assert.Eventually` callbacks** — calls `t.FailNow()` → `runtime.Goexit()` on the first error (e.g. config key not yet present during transition), terminating the goroutine immediately instead of retrying. Fixed by using `err == nil` guards.

2. **Hardcoded `/tmp/milvus/test` temp dirs** — causes directory collisions when tests run concurrently on CI. Fixed by using `t.TempDir()` for both etcd data dir and yaml config file dir (auto-cleaned by Go test framework).

## Test plan
- [ ] `go test -v -count=1 -tags "dynamic,test" -run TestOnEvent ./pkg/config/`
- [ ] `go test -v -count=1 -tags "dynamic,test" -run TestCachedConfig ./pkg/config/`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: xiaofan-luan

commits.message:
fix: fix flaky TestOnEvent and TestCachedConfig in pkg/config

Two issues causing intermittent CI failures:

1. Hardcoded /tmp/milvus/test for etcd and yaml temp dirs causes
   directory collisions when tests run concurrently. Use t.TempDir()
   instead so Go's test framework handles isolation and cleanup.

2. assert.NoError inside assert.Eventually callbacks calls t.FailNow()
   via runtime.Goexit on the first error (e.g. key not yet present),
   terminating the goroutine immediately instead of retrying. Replace
   with err == nil guards so Eventually can keep polling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: xiaofanluan <xiaofan.luan@zilliz.com>

fix: prevent cache eviction when no config changes in EvictCacheValueByFormat

Background refreshers (FileSource + EtcdSource) call EvictCacheValueByFormat
even when there are no config changes (empty events slice), causing the entire
config cache to be cleared every refresh interval (e.g. 10ms). This makes
CASCachedValue → GetCachedValue sequences racy in TestCachedConfig.

Add early return in EvictCacheValueByFormat when len(keys) == 0.
Also harden TestCachedConfig: check CASCachedValue return value with
require.True and use assert.Eventually instead of time.Sleep.

Signed-off-by: xiaofanluan <xiaofan.luan@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: test: Fix chaos checker dimension mismatch and vector field limit handling
body:
## Summary

Fix two bugs in chaos test checkers that cause `test_concurrent_operation.py` to fail consistently (broken since build #24014 in chaos-test-cron):

- **Op.hybrid_search 0% success rate**: `HybridSearchChecker.gen_hybrid_search_request()` used a single cached `self.dim` for all vector fields. When `AddVectorFieldChecker` adds fields with different dimensions to the same collection, hybrid search sends vectors with wrong dimensions, causing `vector dimension mismatch` errors.
- **Op.add_vector_field 0% success rate**: Hardcoded `dim=32` created dimension inconsistency with the collection schema (default `dim=128`). The pre-check via `describe_collection` field counting was unreliable — replaced with server-side error handling that catches `maximum vector field` error and gracefully falls back to insert-only.

### Changes

**`tests/python_client/chaos/checker.py`:**
- `HybridSearchChecker.gen_hybrid_search_request()`: Query each vector field's actual dimension from schema via `get_schema()` instead of using a single cached dim
- `AddVectorFieldChecker.add_vector_field()`: Use `self.dim` instead of hardcoded `dim=32`; catch server-side "maximum vector field" error and fallback to insert

### Jenkins failure reference
https://qa-jenkins.milvus.io/blue/organizations/jenkins/chaos-test-cron/detail/chaos-test-cron/24056/pipeline

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: yanliang567

commits.message:
test: Fix chaos checker dimension mismatch and vector field limit handling

1. HybridSearchChecker: Query each vector field's actual dimension from
   schema instead of using a single cached dim for all fields. When
   AddVectorFieldChecker adds fields with different dimensions, hybrid
   search now uses the correct dimension per field.

2. AddVectorFieldChecker: Use self.dim instead of hardcoded dim=32 to
   keep new vector fields consistent with the collection's schema.
   Replace unreliable pre-check with server-side error handling -
   catch "maximum vector field" error and gracefully fallback to
   insert-only operation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: yanliang567 <82361606+yanliang567@users.noreply.github.com>

title: fix: empty result set crashes Milvus on GROUP BY aggregation query
body:
## Summary

Fixes #47316

- **GroupingSet::outputRowCount()**: Add null check for `lookup_` to prevent SIGSEGV when filter matches zero rows and no input data arrives
- **ExecPlanNodeVisitor::setupRetrieveResult()**: When query result is nullptr, build empty field_data arrays with correct schema (0 rows, N columns) so downstream receives proper column structure instead of missing columns
- Enable previously skipped E2E test `test_empty_result_aggregation`

## Test plan

- [x] E2E test `test_empty_result_aggregation` passes (GROUP BY + aggregation with filter matching 0 rows returns empty result `[]`)
- [ ] Existing aggregation E2E tests still pass
- [ ] CI green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: MrPresent-Han

commits.message:
fix: empty result set crashes Milvus on GROUP BY aggregation query (#47316)

Two fixes for empty result set handling in query pipeline:

1. GroupingSet::outputRowCount() - add null check for lookup_ to prevent
   SIGSEGV when no input data arrives (empty filter result).

2. ExecPlanNodeVisitor::setupRetrieveResult() - when result is nullptr,
   build empty field_data arrays with correct schema (0 rows, N columns)
   so downstream receives proper column structure.

Also enable the previously skipped E2E test for empty result aggregation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: MrPresent-Han <chun.han@gmail.com>

title: fix: [2.6] remove interactive logic between import and L0 compaction
body:
The interactive coordination between import and L0 compaction (pause/resume channels, skip collections, L0 import task waiting) causes BulkInsert to fail with "too many input paths for binlog import" when L0 compaction triggers during import. Remove this coordination entirely so import and L0 compaction operate independently.

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47762

pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/47768

The interactive coordination between import and L0 compaction (pause/resume

with "too many input paths for binlog import" when L0 compaction triggers
during import. Remove this coordination entirely so import and L0 compaction
operate independently.

title: enhance: add async warmup policy support for caching layer
body:
issue: #47902

Integrate milvus-common commit 7b54b6e which adds CacheWarmupPolicy_Async and a prefetch thread pool for background cache warmup. This enables segments to be marked as loaded immediately while cache warming happens asynchronously, reducing load latency.
author: sparknack

commits.message:
enhance: add async warmup policy support for tiered storage cache

Integrate milvus-common commit 7b54b6e which adds CacheWarmupPolicy_Async
and a prefetch thread pool for background cache warmup. This enables
segments to be marked as loaded immediately while cache warming happens
asynchronously, reducing load latency.

Signed-off-by: Shawn Wang <shawn.wang@zilliz.com>

enhance: cancel async warmup before evicting or destroying cache slots

Cancel any in-progress async warmup before evicting or dropping a cache
slot so that the prefetch task aborts mid-load (via the OpContext token)
rather than completing wasted I/O and keeping the slot alive longer than
necessary.

Signed-off-by: Shawn Wang <shawn.wang@zilliz.com>

add some testcases

Signed-off-by: Shawn Wang <shawn.wang@zilliz.com>

update milvus-common

Signed-off-by: Shawn Wang <shawn.wang@zilliz.com>

Added Rust FFI function to control Tantivy log level via log::set_max_level() and call it from C++ SetLogLevel() to sync levels across all subsystems. Removed MY_LOG_LEVEL environment variable dependency from Tantivy initialization.

log levels across Go zap, C++ glog, and Rust tantivy subsystems. Remove MY_LOG_LEVEL

title: fix: [RBAC][2.6] grant cleanup on drop and migration on rename and meta cache interceptor
body:
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48140

related: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48061
related: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48062
related: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48137

Reconstruct logical etcd keys to avoid double rootPath prefix in
delete/migrate operations
Use typeutil.After for privilege name extraction instead of broken
length-based substring
Match wildcard dbName and use DefaultTenant consistently
Move grant cleanup to DropCollection ack callback
Enable resolveAliasForPrivilege by default and fix alias cache
passed rbac alias e2e tests
author: shaoting-huang

commits.message:
fix: fix RBAC grant cleanup on drop and migration on rename

  - Reconstruct logical etcd keys to avoid double rootPath prefix in
    delete/migrate operations
  - Use typeutil.After for privilege name extraction instead of broken
    length-based substring
  - Match wildcard dbName  and use DefaultTenant consistently
  - Move grant cleanup to DropCollection ack callback
  - Enable resolveAliasForPrivilege by default and fix alias cache

  issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48061
  issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48062
  e2e: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48137

Signed-off-by: shaoting-huang <shaoting.huang@zilliz.com>

fix: fix RBAC alias cache pollution, grant half-migration, and crash recovery

- Prevent empty collectionName from being treated as alias in MetaCache
  UpdateByID path, which polluted the alias cache with empty-string keys
- Move GranteeID LoadWithPrefix before parent key queuing in
  MigrateGrantCollectionName to avoid half-migration when child load fails
- Add idempotent DeleteGrantByCollectionName in RemoveCollection as safety
  net for crash recovery between state change and grant deletion

Signed-off-by: shaoting-huang <shaoting.huang@zilliz.com>

fix yaml

Signed-off-by: shaoting-huang <shaoting.huang@zilliz.com>

fix conflict

Signed-off-by: shaoting-huang <shaoting.huang@zilliz.com>

fix: fix CI failures for 2.6 branch compatibility

- Remove extra blank line in meta_cache.go to pass gofmt check
- Add missing DeleteGrantByCollectionName mock expectation in
  TestMetaTable_RemoveCollection/normal_case (RemoveCollection still
  calls it as safety net)
- Remove fileResourceRefCnt field from MetaTable struct literals in
  TestMetaTable_DropCollection_GrantCleanup (field does not exist on 2.6)

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address code review findings for RBAC alias PR

P0: Do not delete/migrate wildcard (*.col) grants on single-collection
drop/rename. Wildcard grants span all databases; dropping db1.col1 must
not remove *.col1 which may still protect db2.col1.

P1: Use nil-safe accessors for schema in meta_cache.go to prevent
potential nil pointer panics in ResolveCollectionAlias and
removeCollectionByID.

P1: Add test case with rootPath-prefixed etcd keys to verify the
double-prefix fix actually works end-to-end.

P2: Restore TestMetaTable_RemoveCollection_GrantDeleteBestEffort to
cover the fallback grant cleanup path in RemoveCollection.

title: fix: [2.6] remove bounded channel capacity for system field loading to prevent deadlock
body:
Cherry-pick from master
pr: #48036
When loading V1 segments with system fields (RowID/Timestamp) that have more than 16 binlog files, a deadlock occurs because the producer (LoadArrowReaderFromRemote) and consumer (load_system_field_internal) run sequentially on the same thread with a bounded channel. The producer fills the channel and blocks, while the consumer never starts.

Remove the set_capacity call so the channel is unbounded, matching the approach used in ChunkTranslator.

issue: #48035

title: enhance: [loon] move storage v3 deltalog files under basePath/_delta/ to align with loon convention
body:
Related to #44956

Previously, V3 deltalogs were written to {rootPath}/delta_log/{collID}/{partID}/{segID}/{logID}, separate from the segment's basePath. The manifest used complex "../" relative paths to bridge the two locations. This change writes V3 deltalogs directly to {basePath}/_delta/{logID}, aligning with the C loon library's native _delta/ convention and simplifying the manifest relative path to just the logID filename. Legacy V1 segments and existing manifests remain backward compatible.

title: enhance: [loon] support loading deltalogs from StorageV3 manifest in QueryNode
body:
Related to #44956

Change LoadDeltaLogs interface to accept *querypb.SegmentLoadInfo instead of []*datapb.FieldBinlog, enabling access to ManifestPath. When a segment has a manifest path, deltalogs are also read from the Loon manifest via storage.NewDeltalogReaderFromManifest in addition to traditional binlog paths. Add createStorageConfig helper for building storage config from paramtable.

title: fix: [2.6] Fix rate limit not work when set collection rate limit properties (#46714)
body:
pr: #46714

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/46713 /kind bug

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
- Core invariant: a Limiter's hasUpdated flag controls whether its non-Infinite limit is exported to proxies via toRequestLimiter(); only limiters with hasUpdated==true and non-Inf rates produce rate updates delivered to proxies (pkg/util/ratelimitutil/limiter.go: HasUpdated / toRequestLimiter behavior unchanged).

- Exact bug and fix (issue #46713): collection-level limiters created from configured collection/partition/database properties were constructed with correct limits but left hasUpdated==false, so they were skipped by the existing !HasUpdated() check and never sent to proxies. Fix: add Limiter.SetHasUpdated(updated bool) and call a new updateLimiterHasUpdated helper immediately after creating limiter nodes during initialization/reset (internal/rootcoord/quota_center.go) to mark non-Inf newly-created limiters as updated so they are included in toRequestLimiter exports.

- Logic simplified / redundancy removed: initialization now explicitly sets limiter initialization state (hasUpdated) for newly-created non-Infinite limiters instead of relying on implicit later side-effects to toggle the flag; this removes the implicit gap between creation and the expectation that a configured limiter should be published.

- No data-loss or behavior regression: the change only mutates the in-memory hasUpdated flag for freshly created limiter instances (pkg/util/ratelimitutil/limiter.go: SetHasUpdated) and sets it in the limiter initialization path (internal/rootcoord/quota_center.go). It does not alter token accounting (advance, AllowN, Cancel), rate computation, SetLimit semantics, persistence, or proxy filtering logic—only ensures intended collection-level rates are delivered to proxies—so no persisted data or runtime rate behavior is removed or degraded.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: guanghuihuang88

title: enhance:[2.6] propagate TLS minimum version config to C++ storage layer (#48028)
body:
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48028
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44999

The previous PR (#48000) added the configurable TLS minimum version on the Go side but didn't wire it through to the C++ core. This commit fills that gap by adding the tls_min_version field to CStorageConfig and passing it down to all the places that build storage configs — index builder, clustering analyzer, packed reader/writer, chunk manager, arrow filesystem, and the loon FFI layer. Also bumps milvus-storage to a version that supports the new property.
author: jiaqizho

enhance:[2.6] propagate TLS minimum version config to C++ storage layer (#48028)

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44999

the Go side but didn't wire it through to the C++ core. This commit
fills that gap by adding the tls_min_version field to CStorageConfig and
passing it down to all the places that build storage configs — index
builder, clustering analyzer, packed reader/writer, chunk manager, arrow
filesystem, and the loon FFI layer. Also bumps milvus-storage to a
version that supports the new property.

title: fix: [2.6] restore hard-coded thread pool size limit of 16
body:
## Summary
- Restore the hard-coded maximum of 16 threads for the C++ IO thread pool
- In large file scenarios, CGo threads can consume all available thread resources, starving Go goroutines
- This reverts commit ffd3435c4f and the related test changes from a41d170c14

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48060
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48128

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify thread pool size is clamped to 16 via C++ unit test (`ThreadPoolTest.ResizeAboveMaximum`)
- [ ] Test large file load scenarios to confirm Go goroutines are no longer starved

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

title: fix: Optimize namespace compaction and query implementation
body:
### **User description** issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44011 Updated the DescribeCollection functionality to exclude the namespace field from the response schema. This change ensures that these fields are not returned in the collection description. ___ ### **PR Type** Bug fix, Enhancement ___ ### **Description** - Filter namespace field from DescribeCollection response schema - Add support for PartitionKeySortCompaction in compaction task handling - Fix lambda capture issues in C++ expression evaluation code - Add comprehensive test coverage for namespace field filtering ___ ### Diagram Walkthrough ```mermaid flowchart LR A["DescribeCollection Request"] --> B["Filter Fields"] B --> C["Exclude Dynamic Fields"] B --> D["Exclude Namespace Field"] C --> E["Response Schema"] D --> E F["Compaction Task"] --> G["Create/Complete Task"] G --> H["Support PartitionKeySortCompaction"] H --> I["Task Execution"] ```

File Walkthrough
Relevant files
Bug fix
service_provider.go
Filter namespace field from DescribeCollection response   
internal/proxy/service_provider.go
  • Added import for common package to access NamespaceFieldName constant
  • Modified field filtering logic in DescribeCollection to exclude
    namespace field alongside dynamic fields
  • Updated filter condition to check both IsDynamic flag and field name
    equality
 +2/-1     
task.go
Exclude namespace field from task execution                           
internal/proxy/task.go
  • Updated describeCollectionTask.Execute to filter out namespace field
    from response schema
  • Modified condition to skip fields that are either dynamic or have
    namespace field name
  • Ensures namespace field is not included in collection description
    results
 +1/-1     
Expr.cpp
Fix lambda capture and remove duplicate includes                 
internal/core/src/exec/expression/Expr.cpp
  • Removed duplicate include of expr/ITypeExpr.h
  • Removed unused include of fmt/format.h
  • Fixed lambda capture issues in SetNamespaceSkipIndex function by using
    pointer capture
  • Changed from reference capture [&] to explicit pointer and value
    captures to avoid dangling references
  • Extracted namespace field ID and value as const variables for safer
    lambda capture
 +16/-16 
Tests
service_provider_test.go
Add test for namespace field filtering                                     
internal/proxy/service_provider_test.go
  • Added imports for schemapb and common packages
  • Created new test
    TestCachedProxyServiceProvider_DescribeCollection_FilterNamespaceField
  • Test verifies namespace and dynamic fields are filtered while user
    fields are preserved
  • Uses mock cache to simulate collection metadata with namespace field
 +65/-0   
task_test.go
Add namespace field filtering test case                                   
internal/proxy/task_test.go
  • Added comprehensive test
    TestDescribeCollectionTask_FilterNamespaceField
  • Test creates schema with namespace field as partition key and dynamic
    metadata field
  • Verifies both namespace and dynamic fields are excluded from describe
    result
  • Confirms user fields like id and fvec are properly included
 +90/-0   
Enhancement
compaction_inspector.go
Support PartitionKeySortCompaction in task creation           
internal/datacoord/compaction_inspector.go
  • Added datapb.CompactionType_PartitionKeySortCompaction to switch case
    in createCompactTask
  • Now handles PartitionKeySortCompaction alongside MixCompaction and
    SortCompaction
  • Routes PartitionKeySortCompaction to newMixCompactionTask handler
 +1/-1     
meta.go
Handle PartitionKeySortCompaction in mutation completion 
internal/datacoord/meta.go
  • Added datapb.CompactionType_PartitionKeySortCompaction to switch case
    in CompleteCompactionMutation
  • Routes PartitionKeySortCompaction to completeSortCompactionMutation
    handler
  • Ensures proper mutation completion for partition key sort compaction
    type
 +1/-1     
___ - Core invariant: schemapb.CollectionSchema.EnableNamespace is the authoritative per-collection flag for namespace behavior — it is persisted on Collection models, copied into DescribeCollection responses, and used at runtime to derive datapb.SegmentInfo.IsNamespaceSorted and datapb.CompactionSegmentBinlogs/CompactionSegment.IsNamespaceSorted (i.e., per-collection EnableNamespace → per-segment IsNamespaceSorted). - Removed/simplified logic: eliminated property-based namespace toggles and partition-key-sort special-cases (TriggerTypePartitionKeySort / TriggerTypeClusteringPartitionKeySort and IsPartitionKeySortCompactionEnabled) and removed the dual namespace-skip API in expression evaluation (SetNamespaceSkipFunc / SetNamespaceSkipIndex); routing now reuses existing Mix/Sort/Clustering handlers and uniformly treats IsNamespaceSorted alongside IsSorted in compaction, index, and inspection checks. - Why no data loss or behavior regression: wire compatibility preserved (SegmentInfo field number unchanged when renaming is_partition_key_sorted → is_namespace_sorted), binlog/segment IDs, manifest paths and storage versions are unchanged, and compaction requests only add/pass an extra boolean flag — no binlog content, identifiers, or storage layout are modified; DescribeCollection now only changes user-facing filtering of schema fields (internal stored schema unchanged) and unit tests were added for filtering to prevent regressions. - Bug fix (milvus-io/milvus#44011): fixes leakage of internal namespace/meta fields in DescribeCollection by filtering out fields with IsDynamic==true and the Namespace field name (common.NamespaceFieldName) and by propagating EnableNamespace through DescribeCollectionTask.Execute; tests added in internal/proxy/service_provider_test.go and internal/proxy/task_test.go validate the fix.
author: sunby

title: enhance: [2.6] bump OpenTelemetry to v1.40.0 to fix CWE-426 untrusted search path
body:
Related to #48070

Upgrade go.opentelemetry.io/otel and related packages from v1.34.0 to v1.40.0 across all Go modules to address CWE-426 (Untrusted Search Path) vulnerability. Also bumps transitive dependencies including auto/sdk v1.1.0 -> v1.2.1, go-logr v1.4.2 -> v1.4.3, and golang.org/x/sys v0.38.0 -> v0.40.0.

See: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/426.html

title: enhance: optimize count aggregation performance for nullable fields
body:
## Summary
- Optimize `addSingleGroupRawInput` (global aggregation): replace O(N) row-by-row `ValidAt()` loop with O(1) `nullCount()`-based computation
- Optimize `addRawInput` (GROUP BY): add `nullCount()==0` fast path to skip validity checks entirely; for columns with actual nulls, use direct bitmap access instead of virtual `ValidAt()` calls

issue: #47566

## Test plan
- [ ] Existing aggregation E2E tests pass
- [ ] C++ unit tests for query aggregation pass
- [ ] Benchmark nullable vs non-nullable count aggregation performance

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: MrPresent-Han

reviews.body:
## PR #48055 Review: optimize count aggregation performance for nullable fields

### 整体评价

这个 PR 对 count 聚合的两个热路径做了合理的性能优化。优化策略正确：全局聚合用 O(1) 算术替代 O(N) 循环，分组聚合通过快速路径和去虚拟化加速。变更量小且聚焦。

---

### 🔴 Bug Risk: `addRawInput` 快速路径循环变量使用 `numGroups` 而非 `input_column->size()`

**文件**: `CountAggregateBase.h`, 第 63-66 行

```cpp
if (input_column->nullCount() == 0) {
    for (auto i = 0; i < numGroups; i++) {  // ← should use input_column->size()
        addToGroup(groups[i], 1);
    }
}
```

原始代码遍历 `input_column->size()`，新代码遍历 `numGroups`。从调用方 `GroupingSet::addInputForActiveRows` 可以确认 `numGroups` 实际上等于输入行数（`hits.size()` 来自 groupProbe，对应每个输入行的分组映射），所以功能上当前没有差异——但这里有两个问题：

1. **语义不一致**：在 else 分支中使用了 `input_column->size()`（第 73 行），但快速路径用 `numGroups`，同一函数内对同一语义用不同变量，容易造成混淆
2. **脆弱性**：`numGroups` 的语义是"分组映射数组的大小"而非"列的行数"，虽然当前调用方保证相等，但未来如果聚合框架演进（比如支持 selection vector/过滤），两者可能不再相等

**建议**: 统一使用 `input_column->size()` 保持与 else 分支一致。

---

### 🟡 `reinterpret_cast<const uint64_t*>` 的正当性确认

**文件**: `CountAggregateBase.h`, 第 71-72 行

```cpp
const uint64_t* validity = reinterpret_cast<const uint64_t*>(
    input_column->GetValidRawData());
```

检查了底层实现链：
- `valid_values_` 是 `TargetBitmap` = `CustomBitset` = `Bitset<VectorizedElementWiseBitsetPolicy<uint64_t, ...>, fbvector<uint8_t>, true>`
- `GetValidRawData()` 返回 `valid_values_.data()`，即 `fbvector<uint8_t>` 的底层存储
- 位操作 `validity[i >> 6] & (uint64_t(1) << (i & 63))` 使用 LSB-first 位序

✅ 这与 `Vector.h` 中已有的模式一致（第 331-333 行、373-375 行已有相同的 `reinterpret_cast`）。

需要注意：
- **对齐**：`fbvector<uint8_t>` 的分配器通常返回 >= 16-byte 对齐内存，uint64_t 对齐满足。代码库已建立此模式。
- **尾部安全性**：当 `size()` 不是 64 的倍数时，循环只到 `i < input_column->size()`，不会读到超出范围的位，**安全**。但需要确保底层容器分配了足够字节包含最后一个完整 uint64_t word（避免越界读），`CustomBitset` 的 resize 应该保证了这一点。

---

### ✅ `addSingleGroupRawInput` 优化 — 正确且干净

```cpp
addToGroup(group, column->size() - static_cast<int64_t>(column->nullCount()));
```

O(N) 循环到 O(1) 算术的完美等价变换。`nullCount()` 返回 `size_t`，`static_cast<int64_t>` 确保减法正确。Bitmap 路径保持不变，语义一致。

---

##

## Round 3 Review — Final Consolidated Response

### 1. Coverage completeness

Only one file changed (`CountAggregateBase.h`), two functions modified. Both fully reviewed in Round 1. No additional files or functions to cover.

### 2. What codex-cli missed or got wrong

**codex-cli Round 3 introduced a new "High" finding about `null_count_` cache staleness — this is incorrect for this PR's scope.**

codex-cli cites expression paths like `CompareExpr.cpp` and `JsonContainsExpr.cpp` where `valid_res[i] = false` mutates validity bits through a `TargetBitmapView` without updating `null_count_`. This is true, but **irrelevant** to count aggregation input columns:

- I traced the full data flow: `GroupingSet::addInput` → `populateTempVectors` → `input->child(channel_idxes[i])`. The input columns are **data columns from the RowVector**, not expression result vectors.
- Expression evaluation creates **separate** `ColumnVector` instances (`res_vec`) for filter results. These never flow into the aggregation operator's data input.
- Data columns' `null_count_` is set at construction time (segment loading) via the constructor at `Vector.h:145-156` which computes it by scanning the validity bitmap. After construction, data columns' validity bitmaps are not mutated by expression paths.

So `null_count_` cache staleness in expression result vectors is a **pre-existing codebase design concern** but does NOT affect the correctness of this PR's optimizations. Labeling it "High" is misleading.

codex-cli also said `addSingleGroupRawInput` as "no risk" is wrong because of this concern. I disagree — for the reasons above, `nullCount()` is authoritative for the data columns that flow into aggregation.

### 3. Response to codex-cli Round 3 points

| Point | My assessment |
|-------|--------------|
| `null_count_` cache staleness (new "High") | **Disagree** — expression result vectors ≠ aggregation input vectors. Different ColumnVector instances. Not a risk for this PR. |
| `numGroups` loop

commits.message:
enhance: optimize count aggregation performance for nullable fields (#47566)

- addSingleGroupRawInput: replace O(N) row-by-row ValidAt() loop with
  O(1) nullCount()-based computation for non-bitmap columns
- addRawInput (GROUP BY): add nullCount()==0 fast path to skip validity
  checks entirely; for columns with nulls, use direct bitmap access
  instead of virtual ValidAt() calls

Signed-off-by: MrPresent-Han <chun.han@gmail.com>

title: enhance:[2.6] support configurable TLS minimum version for object storage connections (#48000)
body:
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48000
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44999

Currently Milvus doesn't allow users to control the TLS version used when connecting to object storage (MinIO/S3/Azure/GCP). Some environments require enforcing TLS 1.3 for compliance, but there's no way to set that today.

This adds a new config option `minio.ssl.tlsMinVersion` that lets users specify the minimum TLS version ("1.0", "1.1", "1.2", "1.3", or "default"). It works across all supported storage backends including MinIO/S3, Azure Blob, and GCP native. The setting is plumbed through paramtable, proto StorageConfig, and all the places that create storage clients (compaction, datacoord, datanode, storagev2, etc.).

For the GCP native backend, this also adds proper UseIAM/ADC support that was previously missing, since the TLS transport injection needed to handle both credential modes correctly.

Also fixed the GCP MinIO-compatible path to reuse any custom transport (e.g. with TLS config) as the backend for the OAuth2 token wrapping, instead of always creating a new default transport.

Unit tests cover the TLS version parsing, HTTP client construction, and version enforcement (proving a TLS 1.3 client correctly rejects a TLS 1.2-only server). Integration tests are included but gated behind environment variables.
author: jiaqizho

commits.message:
enhance:[2.6] support configurable TLS minimum version for object storage connections (#48000)

Related to https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44999

Currently Milvus doesn't allow users to control the TLS version used
when connecting to object storage (MinIO/S3/Azure/GCP). Some
environments require enforcing TLS 1.3 for compliance, but there's no
way to set that today.

This adds a new config option `minio.ssl.tlsMinVersion` that lets users
specify the minimum TLS version ("1.0", "1.1", "1.2", "1.3", or
"default"). It works across all supported storage backends including
MinIO/S3, Azure Blob, and GCP native. The setting is plumbed through
paramtable, proto StorageConfig, and all the places that create storage
clients (compaction, datacoord, datanode, storagev2, etc.).

For the GCP native backend, this also adds proper UseIAM/ADC support
that was previously missing, since the TLS transport injection needed to
handle both credential modes correctly.

Also fixed the GCP MinIO-compatible path to reuse any custom transport
(e.g. with TLS config) as the backend for the OAuth2 token wrapping,
instead of always creating a new default transport.

Unit tests cover the TLS version parsing, HTTP client construction, and
version enforcement (proving a TLS 1.3 client correctly rejects a TLS
1.2-only server). Integration tests are included but gated behind
environment variables.

Signed-off-by: jiaqizho <jiaqi.zhou@zilliz.com>

title: enhance:[2.6.10] propagate TLS minimum version config to C++ storage layer (#48028)
body:
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48028
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44999

The previous PR (#48000) added the configurable TLS minimum version on the Go side but didn't wire it through to the C++ core. This commit fills that gap by adding the tls_min_version field to CStorageConfig and passing it down to all the places that build storage configs — index builder, clustering analyzer, packed reader/writer, chunk manager, arrow filesystem, and the loon FFI layer. Also bumps milvus-storage to a version that supports the new property.
author: jiaqizho

commits.message:
enhance:[2.6.10] propagate TLS minimum version config to C++ storage layer (#48028)

passing it down to all the places that build storage configs — index
builder, clustering analyzer, packed reader/writer, chunk manager, arrow

title: enhance: [2.6] optimize mixcoord cpu usage
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47055
master pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/47618
author: sijie-ni-0214

commits.message:
enhance: optimize logging to reduce CPU overhead

- Add fast path in WithRateGroup to avoid creating RateLimiter when already exists
- Add lazy log initialization in checkDelegatorServiceable for serviceable delegators
- Remove duplicate log in GetQueryVChanPositions (already logged in GetRecoveryInfoV2)

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

enhance: optimize MixCoord metrics to avoid JSON marshal/unmarshal overhead

Add direct topology methods to MixCoord interface that return struct pointers
instead of JSON strings. This optimization eliminates unnecessary JSON
serialization/deserialization when coordinators call each other within the
same process (MixCoord mode).

Changes:
- Add GetDataCoordTopology and GetQueryCoordTopology methods to MixCoord interface
- Refactor datacoord and querycoord to expose topology struct directly
- Update QuotaCenter to use direct topology methods
- Update tests to use new interface

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

enhance: optimize SegmentChecker and ChannelChecker with version-based skip

Add version cache to skip collection checks when target and dist versions
are unchanged. This reduces CPU overhead by avoiding unnecessary replica
iteration when nothing has changed.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

fix: add missing MixCoord interface methods to mockMixCoord in datacoord tests

Add GetDataCoordTopology and GetQueryCoordTopology methods to
mockMixCoord to satisfy the updated MixCoord interface.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

reviews.body: Minor note: `setenv.sh` (around line 67) has a comment saying "LLVM 18 is NOT supported because Conan 1.x cannot handle the newer", but `DEVELOPMENT.md` was just updated to list Clang 15–18 as supported. Could you align these two? If LLVM 18 works now with the upgraded deps, the `setenv.sh` comment should be updated; if not, `DEVELOPMENT.md` should say 15–17.

commits.message:
fix: upgrade C++ dependencies for macOS 15 compatibility

Upgrade Conan dependencies to fix compilation on macOS 15+:
- fmt: 9.1.0 → 11.0.2 (fix atomic/json/protobuf-enum formatting)
- folly: 2023.10.30.08 → 2024.08.12.00 (WriteHolder/ReadHolder → std locks)
- glog: 0.6.0 → 0.7.1 (LogAtLevel signature change)
- protobuf: 3.21.4 → 5.27.0
- google-cloud-cpp: 2.5.0 → 2.28.0 (auth API changes)
- opentelemetry-cpp: 1.8.1.1 → 1.23.0 (with_stl=True, STL version 2017)

title: fix: Prevent GC from deleting index files referenced by snapshots
body:
## Summary

- Add buildID tracking to `SnapshotRefIndex` to precisely protect snapshot-referenced index files from GC deletion
- Fix the `segIdx==nil` branch in `recycleUnusedIndexFiles` where orphan index files were deleted without snapshot checks (root cause of persistent RestoreSnapshot failures after PR #47669)
- Add snapshot protection to `recycleUnusedBinLogWithChecker`, `recycleUnusedTextIndexFiles`, and `recycleUnusedJSONStatsFiles`
- Use per-collection `IsRefIndexLoadedForCollection` instead of global `IsAllRefIndexLoaded` where collection context is available

issue: #47658

## Root Cause

Previous fix PR #47669 added snapshot protection to GC paths but missed the `segIdx==nil` branch in `recycleUnusedIndexFiles`. After compaction, `recycleUnusedSegIndexes` removes old segment index metadata. Later, `recycleUnusedIndexFiles` walks storage, finds orphan index files, and `CheckCleanSegmentIndex(buildID)` returns `(true, nil)`. The `segIdx==nil` branch deleted all files without any snapshot reference check.

## Fix Approach

Add `build_ids` field to `SnapshotMetadata` proto and `SnapshotRefIndex`. When a snapshot is saved, all buildIDs from segment index files are extracted and persisted. GC functions use `GetSnapshotByBuildID(buildID)` for direct set lookups instead of relying on `segIdx` for collection/index context.

## Test plan

- [x] All 433 datacoord unit tests pass
- [x] `make milvus` build OK
- [x] `make lint-fix` lint OK
- [ ] E2E snapshot performance test suite (RS-01, RS-04, SI-02 scenarios)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: Prevent GC from deleting index files referenced by snapshots

issue: #47658

Previous fix PR #47669 added snapshot protection to GC paths, but
missed the segIdx==nil branch in recycleUnusedIndexFiles. When a
segment's index metadata is removed by recycleUnusedSegIndexes after
compaction, recycleUnusedIndexFiles later finds orphan index files
and deletes them without checking snapshot references.

Root fix: add buildID tracking to SnapshotRefIndex. Index files are
organized by buildID, so a direct buildID set lookup replaces the
expensive WalkWithPrefix path parsing and eliminates the need for
segIdx to perform snapshot checks.

Key changes:
- Add build_ids field to SnapshotMetadata proto and SnapshotData
- Add GetSnapshotByBuildID to snapshotMeta for buildID-based queries
- Fix segIdx==nil branch in recycleUnusedIndexFiles with buildID check
- Replace indexID-based check in recycleUnusedSegIndexes with buildID
- Add snapshot protection to recycleUnusedBinLogWithChecker,
  recycleUnusedTextIndexFiles, and recycleUnusedJSONStatsFiles
- Use per-collection IsRefIndexLoadedForCollection where possible
- Fix CopyObject error reporting to use source object name

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: test: add entity TTL E2E test cases
body:
## Summary
- Add comprehensive E2E test cases for the Entity TTL feature (#47482)
- Cover collection creation/alter with `ttl_field`, insert with future/expired/null TTL values, upsert to extend/shorten TTL, delete before expiry, release/reload behavior, and query/search filtering of expired data
- Add invalid parameter test cases: non-timestamptz field, nonexistent field, nullable=false, and conflict with collection-level TTL
- Fix existing `test_milvus_client_ttl_edge` to properly assert error on out-of-range TTL

## Test plan
- [x] All new test cases pass against a Milvus instance with entity TTL support enabled
- [x] Existing TTL test cases remain passing

issue: #47482
author: FeilongHou

reviews.body:
## Test Review for Entity TTL E2E Test Cases

### Overview

The PR adds ~1100 lines of Entity TTL E2E tests across 3 test classes with 20+ test cases, plus a small fix to re-enable the multi-analyzer highlighter test (BUG #46498 resolved).

### Strengths

1. **Comprehensive coverage**: CRUD on ttl_field property, future/past/NULL TTL values, mixed TTL values, upsert extend/shorten/preserve TTL, release/reload persistence, explicit delete vs TTL expiry, re-insert after expiry.
2. **Good negative tests** (`TestMilvusClientEntityTTLInvalid`): non-TIMESTAMPTZ type, non-existent field, entity TTL + collection TTL mutual exclusion, non-nullable NULL insert.
3. **Shared collection pattern** (`TestMilvusClientEntityTTLQuery`): `scope="class"` fixture + `_wait_for_ttl_expire()` caching avoids redundant setup and wait time. `xdist_group` ensures same worker.
4. **Well-documented**: Every test has target/method/expected docstring.

---

### Critical Issues

#### P0 - Test order dependency bug in `TestMilvusClientEntityTTLQuery`

`test_query_filter_expired_entity_data` asserts `count == 300` **before** calling `_wait_for_ttl_expire()`. However, pytest does not guarantee execution order within a class. If `test_query_count_expired_entity_data` (or `test_search_filter_expired_entity_data`) runs first and triggers `_wait_for_ttl_expire()`, the data will already be expired when `test_query_filter_expired_entity_data` runs, causing the `count == 300` assertion to fail.

```python
# In test_query_filter_expired_entity_data:
res = self.query(...)  # Expects count == 300 (before expiry)
assert res[0].get('count(*)') == 300  # ← FAILS if another test already triggered expire

self._wait_for_ttl_expire()  # Too late - other tests may have already called this
```

**Suggestion**: Move the "before expiry" assertion into the `prepare_data` fixture (which has guaranteed execution order), or split into two fixture phases (pre-expire / post-expire), or use `pytest-ordering` to enforce test exe

title: feat: impl StructArray -- support ARRAY_CONTAINS*
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/42148
design doc: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus-design-docs/blob/main/design_docs/20260306-struct.md
author: SpadeA-Tang

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces support for `ARRAY_CONTAINS`, `ARRAY_CONTAINS_ALL`, and `ARRAY_CONTAINS_ANY` operators on sub-fields of struct arrays. The changes are comprehensive, spanning the query parser, expression evaluation engine, and index querying logic.

Key changes include:
- Updating the parser to recognize the `struct_field[sub_field]` syntax for accessing struct sub-fields.
- Modifying `JsonContainsExpr` to handle indexed struct array fields, including performing element-to-row bitset conversion internally for nested indexes.
- Introducing a `ForEachRowElementRange` helper in `ArrayOffsets` to facilitate this conversion.
- Refactoring `Expr::ProcessIndexChunks` to allow index functions to return row-level bitsets directly, which is a nice improvement for flexibility.
- Adding a thorough GTest to validate the new functionality for both indexed and non-indexed scenarios.

The implementation looks solid. I have a couple of suggestions for improving code quality and robustness. Also, please remember to revert the changes in `internal/core/unittest/CMakeLists.txt` that disable other tests before merging.
