title: enhance:[hotfix-2.6.10] support configurable TLS minimum version for object storage connections (#48000)
body:
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48000
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44999

Currently Milvus doesn't allow users to control the TLS version used when connecting to object storage (MinIO/S3/Azure/GCP). Some environments require enforcing TLS 1.3 for compliance, but there's no way to set that today.

This adds a new config option `minio.ssl.tlsMinVersion` that lets users specify the minimum TLS version ("1.0", "1.1", "1.2", "1.3", or "default"). It works across all supported storage backends including MinIO/S3, Azure Blob, and GCP native. The setting is plumbed through paramtable, proto StorageConfig, and all the places that create storage clients (compaction, datacoord, datanode, storagev2, etc.).

For the GCP native backend, this also adds proper UseIAM/ADC support that was previously missing, since the TLS transport injection needed to handle both credential modes correctly.

Also fixed the GCP MinIO-compatible path to reuse any custom transport (e.g. with TLS config) as the backend for the OAuth2 token wrapping, instead of always creating a new default transport.

Unit tests cover the TLS version parsing, HTTP client construction, and version enforcement (proving a TLS 1.3 client correctly rejects a TLS 1.2-only server). Integration tests are included but gated behind environment variables.
author: jiaqizho

commits.message:
enhance:[hotfix-2.6.10] support configurable TLS minimum version for object storage connections (#48000)

Related to https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44999

Currently Milvus doesn't allow users to control the TLS version used
when connecting to object storage (MinIO/S3/Azure/GCP). Some
environments require enforcing TLS 1.3 for compliance, but there's no
way to set that today.

This adds a new config option `minio.ssl.tlsMinVersion` that lets users
specify the minimum TLS version ("1.0", "1.1", "1.2", "1.3", or
"default"). It works across all supported storage backends including
MinIO/S3, Azure Blob, and GCP native. The setting is plumbed through
paramtable, proto StorageConfig, and all the places that create storage
clients (compaction, datacoord, datanode, storagev2, etc.).

For the GCP native backend, this also adds proper UseIAM/ADC support
that was previously missing, since the TLS transport injection needed to
handle both credential modes correctly.

Also fixed the GCP MinIO-compatible path to reuse any custom transport
(e.g. with TLS config) as the backend for the OAuth2 token wrapping,
instead of always creating a new default transport.

Unit tests cover the TLS version parsing, HTTP client construction, and
version enforcement (proving a TLS 1.3 client correctly rejects a TLS
1.2-only server). Integration tests are included but gated behind
environment variables.

Signed-off-by: jiaqizho <jiaqi.zhou@zilliz.com>

title: fix: [2.6] handle non-nullable fields with defaultValue in insert and upsert
body:
pr: #48019

## Summary
- **Insert path**: `fillWithValue()` now initializes `ValidData` for non-nullable fields with `defaultValue`, not just nullable fields. Previously caused `FillWithDefaultValue` to crash with "the length of valid_data is wrong".
- **Upsert path**: `queryPreExecute()` now dispatches to `FillWithDefaultValue` when a field has `defaultValue`, instead of always calling `FillWithNullValue` which rejects `ValidData` on non-nullable fields.
- Unskips `TestDefaultValueDefault` and `TestDefaultValueQuery` e2e tests that were blocked by this bug.
- Adds unit tests for both fixes and e2e tests for partial update / upsert with default value fields.

issue: #48002

## Test plan
- [x] `Test_validateUtil_fillWithValue` — 84 subtests including 3 new default value subtests
- [x] `TestUpsertTask_queryPreExecute_DefaultValueWithValidData` — compressed ValidData expansion
- [x] `TestUpsertTask_queryPreExecute_DefaultValueError` — error path validation
- [x] `TestDefaultValueDefault` — e2e: insert with default column (unskipped)
- [x] `TestDefaultValueQuery` — e2e: query default value fields (unskipped)
- [x] `TestUpdateDefaultValueFieldBehavior` — e2e: partial update retains original values
- [x] `TestUpsertDefaultValueWithCompressedValidData` — e2e: upsert with compressed ValidData
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: handle non-nullable fields with defaultValue in insert and upsert

issue: #48002

Two code paths crash when a field has nullable=false and defaultValue set:

1. Insert path (fillWithValue): only initializes ValidData for nullable
   fields, causing FillWithDefaultValue to fail with "the length of
   valid_data is wrong" for non-nullable fields with defaultValue.

2. Upsert path (queryPreExecute): always calls FillWithNullValue which
   rejects ValidData on non-nullable fields. Should dispatch to
   FillWithDefaultValue when field has defaultValue.

Also unskips TestDefaultValueDefault and TestDefaultValueQuery e2e tests
that were blocked by this bug, and adds unit tests and e2e tests for
both fixes.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: fix TestDefaultValueDefault go-sdk test failures

- Add WithConsistencyLevel(entity.ClStrong) to ensure inserted data is
  immediately visible for queries
- Use different PK range for second insert to avoid PK overlap
- Fix first query to use int64 field (default=10000, no overlap with
  generated range 0..2999) instead of int8 (default=-1 overlaps due
  to int8 wrapping)
- Fix expected counts: int8 wrapping produces 16 extra matches (not 10),
  int32 has 1 match from first insert (i=2000)

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: keep same PK behavior in TestDefaultValueDefault

Remove PK offset for second insert - count(*) doesn't dedup across
segments so same PKs produce the same count results.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: fix: [2.6] add ValidData to auto-generated $meta field for partial update
body:
## Summary
- Cherry-pick of #47967 to 2.6 branch
- Fix partial update failure when `enable_dynamic_field=true` but upsert data contains only static fields (no dynamic fields)
- Root cause: PR #46419 changed `$meta` field schema to `Nullable: true` + `DefaultValue: "{}"`. After this change, `autoGenDynamicFieldData` generates `$meta` data without `ValidData`, so it cannot enter the `FillWithDefaultValue` branch in `queryPreExecute` (line 312-323 of `task_upsert.go`), leaving data in compressed format instead of the FULL format expected by the merge logic.
- Fix: add all-true `ValidData` to auto-generated `$meta` field data, consistent with its nullable+default-value schema definition

issue: #47957
pr: #47967

## Test plan
- [x] E2e test `TestPartialUpdateDynamicSchemaStaticOnly` included
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: add ValidData to auto-generated $meta field for partial update

issue: #47957

When enable_dynamic_field=true and partial_update=true, if user data
contains no dynamic fields, the auto-generated $meta field lacked
ValidData. During the column-based merge in queryPreExecute, the update
path added ValidData entries from existField, but the insert path
skipped ValidData appending because srcField had none. This caused a
valid_data length mismatch error (expected=total_rows, actual=update_rows).

Fix by initializing ValidData (all true) in autoGenDynamicFieldData,
since auto-generated "{}" values are valid non-null entries.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: add per-cluster TLS config for CDC outbound mTLS connections
body:
## Summary
- Add `BuildTLSConfig` helper and `TLSConfig` field to SDK `ClientConfig` for mTLS support
- Add `GetClusterTLSConfig(clusterID)` for dynamic per-cluster paramtable lookup via `tls.clusters.<clusterID>.{caPemPath,clientPemPath,clientKeyPath}`
- CDC `NewMilvusClient` reads per-cluster TLS config by target cluster ID, enabling different certs per target cluster

All target clusters' certs are pre-configured on every node, so CDC topology switchover (e.g., A→B,C to B→A,C) works without process restart.

## Test plan

title: fix: handle non-nullable fields with defaultValue in insert and upsert
body:
issue: #48002

## Summary
- **Insert path**: `fillWithValue()` now initializes `ValidData` for non-nullable fields with `defaultValue`, not just nullable fields. Previously caused `FillWithDefaultValue` to crash with "the length of valid_data is wrong".
- **Upsert path**: `queryPreExecute()` now dispatches to `FillWithDefaultValue` when a field has `defaultValue`, instead of always calling `FillWithNullValue` which rejects `ValidData` on non-nullable fields.
- Unskips `TestDefaultValueDefault` and `TestDefaultValueQuery` e2e tests that were blocked by this bug.
- Adds unit tests for both fixes and e2e tests for partial update / upsert with default value fields.

## Test plan
- [x] `Test_validateUtil_fillWithValue` — 84 subtests including 3 new default value subtests
- [x] `TestUpsertTask_queryPreExecute_DefaultValueWithValidData` — compressed ValidData expansion
- [x] `TestUpsertTask_queryPreExecute_DefaultValueError` — error path validation
- [ ] `TestDefaultValueDefault` — e2e: insert with default column (unskipped)
- [ ] `TestDefaultValueQuery` — e2e: query default value fields (unskipped)
- [ ] `TestUpdateDefaultValueFieldBehavior` — e2e: partial update retains original values
- [ ] `TestUpsertDefaultValueWithCompressedValidData` — e2e: upsert with compressed ValidData
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: handle non-nullable fields with defaultValue in insert and upsert

issue: #48002

Two code paths crash when a field has nullable=false and defaultValue set:

1. Insert path (fillWithValue): only initializes ValidData for nullable
   fields, causing FillWithDefaultValue to fail with "the length of
   valid_data is wrong" for non-nullable fields with defaultValue.

2. Upsert path (queryPreExecute): always calls FillWithNullValue which
   rejects ValidData on non-nullable fields. Should dispatch to
   FillWithDefaultValue when field has defaultValue.

Also unskips TestDefaultValueDefault and TestDefaultValueQuery e2e tests
that were blocked by this bug, and adds unit tests and e2e tests for
both fixes.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: fix TestDefaultValueDefault go-sdk test failures

- Add WithConsistencyLevel(entity.ClStrong) to ensure inserted data is
  immediately visible for queries
- Use different PK range for second insert to avoid PK overlap
- Fix first query to use int64 field (default=10000, no overlap with
  generated range 0..2999) instead of int8 (default=-1 overlaps due
  to int8 wrapping)
- Fix expected counts: int8 wrapping produces 16 extra matches (not 10),
  int32 has 1 match from first insert (i=2000)

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: keep same PK behavior in TestDefaultValueDefault

Remove PK offset for second insert - count(*) doesn't dedup across
segments so same PKs produce the same count results.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: [2.6] optimize qn load speed
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47422
master pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/47423
author: sijie-ni-0214

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces several optimizations to improve the query node's loading speed. Key changes include making the metrics cleanup process asynchronous to prevent blocking during collection release, optimizing the cleanup function itself to reduce allocations and remove a redundant operation, and removing calls to `debug.FreeOSMemory()` to avoid latency spikes during segment loading. These changes are well-aligned with the goal of enhancing performance. I have one suggestion regarding the asynchronous cleanup, which is to consider a worker pool to manage concurrency and resource usage more effectively under high load.

commits.message:
optimize CleanupQueryNodeCollectionMetrics

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

enhance: remove manual GC calls during segment loading

Remove debug.FreeOSMemory() calls from segment loading path.
Let Go runtime handle memory management instead of forcing GC
after each segment load, which reduces loading overhead.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

- Add dist check in checkStale to prevent duplicate segment load tasks
- Reduce time window for segment task addition to prevent duplicates

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

enhance: remove hard-coded thread pool size limit of 16

Remove the hard-coded maximum thread pool size limit of 16 in ThreadPool,
allowing the pool size to be fully controlled by yaml configuration
(CPU_NUM * thread_core_coefficient).

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

fix: remove node entry from dist manager when node goes offline

Delete map entry when Update is called with empty segments/channels
to prevent memory leak from accumulating offline node entries.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

enhance: optimize ReplicaManager lock granularity for node recovery operations

Split four node-recovery-related methods into three-phase lock-splitting
to reduce lock contention during etcd IO:
  Phase 1 (RLock): compute operation plan from in-memory state
  Phase 2 (no lock): perform etcd persistence
  Phase 3 (WLock): apply changes to in-memory state

Affected methods:
- RecoverNodesInCollection
- RecoverSQNodesInCollection
- RemoveNode
- RemoveSQNode

This reduces read blocking time from ~100ms to ~1ms during node
recovery, allowing Checker to read Replica info while etcd persistence
is in progress.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

fix: update unit tests for thread pool size limit removal

Update test assertions to match the new behavior after removing
the hard-coded thread pool size limit of 16:
- ThreadPoolTest.ResizeAboveMaximum: no upper limit clamping
- TestComponentParam: default parallelism factor changed from 1 to 20

fix: set etcd data-dir to match volume mount path

Add --data-dir /etcd flag to etcd command in Apple Silicon
docker-compose config to ensure etcd data is persisted correctly
with the mounted volume.

title: fix: [2.6] resolve collection alias in RBAC and clean up grants on collecti…[
body:
cherry-pick from master: #47851

…on lifecycle (#47851)

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47850
1. Privilege interceptor: resolve collection alias to real collection name
   before RBAC permission check, so that operating via alias checks permission against the real collection, not the alias name.

2. MetaCache alias cache: add aliasInfo cache with positive/negative entries to avoid repeated DescribeAlias RPC calls. Cache is invalidated on alias removal, collection removal, and database removal.

3. Catalog grant cleanup: add DeleteGrantByCollectionName and MigrateGrantCollectionName to RootCoordCatalog interface and kv implementation. On collection drop, delete all associated grants; on collection rename, migrate grants to the new name.

4. Feature flag: add proxy.resolveAliasForPrivilege config to enable/disable alias resolution in the privilege interceptor.

---------

title: fix: [cp26]convert ForceMerge targetSize from MB to bytes for proper validation
body:
- Convert `targetSize` from MB to bytes before comparing with `configMaxSize` in `triggerOneCollection`
- Update error message to display values in MB for better user understanding
- Update `expectedTargetSize` in `ForceMergeSegmentView` to use converted bytes value
- Update test cases to use MB values for `targetSize` parameter
- Add new test case `TestTriggerOneCollection_TargetSizeTooSmall` for boundary validation

According to the ForceMerge design doc, `targetSize` is specified in MB (e.g., `target_size=2048` means 2GB). However, `configMaxSize` from `getExpectedSegmentSize()` returns bytes. The direct comparison caused valid requests like `compact(target_size=32)` to fail when `configMaxSize` was 16MB (16777216 bytes).

- [x] Verify existing unit tests pass with updated `targetSize` values
- [x] Verify new boundary test `TestTriggerOneCollection_TargetSizeTooSmall` passes
- [x] Integration test: Deploy Milvus with `dataCoord.segment.maxSize: 16`, call `compact(target_size=32)` should succeed

title: fix: prevent TOCTOU race and deadlock in snapshot restore broadcast
body:
issue: #44358

## Summary
- Move resource validation inside broadcast lock to prevent TOCTOU race condition where resources could be modified between validation and message broadcast
- Release broadcast lock explicitly before rollback to avoid deadlock (rollback's DropCollection requires the same WAL broadcast lock)
- Add nil-guard on defer Close to prevent double-unlock panic when broadcaster is explicitly closed before defer runs
- Consolidate `validateRestoreSnapshotResources` to validate snapshot, collection, partitions, and indexes in a single function
- Remove deprecated `startBroadcastRestoreSnapshot` function

issue: #47845

## Test plan
- [x] Added 9 unit tests for `validateRestoreSnapshotResources` covering all validation branches (snapshot/collection/partition/index not found, success, edge cases)
- [x] Added 3 unit tests for `RestoreSnapshot` verifying broadcaster lock management (Close-before-rollback ordering, rollback failure path, success path)
- [x] `make test-datacoord` passes with zero failures
- [x] `make lint-fix` passes
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: prevent TOCTOU race and deadlock in snapshot restore broadcast

issue: #47854

Move resource validation inside broadcast lock to prevent concurrent
modifications between validation and message broadcast. Release the
broadcast lock explicitly before rollback to avoid deadlock, since
rollback's DropCollection requires the same WAL broadcast lock. Add
nil-guard on defer Close to prevent double-unlock panic.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: [loon] pass projection (needed_columns) to chunk reader for sealed segment
body:
Related to #44956
Previous PR: #47750

Pass milvus_field_ids as needed_columns projection to get_chunk_reader in ChunkedSegmentSealedImpl::LoadColumnGroup, so the storage layer can skip reading columns that already have index raw data. This reduces I/O when loading column groups where only a subset of fields need field data.

Also bumps milvus-storage to 2955b08 which supports projection push-down in chunk reader, and restores the assertion in ManifestGroupTranslator that was previously a workaround for the missing projection support.

title: enhance: support configurable TLS minimum version for object storage connections
body:
Related to https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/44999

Currently Milvus doesn't allow users to control the TLS version used when connecting to object storage (MinIO/S3/Azure/GCP). Some environments require enforcing TLS 1.3 for compliance, but there's no way to set that today.

This adds a new config option `minio.ssl.tlsMinVersion` that lets users specify the minimum TLS version ("1.0", "1.1", "1.2", "1.3", or "default"). It works across all supported storage backends including MinIO/S3, Azure Blob, and GCP native. The setting is plumbed through paramtable, proto StorageConfig, and all the places that create storage clients (compaction, datacoord, datanode, storagev2, etc.).

For the GCP native backend, this also adds proper UseIAM/ADC support that was previously missing, since the TLS transport injection needed to handle both credential modes correctly.

Also fixed the GCP MinIO-compatible path to reuse any custom transport (e.g. with TLS config) as the backend for the OAuth2 token wrapping, instead of always creating a new default transport.

Unit tests cover the TLS version parsing, HTTP client construction, and version enforcement (proving a TLS 1.3 client correctly rejects a TLS 1.2-only server). Integration tests are included but gated behind environment variables.
author: jiaqizho

commits.message:
enhance: support configurable TLS minimum version for object storage connections

Currently Milvus doesn't allow users to control the TLS version used when connecting to object storage (MinIO/S3/Azure/GCP). Some environments require enforcing TLS 1.3 for compliance, but there's no way to set that today.

This adds a new config option `minio.ssl.tlsMinVersion` that lets users specify the minimum TLS version ("1.0", "1.1", "1.2", "1.3", or "default"). It works across all supported storage backends including MinIO/S3, Azure Blob, and GCP native. The setting is plumbed through paramtable, proto StorageConfig, and all the places that create storage clients (compaction, datacoord, datanode, storagev2, etc.).

For the GCP native backend, this also adds proper UseIAM/ADC support that was previously missing, since the TLS transport injection needed to handle both credential modes correctly.

Also fixed the GCP MinIO-compatible path to reuse any custom transport (e.g. with TLS config) as the backend for the OAuth2 token wrapping, instead of always creating a new default transport.

Unit tests cover the TLS version parsing, HTTP client construction, and version enforcement (proving a TLS 1.3 client correctly rejects a TLS 1.2-only server). Integration tests are included but gated behind environment variables.

Signed-off-by: jiaqizho <jiaqi.zhou@zilliz.com>

title: fix: [loon] read deltalogs from manifest delta_logs section instead of column_groups
body:
Related to #39173 #45279

NewDeltalogReaderFromManifest was using NewManifestReader which reads from column_groups (insert data), not from the delta_logs section. When pk type is VarChar, the type mismatch between insert data columns and deltalog schema causes sigpanic in sort compaction.

Fix by extracting delta log file paths from the manifest's delta_logs field via FFI and reading them with the existing V2 parquet deltalog reader. Also fix LoonManifest memory leak in NewFFIPackedReader.
author: congqixia

reviews.body:
I'm sorry that the bug is not covered by existing tests, here is the claude code analysis and I suggest that we add necessary tests in this PR:

--

  There are three layered reasons why this bug escaped unit tests:                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                       
  1. NewDeltalogReaderFromManifest has zero test coverage                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                       
  grep -rn "NewDeltalogReaderFromManifest" --include="*_test.go"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             

  No test file anywhere in the codebase calls this function. It was introduced as part of the V2/manifest deltalog reading path, but nobody wrote a unit test for it.

  2. readDeltalogsV2 is also untested — only V1 is tested

  The existing test TestComposeDeleteFromDeltalogs in common_test.go only tests the V1 path (binlog-based deltalogs). There is no "manifest" or "V2" reference anywhere in the test file. The V1/V2
  dispatch happens in readFromSegment at common.go:69:

  if segment.GetManifest() != "" {
      return readDeltalogsV2(...)  // never tested
  }
  return readDeltalogsV1(...)      // all tests go here

  No test ever constr

commits.message:
fix: read deltalogs from manifest delta_logs section instead of column_groups

Related to #39173 #45279

NewDeltalogReaderFromManifest was using NewManifestReader which reads
from column_groups (insert data), not from the delta_logs section. When
pk type is VarChar, the type mismatch between insert data columns and
deltalog schema causes sigpanic in sort compaction.

Fix by extracting delta log file paths from the manifest's delta_logs
field via FFI and reading them with the existing V2 parquet deltalog
reader. Also fix LoonManifest memory leak in NewFFIPackedReader.

title: test: Add null vector checkers to chaos test pipeline
body:
## Summary

Wire three null-vector checkers into the chaos test concurrent operation pipeline (`test_concurrent_operation.py`):

- **NullVectorSearchChecker** — detects NaN distances from null vector leaks in search index
- **NullVectorQueryChecker** — validates null/non-null ratio consistency in queries
- **AddVectorFieldChecker** — dynamically adds nullable FLOAT_VECTOR fields, creates index, inserts data, and verifies

These checkers are already implemented in `checker.py` but were not registered in `init_health_checkers()`. The default collection schema already has nullable vector fields (`float_vector` and `image_emb` both `nullable=True`), so the checkers use the shared collection name.

issue: #47867

### Changes

**Modified:**
- `tests/python_client/chaos/testcases/test_concurrent_operation.py`: Added imports and registered 3 checkers

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

AddVectorFieldChecker into the concurrent operation chaos test.

title: fix: Fix rate limit not work when set collection rate limit properties
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/46713
/kind bug

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
- Core invariant: a Limiter's hasUpdated flag controls whether its non-Infinite limit is exported to proxies via toRequestLimiter(); only limiters with hasUpdated==true and non-Inf rates produce rate updates delivered to proxies (pkg/util/ratelimitutil/limiter.go: HasUpdated / toRequestLimiter behavior unchanged).

- Exact bug and fix (issue #46713): collection-level limiters created from configured collection/partition/database properties were constructed with correct limits but left hasUpdated==false, so they were skipped by the existing !HasUpdated() check and never sent to proxies. Fix: add Limiter.SetHasUpdated(updated bool) and call a new updateLimiterHasUpdated helper immediately after creating limiter nodes during initialization/reset (internal/rootcoord/quota_center.go) to mark non-Inf newly-created limiters as updated so they are included in toRequestLimiter exports.

- Logic simplified / redundancy removed: initialization now explicitly sets limiter initialization state (hasUpdated) for newly-created non-Infinite limiters instead of relying on implicit later side-effects to toggle the flag; this removes the implicit gap between creation and the expectation that a configured limiter should be published.

- No data-loss or behavior regression: the change only mutates the in-memory hasUpdated flag for freshly created limiter instances (pkg/util/ratelimitutil/limiter.go: SetHasUpdated) and sets it in the limiter initialization path (internal/rootcoord/quota_center.go). It does not alter token accounting (advance, AllowN, Cancel), rate computation, SetLimit semantics, persistence, or proxy filtering logic—only ensures intended collection-level rates are delivered to proxies—so no persisted data or runtime rate behavior is removed or degraded.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: fix: fast-fail ServerIDMismatch for node connections and increase walBalancer operationTimeout
body:
For node connections (isNode=true), ServerIDMismatch now returns needRetry=false immediately instead of retrying 10 times with exponential backoff (~52.6s). Retrying is futile because the NodeID injected via the interceptor at connection time never changes during retry. Coord connections keep existing retry behavior.

Also increase streaming.walBalancer.operationTimeout default from 30s to 30m.

For node connections (isNode=true), ServerIDMismatch now returns needRetry=false immediately instead of retrying 10 times with exponential backoff (~52.6s). Retrying is futile because the NodeID injected via the interceptor at connection time never changes during retry. Coord connections keep existing retry behavior.

Also increase streaming.walBalancer.operationTimeout default from 30s to 30m.

issue: #46182

title: fix: compaction forward delete replay blocks tsafe advancement
body:
Problem:
During compaction, loadStreamDelete() holds deleteMut.RLock for 30-60+ seconds while replaying historical WAL deletes via bloom filter checking. This blocks ProcessDelete() (needs WLock), which in turn blocks UpdateTSafe() (called after ProcessDelete in DeleteNode.Operate()). Strong consistency queries timeout waiting for tsafe.

Blocking chain:
  loadStreamDelete holds deleteMut.RLock (30-60s)
    → ProcessDelete blocked on deleteMut.Lock
      → DeleteNode.Operate blocked
        → UpdateTSafe never called → tsafe frozen → query timeout

Solution:
Split loadStreamDelete into a 3-phase snapshot-and-release pattern to minimize lock hold time:

- Phase 1 (RLock, microseconds): snapshot delete buffer entries, copy the slice, release lock immediately.
- Phase 2 (no lock, seconds): perform expensive BF check and forward deletes from snapshot. WAL pipeline is fully unblocked.
- Phase 3 (RLock, milliseconds): catch up new entries added during Phase 2 using index-based slicing, flush the forwarder, then atomically add segment to distribution.

Additional changes:
- Extract processDeleteRecords helper for BF check + forward logic.
- Move loadBM25Stats before loadStreamDelete so stats are ready before segment becomes visible. Add rollback via UnregisterSealed on failure.
- Use one BufferForwarder per segment shared across Phase 2 and Phase 3, flushed once after all records are processed.

issue: #47882

title: fix: prevent streaming node crash-loop on WAL replay for dropped collections
body:
issue: #47802
pr: #47886

When WAL recovery replays messages for collections that have been dropped (due to non-atomic etcd persistence or Kafka offset compaction), orphaned GROWING segments can appear in the RecoverySnapshot, causing newSegmentAllocManagersFromRecovery to panic.

Three fixes ensure orphaned segments never reach the snapshot:

1. handleDropCollection/handleDropPartition: always flush segments first even when vchannel is already DROPPED or missing, making replay idempotent.
2. handleCreateSegment: skip segment creation when vchannel is dropped or non-existent.
3. getSnapshot(): filter out GROWING segments whose vchannel is not active or whose partition has been dropped.

title: fix: [2.6] fix non-atomic CreateCollection causes schema loss
body:
pr: #47900
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47898

## Summary

Cherry-pick of #47900 to 2.6 branch.

- **Root cause fix**: Reverse write order in `Catalog.CreateCollection` to save fields/partitions/functions first, then collection key last. The collection key is the "commit point" — if a crash happens before it's written, the DDL ack callback retries the full write.
- **Defense-in-depth**: Stats task and import task validate schema before proceeding.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bigsheeper

commits.message:
fix: fix non-atomic CreateCollection causes schema loss

During RootCoord failover, Catalog.CreateCollection was non-atomic with
collection key saved first. If the process crashed between the collection
key write and the fields write, the collection was persisted without
fields. On restart, AddCollection's idempotency check short-circuited
because the collection already existed in collID2Meta, so fields were
never saved. This caused "primary field is not found" errors downstream.

Fix by reversing the write order: save fields/partitions/functions first,
then collection key last. The collection key is the commit point — if a
crash happens before it's written, the DDL ack callback retries the full
write since the collection won't be in collID2Meta on reload.

Also add schema validation as defense-in-depth:
- Stats task validates schema before building request
- Import PreExecute validates schema before broadcasting

Issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47898

Signed-off-by: bigsheeper <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: fix TestProxy_Import using empty schema that fails new validation

Add a field to the mock CollectionSchema in TestProxy_Import/Import so it
passes the new empty-schema check added in task_import.go PreExecute.

Signed-off-by: bigsheeper <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: unify copy segment logic with manifest path support
body:
## Summary

issue: #44358

Refactors the copy segment pipeline for snapshot restore, consolidating the implementation with a unified file collection approach that eliminates code duplication between manifest mode and binlog mode.

Key changes:
- **Unified file collection**: Add `SegmentFiles` struct and `collectSegmentFiles` with manifest/pb automatic fallback
- **Manifest path support (StorageV3+)**: Resolve binlog paths from packed manifest instead of protobuf fields, with `storage_version` field added to `CopySegmentSource` proto
- **Fail-fast error handling**: `SyncCopySegmentTask` errors now call `markTaskAndJobFailed` instead of just logging warnings
- **Testability improvements**: Extract `copyFile`/`listAllFiles` helpers, refactor all tests from mockery to mockey

Architecture:
- InsertBinlogs: try manifest (S3 list) first, fallback to pb if empty
- Other 7 types: always from pb (not yet in manifest)
- Single unified flow: collect -> map -> copy -> build result

Additional fixes:
- Fix `BinlogTypeBM25` constant to match actual path format (`bm25_stats`)
- Improve error messages with segment ID and path context
- Reduce `CopySegmentAndIndexFiles` from 136 lines to 93 lines

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests pass (`go test -tags dynamic,test` for `importv2` package)
- [x] `make lint-fix` passes
- [x] Snapshot E2E tests pass (7/7):
  - TestCreateSnapshot
  - TestSnapshotRestoreWithMultiSegment
  - TestSnapshotRestoreWithMultiShardMultiPartition
  - TestSnapshotRestoreWithMultiFields
  - TestSnapshotRestoreEmptyCollection
  - TestSnapshotRestoreWithJSONStats
  - TestSnapshotRestoreAfterDropPartitionAndCollection

refactor: unify copy segment logic with manifest path support

This refactoring consolidates the copy segment implementation to use a

Key changes:

- Add helper functions for file extraction (extractFromPb, extractIndexFiles, etc.)
- Implement collectSegmentFiles with manifest/pb automatic fallback
- Implement generateMappingsFromFiles for unified path mapping

Architecture improvement:

- Other 7 types: always from pb (not yet in manifest)
- Single unified flow: collect -> map -> copy -> build result

Code quality:

title: enhance: use effective version in banner and metrics BuildInfo label
body:
## Summary
- Use `getEffectiveVersion()` instead of hardcoded `common.Version` for startup banner and `milvus_build_info` Prometheus metric
- Dev builds now correctly display `master-20260228-add6e4c6d4` instead of always showing `2.6.6`
- Release builds are unaffected (git tag matches semver, so `getEffectiveVersion()` returns the same value)

## Motivation

The startup banner (`Version:` line) and `milvus_build_info{version="..."}` metric currently always show the hardcoded semver from `common.Version` (e.g. `2.6.6`), regardless of whether the binary is a release build or a dev build. This makes it impossible to identify the actual build version from logs or monitoring dashboards.

`getEffectiveVersion()` already exists and is used by the `GetVersion` RPC and `Connect` RPC (via `MILVUS_GIT_BUILD_TAGS` env var, introduced in PR #47822). This PR makes the banner and metrics consistent with those APIs.

### What changes

| Component | Before | After (release) | After (dev) |
|-----------|--------|-----------------|-------------|
| Banner `Version:` | `2.6.6` | `2.6.6` | `master-20260228-add6e4c6d4` |
| Metric `milvus_build_info{version=}` | `2.6.6` | `2.6.6` | `master-20260228-add6e4c6d4` |
| Session etcd version | `2.6.6` | `2.6.6` (unchanged) | `2.6.6` (unchanged) |
| `GetVersion` RPC | `master-20260228-...` | `2.6.6` (unchanged) | `master-20260228-...` (unchanged) |

### What is NOT affected

- `common.Version` itself (still hardcoded semver, used for session compatibility)
- Session registration in etcd (still uses `common.Version` for cluster version negotiation)
- Coordinator version range checks (still uses `common.Version`)

## Test plan
- [x] `go vet ./cmd/milvus/...` passes
- [x] Verified `getEffectiveVersion()` fallback: when `MilvusVersion` is empty or "unknown", returns `common.Version.String()`
- [x] Verified no Grafana dashboard on internal 4am Grafana queries `milvus_build_info` — zero PromQL impact

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: yanliang567

commits.message:
enhance: use effective version in banner and metrics BuildInfo label

Previously, the startup banner and milvus_build_info Prometheus metric
always used the hardcoded semver from common.Version (e.g. "2.6.6"),
even for dev builds. This made it impossible to distinguish dev builds
from release builds in logs and monitoring.

Now both use getEffectiveVersion(), which returns the ldflags-injected
MilvusVersion (e.g. "master-20260228-add6e4c6d4" for dev builds) when
available, falling back to common.Version for release builds where the
git tag matches the semver.

This is consistent with the GetVersion RPC and Connect RPC which
already use getEffectiveVersion() via the MILVUS_GIT_BUILD_TAGS env var
(PR #47822). The internal session compatibility system is unaffected as
it reads common.Version directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: yanliang567 <82361606+yanliang567@users.noreply.github.com>

title: doc: add streaming knowledge base for AI-agent-assisted development
body:
issue: #33285

Add a structured knowledge base under docs/agent_guides/streaming-system/ that documents the Milvus WAL (Write-Ahead Log) subsystem for AI coding agents. The knowledge base uses progressive disclosure: a streaming-system.md overview links to per-component docs with architecture, invariants, data flow, and key package locations.

Components documented:
- Channel model (PChannel/VChannel/CChannel)
- Message model (lifecycle, properties, per-type semantics)
- StreamingCoord (Channel Management, Broadcaster)
- StreamingNode (TimeTick & Transaction, Lock, Shard Management)
- RecoveryStorage (checkpoint persistence, WAL-based state recovery)
- StreamingClient (Append/Read/Broadcast API, local bypass)
- Replication & CDC (star topology, role enforcement, checkpoint tracking)
- WAL Backend (Kafka/Pulsar/Woodpecker/RMQ)

title: fix: fix non-atomic CreateCollection causes schema loss
body:
## Summary

- **Root cause fix**: Reverse write order in `Catalog.CreateCollection` to save fields/partitions/functions first, then collection key last. The collection key is the "commit point" — if a crash happens before it's written, the DDL ack callback retries the full write since the collection won't be in `collID2Meta` on reload.
- **Defense-in-depth**: `AddCollection` detects and repairs collections with empty fields; stats task and import task validate schema before proceeding.

## Details

During RootCoord failover, `Catalog.CreateCollection` was non-atomic with collection key saved first. If the process crashed between the collection key write and the fields write, the collection was persisted without fields. On restart, `AddCollection`'s idempotency check short-circuited because the collection already existed in `collID2Meta`, so fields were never saved. This caused "primary field is not found" errors in stats/index/compaction/import tasks.

### Changes

1. `kv_catalog.go` — Save fields/partitions/functions before collection key
2. `meta_table.go` — Detect and repair collections with empty fields in `AddCollection`
3. `task_stats.go` — Validate schema before building stats request
4. `task_import.go` — Validate schema before broadcasting import

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47898

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bigsheeper

commits.message:
fix: fix non-atomic CreateCollection causes schema loss

During RootCoord failover, Catalog.CreateCollection was non-atomic with
collection key saved first. If the process crashed between the collection
key write and the fields write, the collection was persisted without
fields. On restart, AddCollection's idempotency check short-circuited
because the collection already existed in collID2Meta, so fields were
never saved. This caused "primary field is not found" errors downstream.

Fix by reversing the write order: save fields/partitions/functions first,
then collection key last. The collection key is the commit point — if a
crash happens before it's written, the DDL ack callback retries the full
write since the collection won't be in collID2Meta on reload.

Also add schema validation as defense-in-depth:
- Stats task validates schema before building request
- Import PreExecute validates schema before broadcasting

Issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47898

Signed-off-by: bigsheeper <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: enhance: add configurable skip list for replicate message types
body:
- Add `streaming.replication.skipMessageTypes` config parameter (comma-separated message type names, default `AlterResourceGroup,DropResourceGroup`)
- On the secondary side, `overwriteReplicateMessage()` checks incoming message type against skip set and returns `IgnoreOperation`, which `ReplicateStreamServer` already handles gracefully
- Configurable at runtime (refreshable)

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47776
author: bigsheeper

commits.message:
enhance: add configurable skip list for replicate message types

Add a streaming.replication.skipMessageTypes config parameter
(comma-separated message type names, default
AlterResourceGroup,DropResourceGroup) that allows the secondary
cluster's replicate stream server to ignore specific message types
during replication. When a message type matches the skip list,
overwriteReplicateMessage() returns IgnoreOperation, which
ReplicateStreamServer already handles gracefully.

Signed-off-by: bigsheeper <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address PR review comments for skip replicate message types

- Replace linear scan with map lookup: store skipMessageTypes as a field
  on replicateService, initialized once at construction from paramtable
  config, making shouldSkipReplicateMessageType an O(1) method.
- Fix SwapTempValue("") test bug: tests now pass the skip set directly
  as a struct field, eliminating fallback-to-default issue.
- Send ConfirmedTimeTick for ignored messages: call sendReplicateResult
  before returning nil on IsIgnoredOperation so the client checkpoint
  advances past skipped messages.
- Mark ReplicationSkipMessageTypes as refreshable:false to match the
  static initialization contract.

title: enhance: [ExtTable] add external field mapping support to FieldMeta for lazy load
body:
Related to #45881

Add external_field_mapping to FieldMeta to support fields stored in external storage (e.g., S3). Fields with non-empty external mapping are excluded from segment loading via ShouldLoadField(), enabling lazy load of external table fields.
author: congqixia

enhance: add external field mapping support to FieldMeta for lazy load

Related to #45881

Add external_field_mapping to FieldMeta to support fields stored in
external storage (e.g., S3). Fields with non-empty external mapping
are excluded from segment loading via ShouldLoadField(), enabling
lazy load of external table fields.

title: fix: resolve missing Paginator.h in macOS CI from stale aws-sdk-cpp install
body:
aws-sdk-cpp 1.11.750 shipped with a packaging bug: the core CMakeLists.txt lacked an install() rule for the pagination headers directory, so aws/core/utils/pagination/Paginator.h was never copied to the installation prefix. The STS module's STSPaginationBase.h (transitively included via STSClient.h) references this header, causing a fatal compilation error in milvus-storage on macOS.

Homebrew backported the upstream fix (aws/aws-sdk-cpp@175e803) with rebuild 1, but CI runners that already had the pre-fix bottle installed were unaffected by `brew install` (which is a no-op for already-installed packages).

- mac.yaml: after brew install, check for Paginator.h and reinstall aws-sdk-cpp if missing to pick up the patched bottle
- milvus-storage/CMakeLists.txt: fail early with a clear message if the header is absent, so local developers know to reinstall

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47460
author: congqixia

commits.message:
fix: resolve missing Paginator.h in macOS CI from stale aws-sdk-cpp install

aws-sdk-cpp 1.11.750 shipped with a packaging bug: the core
CMakeLists.txt lacked an install() rule for the pagination headers
directory, so aws/core/utils/pagination/Paginator.h was never copied
to the installation prefix. The STS module's STSPaginationBase.h
(transitively included via STSClient.h) references this header,
causing a fatal compilation error in milvus-storage on macOS.

Homebrew backported the upstream fix (aws/aws-sdk-cpp@175e803) with
rebuild 1, but CI runners that already had the pre-fix bottle
installed were unaffected by `brew install` (which is a no-op for
already-installed packages).

- mac.yaml: after brew install, check for Paginator.h and reinstall
  aws-sdk-cpp if missing to pick up the patched bottle
- milvus-storage/CMakeLists.txt: fail early with a clear message if
  the header is absent, so local developers know to reinstall

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47460

## Code Review

This pull request adds a new RESTful API endpoint for truncating a collection. The changes are straightforward, adding a new constant for the truncate action, registering the new route, and implementing the corresponding handler `truncateCollection`. The implementation follows the existing patterns in `handler_v2.go`.

My main feedback is regarding the unsafe type assertion pattern used in the new handler, which is also present in other handlers in the file. While the current structure of `wrapperPost` makes it unlikely to fail, it's a potential source of panics if the route registration is changed without updating the handler. I've added a specific comment on this.

Overall, the changes look good and are consistent with the existing codebase.

title: enhance: always reset autoBalanceTs after balance check to a…
body: issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47055
author: sijie-ni-0214

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request correctly addresses a potential busy-loop in the `BalanceChecker` by ensuring the `autoBalanceTs` is always updated after a normal balance check. This prevents the checker from running repeatedly when no balance tasks are generated, thus improving efficiency. The change is logical and well-implemented. I've added a comment suggesting a new unit test to cover this specific scenario and prevent future regressions.

commits.message:
fix: [QueryCoord] always reset autoBalanceTs after balance check to avoid excessive CPU usage

Previously, autoBalanceTs was only updated when balance tasks were
generated. This caused the balance checker to repeatedly attempt
balancing without respecting the configured interval when no tasks
were generated, leading to unnecessary CPU consumption.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: [2.6] optimize mixcoord cpu and mem usage by avoiding redundant calculation in balance checker
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47055
master pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/47177
author: sijie-ni-0214

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces an optimization to reduce CPU and memory usage in balance checkers by caching workload status. This is achieved by adding versioning to distribution managers and using a cached status in assignment policies (`RowCountBasedAssignPolicy` and `ScoreBasedAssignPolicy`), avoiding redundant calculations. The changes are well-implemented and include corresponding tests. Additionally, the `ExecutingTaskDelta` is optimized for faster lookups. My review includes suggestions to further improve concurrency in the new caching mechanism by using `RWMutex` to allow for non-blocking reads.

commits.message:
enhance: optimize mixcoord cpu and mem usage by avoiding redundant calculation in balance checker

1. Optimize ExecutingTaskDelta.Get() from O(Nodes×Collections) to O(1)
   by introducing nodeTotalDelta map for fast node-level aggregation
2. Add version-driven workload status caching in ScoreBasedAssignPolicy
   and RowCountBasedAssignPolicy to eliminate redundant GetByFilter calls

title: fix: text log loading failure during cluster upgrade
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/46678

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
- Core invariant: Text index log keys are canonicalized at KV (serialization) boundaries — etcd stores compressed filename-only entries, while in-memory and runtime consumers must receive full object-storage keys so Datanode/QueryNode can load text indexes directly.

- Logic removed/simplified: ad-hoc reconstruction of full text-log paths scattered across components (garbage_collector.getTextLogs, querynodev2.LoadTextIndex, compactor/index task code) was removed; consumers now use TextIndexStats.Files as-provided (full keys). Path compression/decompression was centralized into KV marshal/unmarshal utilities (metautil.ExtractTextLogFilenames in marshalSegmentInfo and metautil.BuildTextLogPaths in kv_catalog.listSegments), eliminating redundant, inconsistent prefix-rebuilding logic that broke during rolling upgrades.

- Why this does NOT cause data loss or regressions: before persist, marshalSegmentInfo compresses TextStatsLogs.Files to filenames (metautil.ExtractTextLogFilenames) so stored KV remains compact; on load, kv_catalog.listSegments calls metautil.BuildTextLogPaths to restore full paths and includes compatibility logic that leaves already-full keys unchanged. Thus every persisted filename is recoverable to a valid full key and consumers receive correct full paths (see marshalSegmentInfo → KV write path and kv_catalog.listSegments → reload path), preventing dropped or malformed keys.

- Bug fix (refs #46678): resolves text-log loading failures during cluster upgrades by centralizing path handling at KV encode/decode and removing per-component path reconstruction — the immediate fix is changing consumers to read TextIndexStats.Files directly and relying on marshal/unmarshal to perform compression/expansion, preventing mixed-format failures during rolling upgrades.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: sijie-ni-0214

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 0**
🧹 Nitpick comments (2)
pkg/util/metautil/binlog.go (1)
`131-140`: **Compatibility check may have a false positive edge case.** The check `strings.Contains(filename, common.TextIndexPath+pathSep)` (i.e., `"text_log/"`) could theoretically match a filename that legitimately contains "text_log/" as part of its name, though this is unlikely in practice. Consider using a more robust check like verifying the path starts with the expected root pattern.
🔎 Alternative approach (optional) ```diff - // Check if filename is already a full path (compatible with old version) - // If it contains the text_log path segment, treat it as a full path - if strings.Contains(filename, common.TextIndexPath+pathSep) { + // Check if filename is already a full path (compatible with old version) + // If it starts with root path or contains the full text_log path pattern, treat it as a full path + if strings.HasPrefix(filename, rootPath) || strings.Contains(filename, pathSep+common.TextIndexPath+pathSep) { ```
pkg/util/metautil/binlog_test.go (1)
`164-196`: **Good test coverage for both new and legacy path formats.** The test verifies: 1. Building paths from filenames (new version) 2. Preserving already-full paths (old version compatibility) Consider adding a test case for an empty `textStatsLogs` map to ensure no panics occur.

`117-149`: **Consider strengthening the compatibility check heuristic.** The compatibility check on line 140 uses `strings.Contains(filename, common.TextIndexPath+pathSep)` to distinguish between full paths and filenames. While this heuristic should work in practice for object storage paths, it could theoretically match a filename that legitimately contains "text_log/". A more robust approach would check if the filename starts with a root path or contains multiple path segments: ```go // More robust check: filename should start from root or contain parent dirs if strings.Contains(filename, pathSep) && strings.Contains(filename, common.TextIndexPath+pathSep) { fullPaths = append(fullPaths, filename) } else { fullPaths = append(fullPaths, path.Join(prefix, filename)) } ``` However, given the controlled naming in object storage and the upgrade scenario this addresses, the current implementation is acceptable.

title: fix: etcd RPC size limit exceeded when dropping collection
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/46410
master pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/46414
author: sijie-ni-0214

commits.message:
fix: etcd RPC size limit exceeded when dropping collection

When dropping a collection, a large number of segments need to be marked for deletion.
At this point, the size of the RPC request written to etcd may exceed the limit, resulting in a panic.

Store only filenames in TextIndexStats.Files instead of full paths
to reduce memory and etcd storage overhead. Full paths are
reconstructed during loading and GC using segment metadata.

Signed-off-by: sijie-ni-0214 <sijie.ni@zilliz.com>

title: fix: etcd RPC size limit exceeded when dropping collection
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/46410

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
- Core invariant: etcd metadata and in-memory Segment/TextIndex records must store only compact filenames for text-index files; full object keys are deterministically reconstructed at use-sites from a stable root + common.TextIndexPath + IDs via metautil.BuildTextLogPaths.

- Bug & fix (issue #46410): the etcd RPC size overflow was caused by persisting full upload keys in segment/TextIndex metadata. Fix: at upload/creation sites (internal/datanode/compactor/sort_compaction.go and internal/datanode/index/task_stats.go) store only filenames using metautil.ExtractTextLogFilenames; at consumption/use sites (internal/datacoord/garbage_collector.go, internal/querynodev2/segments/segment.go, and other GC/loader code) reconstruct full paths with metautil.BuildTextLogPaths before accessing object storage.

- Simplified/removed logic: removed the redundant practice of carrying full object keys through metadata and in-memory structures; callers now persist compact filenames and perform on-demand path reconstruction. This eliminates large payloads in etcd and reduces memory pressure while preserving the same runtime control flow and error handling.

- No data loss / no regression: filename extraction is a deterministic suffix operation (metautil.ExtractTextLogFilenames) and reloadFromKV performs backward compatibility (internal/datacoord/meta.go converts existing full-path entries to filenames before caching). All read paths reconstruct full paths at runtime (garbage_collector.getTextLogs, LocalSegment.LoadTextIndex, GC/loader), so no files are modified/deleted and access semantics remain identical.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: test: Add null vector coverage to chaos tests
body:
## Summary

Add nullable vector field validation to the chaos test framework via three independent checkers, following the existing one-operation-per-checker pattern.

**Related issue**: #47867

### Changes (v2, addressing review feedback)

**Removed** (per review):
- Reverted all modifications to existing `SearchChecker`, `QueryChecker`, `AddFieldChecker`
- Removed `TestNullVectorDataPersistence` from `test_data_persistence.py` — persistence verification is covered by checkers

**New independent checkers (`chaos/checker.py`)**:

| Checker | Op Enum | Responsibility | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|
| `NullVectorSearchChecker` | `null_vector_search` | Search on nullable vector fields, NaN distance detection with accumulation threshold (3 consecutive) | `WAIT_PER_OP / 10` |
| `NullVectorQueryChecker` | `null_vector_query` | Query nullable vector fields, validate null/non-null ratio, fail on all-null (data corruption) | `WAIT_PER_OP / 10` |
| `AddVectorFieldChecker` | `add_vector_field` | Add nullable FLOAT_VECTOR + HNSW index + insert + query verification, with vector field limit guard | `WAIT_PER_OP * 6` |

**Review feedback addressed**:
- Each checker has independent stats and frequency (no sampling bias)
- NaN detection uses accumulation threshold (`NAN_THRESHOLD=3`) to avoid false positives from transient errors or zero-norm vectors
- `AddVectorFieldChecker` checks vector field limit dynamically via `describe_collection` (no hardcoded `MAX_FIELD_NUM`)
- Query results are properly validated (no `result = True` hardcode)

**pytest.ini**: disable locust plugin (`-p no:locust`) to fix `--host` option conflict

### Test Plan

- [x] `checker.py` syntax verified
- [x] New checkers follow existing patterns (TensorSearchChecker, JsonQueryChecker, GeoQueryChecker)
- [x] No modifications to existing checkers

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: yanliang567

reviews.body:
## Review Summary

整体方向是对的——chaos 测试需要覆盖 nullable vector 场景。但实现方式上有几个架构层面的问题，建议调整。

---

### 1. `checker.py`: 不应修改现有 checker，应新建独立 checker

现有 checker 体系（38 个 checker）遵循**一类操作一个 checker** 的模式：`TensorSearchChecker`、`FullTextSearchChecker`、`JsonQueryChecker`、`GeoQueryChecker` 都是独立的新类，没有在 `SearchChecker` 或 `QueryChecker` 里塞逻辑。

当前 PR 在 `SearchChecker`、`QueryChecker`、`AddFieldChecker` 中嵌入 nullable vector 逻辑，用 `_count % 10` 做采样，导致：

- **失败不可见**: secondary validation 的失败不会反映在 `succ_rate` 统计中，`assert_statistic` 看不到
- **职责混淆**: 一个 checker 同时做"操作是否成功"和"结果是否语义正确"两件事
- **采样偏差**: 每 10 次做一次 nullable 校验，改变了原本 field 选择的随机分布

特别是 `AddFieldChecker`，它原本只负责 scalar 字段（`DataType.INT64`），PR 把 vector 字段逻辑塞进去后，`run_task` 变成根据计数器交替执行 scalar/vector，职责不清。而且 vector field 添加需要建索引，代价比 scalar 大得多，应该有独立的执行频率控制。

**建议**: 新建三个独立 checker：

| 新建 Checker | 职责 |
|---|---|
| `NullVectorSearchChecker` | 在 nullable vector 字段上 search，验证无 NaN distance |
| `NullVectorQueryChecker` | query nullable vector 字段，验证 null/non-null ratio |
| `AddVectorFieldChecker` | 动态添加 nullable vector 字段 + index + insert + query |

这样每个 checker 独立统计、独立配置频率，符合现有模式。

---

### 2. `test_data_persistence.py`: 不需要新增测试

`test_data_persistence.py` 使用的是老旧的 `TestcaseBase` ORM 风格 API，不在主要维护路径上。新增的 `TestNullVectorDataPersistence` 使用 `MilvusClient` API，两种风格混在一起很混乱。

更重要的是，这 258 行新增代码做的事情（建 collection → 插数据 → query 验证 null ratio → search 验证无 NaN）在 checker 里就该覆盖完了。checker 的 collection 本身就是持久化的，chaos 恢复后 checker 继续跑天然验证了数据持久性，不需要额外的 persistence test。

**建议**: 删除 `TestNullVectorDataPersistence`，持久化验证由 checker 覆盖。

---

### 3. 其他问题

- **`result = True` 硬编码** (`AddFieldChecker.add_field()` line 1266): query 结果没有检查，`result` 被无条件覆写为 `True`，新的 `add_nullable_vector_field` 继承了同样的缺陷
- **`MAX_FIELD_NUM=60` 硬编码**: 没有从 Milvus 配置读取实际限制值，chaos 环境配置不同时会出错
- **NaN 检测误判**: 零范数向量 + COSINE metric 也可能产生 NaN，chaos 条件下网络错误也可能导致瞬时 NaN，缺少累积判断阈值
- **`pytest.ini` 变更**: `-p no:locust` 全局禁用 locust 插件是 workaround，如果需要可以保留，但建议作为独立 PR 提交

## Review Summary

The direction is right — chaos tests need nullable vector coverage. But there are architectural issues with the implementation approach.

---

### 1. `checker.py`: Don't modify existing checkers — create new dedicated checkers

The existing checker system (38 checkers) follows a **one-operation-per-checker** pattern: `TensorSearchChecker`, `FullTextSearchChecker`, `JsonQueryChecker`, `GeoQueryChecker` are all independent classes — none of them inject logic into `SearchChecker` or `QueryChecker`.

This PR embeds nullable vector logic into `SearchChecker`, `QueryChecker`, and `AddFieldChecker` using `_count % 10` sampling, which causes:

- **Invisible failures**: secondary validation failures won't surface in `succ_rate` stats — `assert_statistic` can't see them
- **Mixed responsibilities**: one checker doing both "did the operation succeed?" and "is the result semantically correct?"
- **Sampling bias**: running nullable validation every 10th iteration changes the random field selection distribution

`AddFieldChecker` in particular was designed for scalar fields only (`DataType.INT64`). After this PR, `run_task` alternates between scalar and vector field additions via a counter — unclear responsibility. Vector field addition requires index creation, which is much heavier than scalar, and should have independent frequency control.

**Suggestion**: Create three independent checkers:

| New Checker | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `NullVectorSearchChecker` | Search on nullable vector fields, validate no NaN distances |
| `NullVectorQueryChecker` | Query nullable vector fields, validate null/non-null ratio |
| `AddVectorFieldChecker` | Dynamically add nullable vector fields + index + insert + query |

Each checker gets independent stats, independent frequency config, consistent with the existing pattern.

---

### 2. `test_data_persistence.py`: No new tests needed

`test_data_persistence.py` uses the legacy `TestcaseBas

commits.message:
test: Add null vector coverage to chaos tests

Add three independent checkers for nullable vector field validation
during chaos testing, following the one-operation-per-checker pattern:

New checkers (checker.py):
- NullVectorSearchChecker: search on nullable vector fields, validate
  no NaN distances with accumulation threshold (3 consecutive NaN
  before failure) to avoid false positives from transient errors
- NullVectorQueryChecker: query nullable vector fields, validate
  null/non-null ratio, fail on all-null results (data corruption)
- AddVectorFieldChecker: dynamically add nullable FLOAT_VECTOR fields
  with HNSW index, insert data, query to verify; vector field limit
  guard via describe_collection

Op enum: add null_vector_search, null_vector_query, add_vector_field

pytest.ini: disable locust plugin (-p no:locust) to fix --host
option conflict with conftest.py

Signed-off-by: yanliang567 <82361606+yanliang567@users.noreply.github.com>

title: enhance: support different replica numbers on secondary CDC cluster
body:
## Summary
- Add `use_local_replica_config` flag to `AlterLoadConfigMessageHeader` so the secondary CDC cluster can use its own `ClusterLevelLoadReplicaNumber` / `ClusterLevelLoadResourceGroups` instead of blindly applying the primary's replica config
- Secondary's `ReplicateService` sets the flag on every replicated `AlterLoadConfigMessage`; `LoadCollectionJob` reads local config when flag is set
- Use `generateReplicas` for idempotent replica generation, ensuring WAL replay does not create duplicate replicas
- Default to 1 replica in `__default_resource_group` when local config is not explicitly set (instead of falling back to primary's config)

## Test plan
- [x] Unit test: replicated AlterLoadConfig gets `UseLocalReplicaConfig=true`
- [x] Unit test: local config overrides primary config when set
- [x] Unit test: defaults to 1 replica in `__default_resource_group` when local config not set
- [x] Unit test: flag=false uses primary config directly
- [x] Unit test: `getLocalReplicaConfig` first load, idempotent replay, not set, no RGs, alloc error

issue: #47779
author: bigsheeper

commits.message:
enhance: allow secondary cluster to use local replica config for replicated AlterLoadConfig

When a replicated AlterLoadConfigMessage is received on the secondary cluster,
use the local cluster-level replica config (ClusterLevelLoadReplicaNumber /
ClusterLevelLoadResourceGroups) instead of the primary's replica config.

- Add use_local_replica_config field to AlterLoadConfigMessageHeader proto
- Set the flag in ReplicateService.overwriteReplicateMessage for AlterLoadConfig
- In LoadCollectionJob.Execute, read local config when the flag is set
- Use generateReplicas for idempotent replica generation on WAL replay
- Default to 1 replica in __default_resource_group when local config not set

Signed-off-by: bigsheeper <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>

title: fix: use per-command safe.directory for git in Makefile
body:
## Summary
- Fix `GetVersion` returning `unknown-YYYYMMDD-` instead of `master-YYYYMMDD-<commit>` when building inside containers (e.g. via `builder.sh`)
- Replace `git config --global --add safe.directory '*'` with a per-command `-c safe.directory='*'` approach that doesn't pollute `~/.gitconfig`
- Remove stale `safe.directory` setup from `print-build-info` and `print-gpu-build-info` targets

## Root Cause
PR #47822 introduced `MILVUS_VERSION` using `:=` (immediate evaluation). The `safe.directory` config was set in `print-build-info` target (line 253), but `MILVUS_VERSION` on line 250 is evaluated first during Makefile parsing. So all git commands (`GIT_BRANCH`, `GIT_COMMIT`) fail silently inside the builder container where `.git` is owned by a different user.

## Fix
Define `GIT := git -c safe.directory='*'` and use `$(GIT)` for all git shell calls. This:
1. Ensures `safe.directory` is effective for every git command at parse time
2. Never modifies `~/.gitconfig` (no `--global --add` accumulation)
3. Scopes the security bypass to this Makefile only, not all repos on the machine

issue: #47822

## Test plan
- [ ] Build milvus inside builder container via `builder.sh`, verify `GetVersion` returns `master-YYYYMMDD-<commit>`
- [ ] Verify `~/.gitconfig` is not modified after `make build-go`
- [ ] Run `make print-build-info` and confirm correct output

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: yanliang567

commits.message:
fix: use per-command safe.directory for git in Makefile

The previous approach used `git config --global --add safe.directory '*'`
inside print-build-info targets, which:
1. Was evaluated too late - MILVUS_VERSION uses := (immediate evaluation)
   so GIT_BRANCH and GIT_COMMIT were already empty by the time
   safe.directory was configured
2. Polluted ~/.gitconfig with duplicate entries on every `make` invocation
3. Permanently disabled git's ownership check for all repositories

This fix introduces a `GIT` variable with `-c safe.directory='*'` that
applies only to each git invocation without modifying any config files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: yanliang567 <82361606+yanliang567@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use GIT_CONFIG_COUNT env vars instead of git -c for safe.directory

git -c safe.directory='*' does not work in some git versions because
the ownership check happens before -c options are applied. The Tekton
CI build container confirmed this - 'fatal: detected dubious ownership'
still appeared with -c flag, causing Build Tag and Git Commit to be empty.

Use GIT_CONFIG_COUNT/GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0/GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0 environment
variables instead, which are processed before any repository access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: yanliang567 <82361606+yanliang567@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: move safe.directory config before all git calls in Makefile

Neither git -c safe.directory='*' nor GIT_CONFIG_COUNT env vars work
for the ownership check - git performs this check before processing
both -c options and config env vars.

The only reliable approach is git config --global, placed at the top
of the Makefile before any git $(shell) calls. Also remove the stale
safe.directory setup from print-build-info and print-gpu-build-info
targets where it was evaluated too late (after MILVUS_VERSION :=).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: yanliang567 <82361606+yanliang567@users.noreply.github.com>

title: fix: Use physical time for entity-level TTL filtering
body:
## Problem
In strong consistency mode, when query_timestamp is significantly older than current physical time (e.g., replaying old data with guaranteeTimestamp from the past), entity-level TTL filtering incorrectly used query_timestamp instead of current physical time. This caused:
- Valid data to be incorrectly filtered as "expired"
- Zero search/query results even when data should be visible

## Root Cause
The TTL filter expression (`ttl_field > physical_us OR ttl_field IS NULL`) was using physical time derived from query_timestamp, which could be hours or days old in strong consistency scenarios. Entity TTL should always use current physical time, not query timestamp.

## Solution
1. **C++ Core Changes**:
   - Add `physical_time_us_` parameter to QueryContext (defaults to query_timestamp)
   - Pass physical_time_us through Search/Retrieve API chain:
     * QueryContext constructor → Search/Retrieve C API → segment implementation
   - CreateTTLFieldFilterExpression now uses QueryContext::get_physical_time_us()
   - Add ExecuteQueryExpr overload with physical_time_us for testing

2. **Go Layer Changes**:
   - Extract physical time from guaranteeTimestamp in query tasks:
     * query_task.go: Search operations
     * query_stream_task.go: Streaming search operations
   - Use tsoutil.ParseHybridTs() to convert TSO timestamp to physical time
   - Pass physical_time_us to C++ Search/Retrieve calls via plan.go/segment.go

3. **Testing**:
   - EntityTTLTest.cpp: Core TTL filtering with physical_time_us
   - EntityTTLEdgeCaseTest.cpp: Edge cases (no TTL field, zero physical_time_us)
   - Tests verify QueryContext stores/returns physical_time_us correctly
   - Tests validate TTL filtering uses physical_time_us, not query_timestamp

## Behavior
- **Before**: TTL filter used query_timestamp (old in strong consistency)
- **After**: TTL filter uses current physical time (from guaranteeTimestamp)
- **Backward compatible**: When physical_time_us=0, falls back to query_timestamp

## Technical Details
- TSO timestamp conversion: `tsoutil.ParseHybridTs(timestamp)` → physical_ms
- Physical time in microseconds: `physical_us = physical_ms * 1000`
- TTL expression unchanged: `ttl_field > physical_us OR ttl_field IS NULL`

Fixes #47413
author: xiaocai2333

commits.message:
fix: Use physical time for entity-level TTL filtering

Signed-off-by: Cai Zhang <cai.zhang@zilliz.com>

title: fix: make snapshot creation idempotent to prevent callback retry loop [DataCoord]
body:
issue: #44358

## Summary

Move snapshot existence checks to occur after acquiring exclusive locks in CreateSnapshot and DropSnapshot. This ensures that when multiple concurrent requests attempt to create snapshots with the same name, only one succeeds and others immediately fail with "already exists" error, allowing DDL callbacks to handle the result properly without entering infinite retry loops.

## Related Issue

issue: #47101

## Changes

- **CreateSnapshot**: Move snapshot name existence check to after lock acquisition
- **DropSnapshot**: Move snapshot existence check to after lock acquisition
- **Test**: Fix concurrent snapshot creation test to verify "already exists" errors by directly calling client methods to bypass ResponseChecker framework masking

## Test Plan

- ✅ Unit test: `test_snapshot_concurrent_create_same_name` passes consistently
- ✅ Verified concurrent behavior: 1 success + 4 "already exists" errors
- ✅ No race conditions observed across multiple test runs
