title: enhance: replace rapidjson with simdjson-ondemand and abseil with std-lib in C++ source
body:
Related to #46199

## Summary
- Replace direct usage of `rapidjson` with `simdjson` ondemand in `Json.h` `ExtractSubJson()` function
- Replace `abseil` time library (`absl::Time`, `absl::CivilSecond`) with standard C `<ctime>` (`struct tm`, `gmtime_r`, `timegm`) in `TimestamptzArithCompareExpr.cpp`
- Remove `nlohmann/json.hpp` include from `Json.h`, reducing preprocessor cost across 265 translation units
- Conan packages for rapidjson/abseil remain in `conanfile.py` as transitive dependencies for folly, grpc, etc.

## Performance impact

| Change | Path type | Perf delta | Matters? |
|--------|-----------|------------|----------|
| `rapidjson` → `simdjson` ondemand (`ExtractSubJson`) | **Hot** — per-row, per-query for dynamic fields | **~2x faster** | **Yes — performance win** |
| `abseil time` → `gmtime_r`/`timegm` (TimestamptzArithCompare) | Warm — per-row, but rare query pattern (calendar interval arithmetic) | ~Neutral (<5%) | No |

### ExtractSubJson: rapidjson → simdjson ondemand

`ExtractSubJson()` is called from `SegmentGrowingImpl::bulk_subscript` and `ChunkedSegmentSealedImpl::BulkRawJsonAt` — per-row during query result assembly for collections with dynamic fields.

The old implementation parsed the full JSON with rapidjson, constructed a new Document with selected keys, and re-serialized via `Writer::Accept()`. The new implementation uses simdjson ondemand to iterate fields once, matching keys via `unescaped_key()` and extracting raw JSON text via `raw_json()` — no re-parsing, no re-serialization.

Benchmark (C++, arm64, `-O2`):
```
Small JSON (41B, 3 keys → extract 2):
  rapidjson:         659 ns/op
  simdjson-ondemand: 323 ns/op  (2.0x faster)
  nlohmann:         1616 ns/op  (2.5x slower — rejected alternative)

Medium JSON (186B, nested objects):
  rapidjson:        1559 ns/op
  simdjson-ondemand: 486 ns/op  (3.2x faster)
```

### abseil → std::tm detail

Both `gmtime_r`/`timegm` and abseil's `ToCivilSecond`/`FromCivil` perform the same epoch↔calendar decomposition. `gmtime_r` is a single call into optimized glibc. Abseil adds timezone object dispatch overhead even for UTC. The difference is <5% and only applies to the uncommon `timestamptz + INTERVAL 'N month/year'` query pattern.

## Test plan
- [x] CI build passes
- [x] CI code-check passes
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] E2E tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: liliu-z

commits.message:
enhance: replace rapidjson/abseil with nlohmann-json/std-lib in C++ source

Replace direct usage of rapidjson with nlohmann/json (already a project
dependency) in Json.h ExtractSubJson function. Replace abseil time
library with standard C <ctime> in TimestamptzArithCompareExpr.cpp.

The Conan packages remain in conanfile.py as they are needed as
transitive dependencies for folly, grpc, and other packages.

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

enhance: use simdjson ondemand for ExtractSubJson instead of nlohmann

Replace nlohmann parse+construct+dump with simdjson ondemand iteration
and raw_json() extraction. This avoids re-parsing and re-serializing
JSON values entirely — raw text fragments are copied directly from the
source string.

Performance vs master (rapidjson): ~2x faster
Performance vs nlohmann (previous commit): ~6x faster

Uses escaped_key() for safe JSON output and unescaped_key() for correct
key matching. Removes nlohmann/json.hpp include from Json.h, eliminating
the preprocessor cost across 265 translation units.

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: floor division for negative timestamps and int overflow check

Address review feedback:
1. Fix pre-epoch timestamp handling: C++ integer division truncates
   toward zero (-1500000/1000000=-1), but time decomposition needs
   floor division (=-2 with 500000us remainder). Also preserve
   sub-second microseconds that were previously lost.
2. Add INT_MIN/INT_MAX bounds check before static_cast<int> on
   protobuf int64 interval fields to prevent silent truncation.

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: thread_local parser reuse and safe floor division

Address review feedback:
1. Make simdjson::ondemand::parser thread_local in ExtractSubJson()
   to avoid per-call allocation on a hot per-row path. Follows the
   existing pattern in Json::doc() (same file, line 141).
2. Replace (current_ts_us - 999999) / 1000000 with div-then-adjust
   pattern (div - (mod < 0 ? 1 : 0)) to avoid signed integer overflow
   UB near INT64_MIN.
3. Document key order behavior change: output follows source JSON field
   order instead of keys parameter order. This is safe because JSON
   objects are unordered (RFC 8259) and downstream consumers parse into
   Go maps.

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: int64 intermediate arithmetic, raw_json error check, reserve

Address review feedback:
1. Replace safe_int(operand) with safe_add(base, delta) that validates
   the RESULT of addition in int64_t before assigning back to int,
   preventing int overflow UB on tm fields.
2. Add timegm return value check (-1 = error). Note: -1 is technically
   valid (1969-12-31T23:59:59Z) but near-impossible via interval
   arithmetic. errno not used due to timegm's non-POSIX status.
3. Add .error() check on raw_json() before .value() in ExtractSubJson,
   matching the unescaped_key() error handling pattern above.
4. Add result.reserve(json.size()) to avoid reallocation in the hot
   per-row string building path.

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: gmtime_r null check, remove timegm assert, throw on field errors

Address review feedback (P0 + P1):

1. Check gmtime_r return value — nullptr means epoch_sec is out of
   range, leaving tm_buf uninitialized (UB). Now throws OpTypeInvalid.

2. Remove timegm != -1 assertion — epoch second -1 (1969-12-31T23:59:59Z)
   is a valid timestamp reachable via legal interval arithmetic
   (e.g., epoch 0 - 1 second). AssertInfo would crash the QueryNode
   on legal input.

3. Replace silent `continue` with ThrowInfo in ExtractSubJson error
   paths — silently dropping fields is worse than failing the query,
   since the caller expects all requested keys to be present.

4. Change safe_add from AssertInfo to ThrowInfo — interval values come
   from user queries and should not crash the process on overflow.

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

revert safe_add ThrowInfo back to AssertInfo

AssertInfo in Milvus throws SegcoreError exception (not process crash),
same as ThrowInfo. The previous change was based on incorrect review
feedback that assumed AssertInfo would crash the process.

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add get_object() error check and use linear scan for key lookup

- Check doc.get_object() result before iterating to handle non-object input
- Replace unordered_set with std::find linear scan — keys is typically 3-5
  elements, linear scan avoids heap allocation and is faster for small N
- Remove unused unordered_set include

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix overflow in interval multiplication and final_us computation

- Cast interval fields to int64_t before multiplying by op_sign to avoid
  int * int overflow UB (e.g. INT32_MIN * -1)
- Add range check on new_epoch_sec before multiplying by 1000000 to
  prevent int64_t overflow in final_us computation

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix: update IndexMeta in PutOrRef for existing collections
body:
issue: #47873 
relate: #45993 

#47829  changed Reopen to succeed instead of failing fast, which makes PutOrRef more likely to find existing collections whose IndexMeta was never updated, causing "field is not loaded" errors on new vector fields.

Add CCollection.UpdateIndexMeta() and call it in PutOrRef to keep IndexMeta in sync with schema updates.
author: marcelo-cjl

reviews.body: `SetIndexMeta` is not thread safe. Previously, it's only invoked during collection object creation procedure so it was safe. Now we need add mutex protect it preventing racing condition

commits.message:
fix: update IndexMeta in PutOrRef for existing collections

When a collection already exists in the collection manager, PutOrRef
only updated the schema but not the IndexMeta. This caused
"field is not loaded" errors when searching on newly added vector
fields, because CollectionIndexMeta::HasField could not find the
new field's metric type for search plan creation.

Add CCollection.UpdateIndexMeta() and call it in PutOrRef to keep
IndexMeta in sync with schema updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix: avoid current working directory in library search path
body:
On Linux, setenv.sh accidentally sets LD_LIBRARY_PATH and RPATH to look up shared objects in the current working directory first if nothing explicitly set an LD_LIBRARY_PATH already, because an empty path on either variable is interpreted as the current working directory. This results in races during parallel builds (e.g. tools invoked by Conan finding corrupt shared libraries, because said libraries are currently compiled and written out by another process in the build). Since the LD_LIBRARY_PATH is also used as the RPATH, the compiled Milvus binary also looks up shared objects in the current working directory, potentially picking up unintended versions of a shared object.

Fix this and similar "empty path" problems for other environment variables on Linux, macOS, and Windows by placing the path separator only if there are pre-existing paths.

issue: #47828
author: markusboehme

commits.message:
fix: avoid current working directory in library search path

On Linux, setenv.sh accidentally sets LD_LIBRARY_PATH and RPATH to look
up shared objects in the current working directory first if nothing
explicitly set an LD_LIBRARY_PATH already, because an empty path on
either variable is interpreted as the current working directory. This
results in races during parallel builds (e.g. tools invoked by Conan
finding corrupt shared libraries, because said libraries are currently
compiled and written out by another process in the build). Since the
LD_LIBRARY_PATH is also used as the RPATH, the compiled Milvus binary
also looks up shared objects in the current working directory,
potentially picking up unintended versions of a shared object.

Fix this and similar "empty path" problems for other environment
variables on Linux, macOS, and Windows by placing the path separator
only if there are pre-existing paths.

Signed-off-by: Markus Boehme <markus.boehme@chainguard.dev>

title: enhance: remove unused Go dependencies (ansi, fastjson, grpc/examples, sizedwaitgroup)
body:
Related to #46199

## Summary

Remove 5 unused or misused Go dependencies to reduce module bloat and consolidate overlapping libraries:

- **`mgutz/ansi`** → replaced with inline ANSI escape codes (only used for 3 color constants in migration console)
- **`valyala/fastjson`** → replaced with `tidwall/gjson` (only 1 file used fastjson; gjson is already used in 22+ files)
- **`google.golang.org/grpc/examples`** → replaced with existing `rootcoordpb` (test file pulled in entire grpc examples repo for a mock server)
- **`remeh/sizedwaitgroup`** → replaced with `chan` semaphore + `sync.WaitGroup` (only 2 files, trivial pattern)
- **`pkg/errors`** → replaced with `cockroachdb/errors` (the project standard; `pkg/errors` was used in 1 file)

## Behavior change: DeleteLog.Parse() fail-fast on missing fields

The `fastjson` → `gjson` migration adds explicit `Exists()` validation for `ts`, `pk`, and `pkType` fields in the JSON parsing branch. Previously, both fastjson and gjson would silently return zero values for missing fields, causing `dl.Pk` to remain nil and panicking downstream. The new code fails fast with a descriptive error at parse time. This is a defensive improvement (the original code had identical silent-failure behavior).

## Performance impact

| Change | Path type | Perf delta | Matters? |
|--------|-----------|------------|----------|
| `pkg/errors` → `cockroachdb/errors` | Cold (offline CLI tool `config-docs-generator`) | Negligible | No |
| `mgutz/ansi` → inline ANSI codes | Cold (offline CLI tool `migration/console`) | Marginally faster (eliminates map lookup) | No |
| `fastjson` → `gjson` (`DeleteLog.Parse`) | Warm — old-format deltalog deserialization only | **~2.5x slower** per JSON parse (143ns→361ns) | **No** — see below |
| `grpc/examples` → `rootcoordpb` | Test only (`client_test.go`) | None | No |
| `sizedwaitgroup` → chan+WaitGroup | Test only (`wal_test.go`, `test_framework.go`) | None | No |

### fastjson → gjson regression detail

`DeleteLog.Parse()` is called per-row during deltalog deserialization, but **only for the legacy single-field format**. The new multi-field parquet format (`newDeltalogMultiFieldReader`) reads pk/ts as separate Arrow columns and bypasses `Parse()` entirely. Legacy deltalogs are rewritten to parquet format during compaction, so this is a dying code path. Additionally, deltalog loading is I/O-bound — the JSON parse cost (~361ns/row) is negligible compared to disk read and Arrow deserialization overhead.

Benchmark (Go 1.24, arm64):
```
BenchmarkFastjsonSmall-4       8,315,624    143.1 ns/op    0 B/op   0 allocs/op
BenchmarkGjsonOptimized-4      3,321,613    361.4 ns/op   96 B/op   1 allocs/op
```

## Test plan

- [x] CI build passes
- [x] CI code-check passes
- [ ] CI ut-go passes
- [ ] CI e2e passes
- [x] Boundary test cases added (bare number, missing pkType/ts/pk)

1. Add Exists() checks for ts/pk fields in DeleteLog.Parse() before
   using gjson values — prevents silent zero-value on malformed JSON.
2. Change RetryServiceNameConfig from "rootcoordpb.GetComponentStates"
   to "milvus.proto.rootcoord.RootCoord" (correct gRPC service name,
   though the field is currently dead code).

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add pkType to Exists() validation and boundary test cases

Address review feedback:
1. Add pkType to the Exists() check alongside ts and pk. The JSON
   format has included pkType since its introduction (#34009), so
   missing pkType indicates corrupt data and should fail fast.
2. Add test cases: bare number input, missing pkType/ts/pk JSON.

Note: this is a defensive improvement, not a regression fix — the
original fastjson code had identical behavior (GetInt64 on missing
field returns 0, no switch case matches, dl.Pk stays nil).

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add default branch, type validation, and test cases for DeleteLog.Parse

Address review feedback:
1. Add default branch to switch dl.PkType — unknown pkType now returns
   an error instead of silently leaving dl.Pk=nil.
2. Add gjson.Type validation: Int64 pk must be gjson.Number, VarChar pk
   must be gjson.String. Prevents gjson's implicit type coercion from
   causing silent data corruption.
3. Add test cases: unknown pkType (999), Int64 pk with string value,
   VarChar pk with number value.

Note: the original fastjson code had identical silent-failure behavior
for all three cases. These are defensive improvements, not regression
fixes.

Signed-off-by: Li Liu <li.liu@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: enhance: make GetVersion return docker-image-tag style version string
body:

- Assemble `MilvusVersion` in Makefile via `git describe --exact-match --tags` (release) or `branch-date-commit` (dev), inject via ldflags

title: feat: add Storage V2 support to delta logs
body:
See: #39173

design doc: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus-design-docs/blob/main/design_docs/20260226-manifest-format.md

feat: add manifest-based deltalog storage
    
This change integrates deltalog storage into the segment manifest for V2/V3
storage format, replacing the separate FieldBinlog-based approach.
    
Key changes:
    
**New Infrastructure:**
- Add `transaction.go` with FFI wrapper for loon deltalog transaction APIs (loon_transaction_begin, loon_transaction_add_delta_log, loon_transaction_commit)
- Add `AddDeltaLogsToManifest()` to append deltalogs to existing manifests
    
**Compaction Layer:**
- L0 compactor now detects V1/V2 format from segment's manifest field
- L0 compaction results update manifest path instead of FieldBinlog for V2
- Added `ComposeDeleteFromManifest()` for reading deltalogs from manifests
    
**Writer Layer:**
- `BulkPackWriterV3.writeDelta()` writes deltalogs via manifest transaction
- Simplified `BulkPackWriter.writeDelta()` to use `storage.BuildDeleteRecord()`
- Removed unused `writeDeltaInternal` helper function
    
**Reader Layer:**
- `NewDeltalogReader()` supports both V1 (FieldBinlog) and V2 (packed) formats
- `NewDeltalogReaderFromManifest()` reads deltalogs from segment manifest
- Import binlog reader uses V1/V2 fallback for reading deltalogs
author: tedxu

reviews.body:
## Review: feat: add Storage V2 support to delta logs

Overall this is a well-structured refactor that cleanly separates V1/V2 storage logic. The composable primitives (`readFromReader`, `readFromSegment`, `readDeltalogsV1/V2`) and the `BuildDeleteRecord` utility are solid improvements that eliminate a lot of duplication.

However, there are **3 critical issues** that should be fixed before merge (detailed in inline comments):

1. **`defer record.Release()` inside a loop** in `splitAndWrite()` — memory leak
2. **`defer reader.Close()` inside a loop** in `loadDeltalogs()` — resource leak
3. **V1/V2 fallback can corrupt data** in import readers — `readInternal` appends to shared state before success is confirmed

Plus a few moderate/minor issues:
- Batch size calculation semantic change (`l0MemSize` vs `allDelta.Size()`) — intentional?
- `common.TimeStampField` used as column index (works by coincidence, semantically wrong)
- Unnecessary IIFE in `DefaultWriterOptions`
- Missing test coverage for download failure in the new `ComposeDeleteDataFromSegments` path (old `TestProcessLoadDeltaFail` was removed)

All 3 critical issues from the previous review have been addressed:

1. **`defer record.Release()` in loop** → Fixed: loop body wrapped in anonymous function (line 862-952), so `defer` scopes correctly per iteration.
2. **`defer reader.Close()` in loop** → Fixed: same anonymous function pattern in `loadDeltalogs` (line 2685-2730).
3. **V1/V2 fallback data corruption** → Fixed: both `l0_reader.go` and `reader.go` now use `tempData` buffers inside `readInternal`, only merging into the final result on success.

Minor issues also fixed:
- IIFE in `DefaultWriterOptions` removed — now just `version: StorageV1`
- `common.TimeStampField` no longer used as column index — all sites consistently use `rec.Column(1)`

One remaining note (non-blocking): the batch size calculation in `process()` now uses `l0MemSize` (metadata-reported size) instead of the old `allDelta.Size()` (actual deserialized size). This seems intentional since the delta data is now loaded inside `ComposeDeleteDataFromSegments` rather than being available before batching.

LGTM.

commits.message:
feat: add manifest-based deltalog storage

Signed-off-by: Ted Xu <ted.xu@zilliz.com>

title: fix: remove incorrect RootPath stripping from packed writer/reader
body:
Related to #48477

The Go-side NewPackedWriter and NewPackedReader stripped RootPath from file paths before passing to C++, based on an incorrect assumption that C++ would prepend RootPath internally. This in-place slice modification caused the caller's pathsMap to also lose RootPath, resulting in binlog LogPath being stored without RootPath in segment metadata.

This path mismatch caused downstream components that directly use LogPath (without going through DecompressBinLog) to fail to resolve the actual binlog file location.

Remove the stripping logic from both packed_writer.go and packed_reader.go to ensure binlog paths consistently include RootPath.
author: congqixia

commits.message:
fix: remove incorrect RootPath stripping from packed writer/reader

Related to #48477

The Go-side NewPackedWriter and NewPackedReader stripped RootPath from
file paths before passing to C++, based on an incorrect assumption that
C++ would prepend RootPath internally. This in-place slice modification
caused the caller's pathsMap to also lose RootPath, resulting in binlog
LogPath being stored without RootPath in segment metadata.

This path mismatch caused downstream components that directly use
LogPath (without going through DecompressBinLog) to fail to resolve
the actual binlog file location.

Remove the stripping logic from both packed_writer.go and
packed_reader.go to ensure binlog paths consistently include RootPath.

Signed-off-by: Congqi Xia <congqi.xia@zilliz.com>

title: enhance: optimize unfiltered search on sealed segments
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48279

Optimize the sealed-segment search path when no scalar filter is applied.

  **MvccNode**: On sealed segments without TTL, when `query_ts >= max_insert_ts`, skip `mask_with_timestamps` (redundant) and only apply `mask_with_delete` (no-op when no deletes). Derive `all_rows_visible` from `data.none()` on the actual bitmap. **VectorSearchNode**: When `all_rows_visible` is set, pass empty `BitsetView` to Knowhere (`IDSelectorAll`), skipping per-vector bit testing. Also adds `-mpopcnt` for x86_64 hardware popcnt, and bumps knowhere to [3b330dd0](https://github.com/zilliztech/knowhere/pull/1533) for a k=1 overflow fix exposed by the empty-BitsetView path.
author: CLiqing

commits.message:
enhance: optimize unfiltered search on sealed segments

Three independent optimizations for the sealed-segment search path:

1. Three-level MVCC fast path (MvccNode): skip bitmap ops when sealed
   + no deletes + query_ts >= max_insert_ts; apply only delete mask
   when deletes exist; default path for filtered/growing/TTL queries.
   Includes timestamp safety guard to ensure correctness under
   Bounded/Eventually consistency where query_ts may lag behind.

2. VectorSearchNode skip_filter: pass empty BitsetView to Knowhere
   (IDSelectorAll) when MvccNode signals no filtering needed.

3. Hardware -mpopcnt for x86_64: enable __builtin_popcountll hardware
   instruction instead of software fallback.

issue: #48279

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: CLiQing <2208529306@qq.com>

fix: remove thread_local cache in MvccNode Level 1 fast path

The Level 1 fast path used a thread_local cached RowVectorPtr to avoid
per-query allocation. However, downstream operators (ElementFilterBitsNode)
modify the bitmap in-place via doc_bitset.flip(), corrupting the cached
data. Subsequent queries on the same thread with the same active_count
reuse the polluted cache, causing ElementFilter tests to fail when run
sequentially.

Replace the thread_local cache with a fresh ColumnVector allocation
each time. The core optimization (skipping mask_with_timestamps and
mask_with_delete) is preserved. The allocation overhead is negligible
compared to the vector search cost.

issue: #48279

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: CLiQing <2208529306@qq.com>

fix: bump knowhere to 3b330dd0 for k=1 L2 overflow fix

Update knowhere version to include zilliztech/knowhere#1533 which
fixes exhaustive_L2sqr_nearest_imp initializing nearest_idx to 0
instead of -1. This caused BF_Overflow test failure when the
VectorSearchNode fast path passes empty BitsetView (IDSelectorAll)
to Knowhere.

issue: #48279

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: CLiQing <2208529306@qq.com>

refactor: simplify MvccNode and VectorSearchNode per review feedback

MvccNode: merge Level 1/2 into a single path. Always call
mask_with_delete (no-op when empty) and derive all_rows_visible
from data.none() instead of the racy get_deleted_count() check.

VectorSearchNode: eliminate duplicated search+metrics code between
fast path and normal path. Build the BitsetView conditionally, then
use a single search+metrics block.

issue: #48279

title: fix: partialResultRequiredDataRatio=0.0 should guarantee search never fails
body:
## Summary

- Fix strict greater-than comparison (`>`) to greater-than-or-equal (`>=`) in `NewRowCountBasedEvaluator` so that `partialResultRequiredDataRatio=0.0` correctly allows empty search results
- **Restrict partial result to Search only** — disable for Query and QueryStream to prevent silent data inconsistency in write paths (delete-by-expr, upsert)

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48628
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/48660

## Root Cause

### Issue 1: Comparison operator (#48628)

In `NewRowCountBasedEvaluator`, the comparison uses `>` (strict greater-than). When `ratio=0.0` and all QueryNodes are unavailable, `0.0 > 0.0 = false` rejects the partial result, causing DEADLINE_EXCEEDED. Fixed to `>=`.

### Issue 2: Query/QueryStream allow partial results (#48660)

`Query` and `QueryStream` are used by **write paths**:
- **`QueryStream`** → `delete-by-expr` (complex delete): queries rows matching a non-PK filter, then deletes them
- **`Query`** → `upsert`'s `retrieveByPKs`: queries existing rows by PK to determine insert vs update

When `partialResultRequiredDataRatio < 1.0` and a QueryNode is unavailable:
- **delete-by-expr**: returns partial PKs → only deletes rows on available QNs → silent data loss
- **upsert**: misses existing rows on unavailable QN → treats them as new → inserts duplicates

**Fix:** Only `Search` is allowed to use partial results. Both `Query` and `QueryStream` now enforce full segment coverage (`ratio=1.0`).

### Changes

| Location | Change |
|----------|--------|
| `NewRowCountBasedEvaluator` | `>` → `>=` for boundary correctness |
| `delegator.Query()` | Remove partial result logic, always `waitTSafe` + `PinReadableSegments(1.0)` |
| `delegator.QueryStream()` | Change taskType from `"Query"` to `"QueryStream"` |
| `executeSubTasks` | `"Query" \|\| "Search"` → `"Search"` only |
| `NewRowCountBasedEvaluator` | `"Query" \|\| "Search"` → `"Search"` only |

## Test plan

- [x] Unit test: `ratio=0.0` with all segments failed → Search returns partial (empty OK)
- [x] Unit test: `ratio=0.5` with exactly 50% data → Search returns partial (boundary OK)
- [x] Unit test: Query with `ratio=0.0` and partial data → does NOT return partial
- [x] Unit test: QueryStream with `ratio=0.0` and partial data → does NOT return partial
- [x] Unit test: Search with `ratio=0.0` and partial data → returns partial (control comparison)
- [ ] Integration: deploy with `ratio=0.5`, NetworkChaos 1 QN, verify delete-by-expr fails instead of silently partial-deleting

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
fix: use >= instead of > for partialResultRequiredDataRatio comparison

issue: #48628

When partialResultRequiredDataRatio=0.0, the evaluator should allow
search to return empty results (search never fails). However, the
strict greater-than comparison (0.0 > 0.0 = false) causes the
evaluator to reject the partial result, leading to DEADLINE_EXCEEDED.

Change the comparison from > to >= so that:
- ratio=0.0: accessedDataRatio=0.0 >= 0.0 = true (empty result OK)
- ratio=0.5: accessedDataRatio=0.5 >= 0.5 = true (exact boundary OK)

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: disable partial result for QueryStream to prevent silent partial deletion

issue: #48660

QueryStream is used by delete-by-expr to find rows matching a non-PK
filter. When a QueryNode is unavailable and partialResultRequiredDataRatio
< 1.0, the QueryStream incorrectly returned partial results, causing
silent data loss — rows on the unavailable QN were never deleted.

Change QueryStream's taskType from "Query" to "QueryStream" so that
executeSubTasks and NewRowCountBasedEvaluator treat it as a non-partial
operation (ratio forced to 1.0), ensuring delete-by-expr either gets
complete data or fails with an error.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

fix: restrict partial result to Search only, disable for Query/QueryStream

issue: #48660

Query and QueryStream are used by write paths (upsert's retrieveByPKs
and delete-by-expr respectively). Allowing partial results for these
operations causes silent data inconsistency — rows on unavailable
QueryNodes are silently skipped, leading to partial deletion or
duplicate data after upsert.

Change partial result policy so only Search allows partial results.
Query now always waits for tSafe and requires full segment coverage
(ratio=1.0). QueryStream already uses ratio=1.0 for PinReadableSegments
and now also uses a distinct taskType to ensure executeSubTasks and
NewRowCountBasedEvaluator both enforce full coverage.

Signed-off-by: Wei Liu <wei.liu@zilliz.com>

This PR fixes issue: When performing element_filter search across multiple segments, if one segment returns 0 hits, C++ reduce creates an empty `LongArray` for `ElementIndices` which proto3 serializes as absent (nil). The Go-side reduce then sees inconsistent `ElementIndices` (nil vs non-nil) across segment results and returns an "inconsistent element-level flag" error.

title: enhance: [ExternalTable Part7] support take() fast path for retrieve and search
body:
## Summary

- Add `Reader::take()` API for small result sets (<10K rows), bypassing full column materialization during retrieve and search — significantly reducing latency for point queries and low-topK searches on external collections
- Extract shared helpers (`ArrowToDataArray`, `BuildTakeContext`, `ExecuteTake`) to eliminate type-switch duplication between retrieve and search paths
- Move `NormalizeVectorArraysToFixedSizeBinary` to `storage/Util` for reuse across `ManifestGroupTranslator` and take() path
- Add eager refresh job state persistence to eliminate race window where `HasActiveJob` sees `InProgress` while all tasks completed
- Add 20 C++ unit tests covering all data types, error paths, virtual PK, and edge cases
- Add E2E benchmark framework and refresh test enhancements

Also includes [ExternalTable Part5] which enables loading and querying external collections (schema validation, segment loading, brute-force search, virtual PK support).

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/45881

## Test plan

- [ ] C++ unit tests: `test_external_take` (20 tests covering all data types, error paths, virtual PK, edge cases)
- [ ] E2E benchmark tests: `external_table_benchmark_test.go` (retrieve/search latency, scalability)
- [ ] E2E refresh tests: enhanced `external_table_refresh_test.go` (eager state persistence, concurrent refresh)
- [ ] Verify retrieve with small result sets uses take() fast path
- [ ] Verify search with low topK uses take() fast path
- [ ] Verify fallback to full materialization for large result sets
author: weiliu1031

commits.message:
enhance: [ExternalTable Part7] support take() fast path for all data types

Add a take()-based fast path for external table retrieve and search,
bypassing full segment materialization when result size ≤ 10K rows.

ArrowToDataArray converts Arrow arrays from reader_->take() directly
to proto DataArray for all Milvus data types:
- Scalars: Bool, Int8-64, Float, Double, VARCHAR/String/Text
- Complex scalars: JSON (String+Binary), Geometry (String+Binary),
  Timestamptz (Timestamp+Int64 with unit normalization), Array
  (Arrow List + protobuf Binary)
- Dense vectors: FloatVector (Reserve+bulk Add), BinaryVector,
  Float16Vector, BFloat16Vector, Int8Vector — all with dim assertions
  and List/FixedSizeList → FixedSizeBinary normalization
- VectorArray: List<FixedSizeBinary> with inner normalization and
  empty-row safety

Infrastructure:
- reader_mutex_ serializes concurrent take() calls (Reader not
  thread-safe)
- NormalizeVectorArraysToFixedSizeBinary centralized in ArrowToDataArray
- ArrowListToScalarFieldProto + ConvertToMicroseconds helpers in Util

normalize is correctly handled in ManifestGroupTranslator.

title: fix: restrict LoadBalance node validation to RW query nodes only
body:
LoadBalance uses Contains() which matches all node types (RW, RO, SQ). With streaming nodes in replicas, a non-existent query node ID could match a streaming node, bypassing the replica check and producing a misleading "segment not found" error instead of "node not found".

Fix server to use ContainRWNode() for both src and dst node validation, and fix E2E tests to derive invalid node IDs from all nodes (not just query nodes) to avoid collisions with streaming node IDs.

issue: #48674
author: chyezh

reviews.body:
LGTM, the fix correctly restricts LoadBalance node validation to RW nodes via ContainRWNode(), preventing false matches with streaming nodes. E2E test fix also looks correct.

— via SuperJames Agent

commits.message:
fix: restrict LoadBalance node validation to RW query nodes only (#48674)

LoadBalance uses Contains() which matches all node types (RW, RO, SQ).
With streaming nodes in replicas, a non-existent query node ID could
match a streaming node, bypassing the replica check and producing a
misleading "segment not found" error instead of "node not found".

Fix server to use ContainRWNode() for both src and dst node validation,
and fix E2E tests to derive invalid node IDs from all nodes (not just
query nodes) to avoid collisions with streaming node IDs.

issue: #48674

title: enhance: separate C++ executor pools for load/search and convert Load/Reopen to async futures
body:
Split the single global CPUThreadPoolExecutor into two isolated pools (MILVUS_SEARCH_ and MILVUS_LOAD_) so that segment load operations cannot starve search/retrieve and vice versa.

Convert SegmentLoad and ReopenSegment from synchronous CGO calls to the async future mechanism (cgo.Async + Future<R>) already used by AsyncSearch/AsyncRetrieve. This gives Load/Reopen proper context cancellation via folly::CancellationToken, replacing the manual CancellationGuard goroutine pattern.

Add OpContext parameter to Reopen(SegmentLoadInfo) so the cancellation token is propagated through the load path.

issue: #33132 #46358
author: congqixia

commits.message:
enhance: separate C++ executor pools for load/search and convert Load/Reopen to async futures

Split the single global CPUThreadPoolExecutor into two isolated pools
(MILVUS_SEARCH_ and MILVUS_LOAD_) so that segment load operations cannot
starve search/retrieve and vice versa.

Convert SegmentLoad and ReopenSegment from synchronous CGO calls to the
async future mechanism (cgo.Async + Future<R>) already used by
AsyncSearch/AsyncRetrieve. This gives Load/Reopen proper context
cancellation via folly::CancellationToken, replacing the manual
CancellationGuard goroutine pattern.

Add OpContext parameter to Reopen(SegmentLoadInfo) so the cancellation
token is propagated through the load path.

issue: #33132 #46358

Signed-off-by: Congqi Xia <congqi.xia@zilliz.com>

title: fix: handle nil VectorField.Data in MergeFieldData for nullable vectors
body:
## Summary

When a nullable vector field has all rows null, `VectorField.Data` is `nil` (protobuf oneof not set). `MergeFieldData`'s switch on `Vectors.Data` type hits the `default` branch, returning `"unsupported data type: FloatVector"`, which causes `embeddingNode` to panic.

This adds a `case nil` to skip vector data merge when the source batch has no vector data (all rows null).

## Root Cause

- Nullable FloatVector with all-null rows → `VectorField.Data = nil`
- `MergeFieldData` switch cannot match any concrete vector type → falls through to `default`
- `embeddingNode.Operate()` calls `panic(err)` on the returned error

## Fix

- Add `case nil:` in the Vectors type switch to skip merging when source data is nil
- Add unit test reproducing the exact scenario

Fixes #48520

## Test plan

- [x] Unit test `TestMergeFieldData/nullable_float_vector_-_all_null_src` passes
- [x] Local standalone: insert 2000 rows with all-null nullable FloatVector + BM25 function — no panic
- [x] Without fix: same script triggers `panic: unsupported data type: FloatVector`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: marcelo-cjl

commits.message:
fix: handle nil VectorField.Data in MergeFieldData for nullable vectors (#48520)

When a nullable vector field has all rows null, VectorField.Data is nil
(protobuf oneof not set). MergeFieldData's switch on Vectors.Data type
hits the default branch, returning "unsupported data type: FloatVector"
which causes embeddingNode to panic.

Add `case nil` to skip vector data merge when source has no data.

Signed-off-by: Shao Yang <yangshao@zilliz.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: marcelo-cjl <marcelo.chen@zilliz.com>

title: fix: use unique mmap file paths to prevent SIGBUS during column group replacement
body:
When a column group is replaced via ApplyLoadDiff, the new GroupChunkTranslator creates a MmapChunkTarget at the same file path as the old one. The FileWriter opens the file with O_TRUNC, truncating it to 0 bytes while the old translator's MAP_SHARED mmap is still active. Concurrent reads on pages not in the page cache then cause SIGBUS.

Fix by appending a monotonic generation counter to the mmap file path, ensuring old and new column groups never collide. The old file is cleaned up by ChunkMmapGuard destructor (munmap + unlink) when the last reference is released.

issue: #48658
author: chyezh

title: enhance: cancel async warmup before replacing or clearing indexes
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/47902

When indexes are replaced (e.g., during reload) or cleared (e.g., during segment release), any in-flight async warmup tasks on the old index must be cancelled first. Without this, warmup tasks could race with the index teardown, leading to wasted work.

This change:
- Adds cancel_warmup/cancel_and_erase/cancel_and_clear helpers for scalar, ngram, and json indexes
- Calls CancelWarmup() on old indexes before erase/clear in LoadScalarIndex, DropIndex, DropJSONIndex, and ClearData
- Deduplicates ngram and json index replacement logic
author: sparknack

commits.message:
enhance: cancel async warmup before replacing or clearing indexes

When indexes are replaced (e.g., during reload) or cleared (e.g., during
segment release), any in-flight async warmup tasks on the old index must
be cancelled first. Without this, warmup tasks could race with the index
teardown, leading to wasted work.

This change:
- Adds cancel_warmup/cancel_and_erase/cancel_and_clear helpers for
  scalar, ngram, and json indexes
- Calls CancelWarmup() on old indexes before erase/clear in
  LoadScalarIndex, DropIndex, DropJSONIndex, and ClearData
- Deduplicates ngram and json index replacement logic

Signed-off-by: Shawn Wang <shawn.wang@zilliz.com>

In sortSegment, when the BinlogRecordWriter (srw) is created but
subsequent operations fail (ComposeDeleteFromDeltalogs, record reader
creation, or Sort), the writer is not closed, leaking resources.

Fix by calling srw.Close() on each error path.

title: fix: generate proper empty aggregation result when sparse filter prunes all segments
body:
When sparse filter (bloom filter) prunes all segments on a QN worker, emptySegcoreResult was returning 0 fieldDatas for aggregation queries because OutputFieldsId is intentionally empty for aggregations. The Delegator's reduce_by_groups operator then failed validation expecting 1 fieldData (the count result) but receiving 0.

Fix emptySegcoreResult to use GroupAggReducer.EmptyAggResult() for aggregation queries, generating semantically correct results (e.g., count=0) instead of relying on FillRetrieveResultIfEmpty which cannot handle aggregation output fields.

issue: #48603
author: chyezh

commits.message:
fix: generate proper empty aggregation result when sparse filter prunes all segments (#48603)

When sparse filter (bloom filter) prunes all segments on a QN worker,
emptySegcoreResult was returning 0 fieldDatas for aggregation queries
because OutputFieldsId is intentionally empty for aggregations. The
Delegator's reduce_by_groups operator then failed validation expecting
1 fieldData (the count result) but receiving 0.

Fix emptySegcoreResult to use GroupAggReducer.EmptyAggResult() for
aggregation queries, generating semantically correct results (e.g.,
count=0) instead of relying on FillRetrieveResultIfEmpty which cannot
handle aggregation output fields.

issue: #48603

Cherry-pick of #48638 to hotfix-2.6.12.

Remove the SuffixSnapshot time-travel layer from RootCoord metadata storage, replacing with plain KV operations. Add background GC to clean up legacy orphan snapshot keys.

issue: #47993
pr: #48638

## Test plan
- [x] `internal/metastore/kv/rootcoord/` — all tests pass
- [x] `pkg/util/paramtable/` — TestServiceParam pass
- [x] `cmd/tools/config/` — TestYamlFile pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: MrPresent-Han

commits.message:
enhance: [2.6] remove SuffixSnapshot mechanism from RootCoord metadata storage (#47993)

SuffixSnapshot was a time-travel layer that wrote snapshot keys to etcd on every
metadata change. Investigation confirmed that:
1. No client/proxy ever sends non-zero timestamp for time-travel metadata queries
2. Tombstone sweeper calls RemoveCollection with ts=0, bypassing snapshot tombstone writes
3. Snapshot keys accumulate as orphans that the old GC couldn't fully clean

This commit removes the entire SuffixSnapshot mechanism:
- Replace SnapShotKV with plain TxnKV in Catalog (~30 call sites)
- Delete suffix_snapshot.go (~750 lines) and its tests (~917 lines)
- Remove SnapShotKV interface from pkg/kv/kv.go
- Remove snapshot GC config params (ttl, reserveTime)
- Keep IsTombstone/ComposeSnapshotKey in rootcoord_constant.go for migration tool compatibility

issue: #47993

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: MrPresent-Han <chun.han@gmail.com>

title: enhance: [2.6] Optimize bool IN/NOT IN expressions with nullable field handling
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/45525
pr: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/48459
author: zhengbuqian

commits.message:
fix: optimize bool IN expressions to use fast SIMD equality path

Bool IN queries (e.g., `field in [true]`) were using a slow scalar loop
with virtual dispatch and std::variant overhead, making them ~7x slower
than equivalent == expressions which use SIMD-vectorized comparison.

This adds an optimization in the expression rewriter:
- `bool_field in [true]` → `bool_field == true` (SIMD path)
- `bool_field in [false]` → `bool_field == false` (SIMD path)
- `bool_field in [true, false]` → AlwaysTrueExpr (non-nullable)
- `bool_field in [true, false]` → IS NOT NULL (nullable field)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: optimize bool NOT IN expressions in expression rewriter

Extend the bool IN optimization to handle NOT IN cases:
- `bool_field not in [true, false]` → AlwaysFalse (non-nullable) / IS NULL (nullable)
- `bool_field not in [true]` → != true
- `bool_field not in [false]` → != false

Also generalize visitUnaryExpr NOT simplification:
- NOT(AlwaysTrueExpr) → AlwaysFalseExpr
- NOT(IS NOT NULL) → IS NULL (and vice versa)
- NOT(col == val) → col != val

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: fix CI failures for bool expression optimization

1. Fix gci import ordering in rewriter/entry.go
2. Update partition key isolation test: NOT(col==val) is now rewritten
   to col!=val (NotEqual) by the expression rewriter
3. Skip Bool in materialized view multi-value IN tests: bool IN
   [false, true] covers the full domain and is correctly optimized
   to AlwaysTrueExpr (and NOT IN to AlwaysFalseExpr)

Signed-off-by: Buqian Zheng <zhengbuqian@gmail.com>

title: enhance: Optimize bool IN/NOT IN expressions with nullable field handling
body:
issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/45525

## Summary
This PR adds optimizations for boolean IN and NOT IN expressions, with special handling for nullable boolean fields. The changes improve query performance by rewriting certain bool IN expressions to simpler, faster operations.

## Key Changes

- **Bool IN expression optimizations:**
  - `BoolField IN [true, false]` → `AlwaysTrueExpr` (non-nullable) or `IS NOT NULL` (nullable)
  - `BoolField IN [true]` → `BoolField == true` (uses fast SIMD path)
  - `BoolField IN [false]` → `BoolField == false`
  - Single-value deduplication: `BoolField IN [true, true]` → `BoolField == true`

- **Bool NOT IN expression optimizations:**
  - `BoolField NOT IN [true, false]` → `AlwaysFalseExpr` (non-nullable) or `IS NULL` (nullable)
  - `BoolField NOT IN [true]` → `BoolField != true`
  - `BoolField NOT IN [false]` → `BoolField != false`

- **Unary expression optimizations:**
  - `NOT AlwaysTrueExpr` → `AlwaysFalseExpr`
  - `NOT (IS NOT NULL)` → `IS NULL` (and vice versa)
  - `NOT (col == val)` → `col != val`

- **Helper functions and test infrastructure:**
  - Added `newNullExpr()` utility to create NULL check expressions
  - Added `allBoolVals()` helper to validate boolean value lists
  - Added `buildSchemaHelperForRewriteNullableT()` for testing nullable fields
  - Comprehensive test coverage for all bool IN/NOT IN optimization paths

## Implementation Details

The optimization logic is implemented in the `visitTermExpr()` method, which:
1. Detects when a TermExpr operates on a boolean column
2. Analyzes the values to determine if both true and false are present
3. Rewrites to appropriate expressions based on nullability and value set
4. Handles single-value cases by converting to equality comparisons

The unary expression visitor was enhanced to handle NOT operations on the newly created expressions, enabling proper composition of optimizations (e.g., NOT IN becomes NOT of an optimized expression).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01GkYztNzb75zgjuevzqVcwb

- `bool_field in [true, false]` → IS NOT NULL (nullable field)

title: enhance: fallback file resource manager to close mode when no storage address configured in yaml
body:
## Summary
When DataNode is configured with `sync` mode for file resource manager but no storage address is set in yaml (i.e., remote storage type with empty address), the initialization previously attempted to create a ChunkManager and failed with an error, causing the node to fail to start. This change detects the missing address upfront and gracefully falls back to `close` mode instead.

title: test: fix nightly E2E test assertions for master #650/#651 failures
body:
## Summary

Fix 16 consistently failing test cases from nightly CI master build 650 (2026-03-26) and build 651 (2026-03-27). All failures reproduced across all 3 configs (kafka / pulsar-mmap / woodpecker).

- Nightly build 650: https://jenkins.milvus.io:18080/job/Milvus%20Nightly%20CI(new)/job/master/650/
- Nightly build 651: https://jenkins.milvus.io:18080/job/Milvus%20Nightly%20CI(new)/job/master/651/

## Changes

### 1. `param_check.py` — fix `output_field_value_check` for sparse and bfloat16 fields

**Fixes:**
- `test_search_by_pk_with_output_fields_all` — `KeyError: -1` (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_with_output_fields_all@TestMilvusClientSearchBasicV2` — `TypeError: ufunc 'equal' not supported` (3/3 configs)

**Root cause:** Sparse vector comparison used hardcoded `original[-1][_id]` instead of pk-based lookup. bfloat16/float16 vectors returned as `bytes` cannot be compared via numpy `ufunc 'equal'`.

**Fix:** Use pk-based index for sparse comparison; skip value check for `bytes` type (bfloat16/float16/binary).

### 2. `test_milvus_client_hybrid_search_v2.py` — fix expected output fields

**Fixes:**
- `test_hybrid_search_with_diff_output_fields` — `AssertionError` (3/3 configs)

**Root cause:** Test excluded ALL sparse fields from expected output, but only BM25-generated sparse fields cannot be output. User-inserted nullable sparse vectors CAN be output.

**Fix:** Only exclude BM25 sparse fields (`sparse_vector_field_name1/2`), keep `nullable_sparse_vec_field_name`.

### 3. `test_milvus_client_search_by_pk.py` / `test_milvus_client_search_v2_new.py` — exclude DISKANN from mmap test

**Fixes:**
- `test_search_by_pk_each_index_with_mmap_enabled_search[DISKANN]` — `alter_index_properties expect True, but got False` (3/3 configs)
- `test_each_index_with_mmap_enabled_search[DISKANN]` — same error (3/3 configs)

**Root cause:** DISKANN is a disk-based index and does not support mmap. `alter_index_properties` correctly rejects it.

**Fix:** Exclude DISKANN from the `dense_float_index_types` parametrize list in both files.

### 4. `test_milvus_client_search_group_by.py` — rewrite nonexistent field group_by tests

**Fixes:**
- `test_search_group_by_nonexistent_field_on_dynamic_enabled_collection[nonexist_field]` — `AssertionError` (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_group_by_nonexistent_field_on_dynamic_enabled_collection[21]` — `TypeError: object of type 'Error' has no len()` (3/3 configs)

**Root cause:** With dynamic field enabled, group_by on a nonexistent field name returns 1 result (all rows have null for that field → one group), not `limit` results. Passing `21` (int) as field name is an invalid type that the server rejects.

**Fix:** Split into 2 tests: `test_search_group_by_nonexistent_field_on_dynamic_enabled_collection` expects `limit=1`; new `test_search_group_by_invalid_field_type` expects `err_res` with code 65535.

### 5. `test_milvus_client_search_pagination.py` — fix search params for pagination topk

**Fixes:**
- `test_search_with_pagination_topk[100]` — `AssertionError` (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_with_pagination_topk[3000]` — `AssertionError` (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_with_pagination_topk[10000]` — `AssertionError` (3/3 configs)

**Root cause:** Missing `metric_type` and low `nprobe=10` caused poor recall at high offset, leading to empty or incorrect pagination results.

**Fix:** Add `metric_type: COSINE` and increase `nprobe` to 128.

### 6. `test_milvus_client_search_v2.py` — fix recall, exists expression, and metric type error

**Fixes:**
- `test_search_with_expression_auto_id` — `recall too low: got 313, expected >= 800` (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_with_expression_exists[json_field]` — `TypeError: object of type 'Error' has no len()` (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_with_expression_exists[float_array]` — same error (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_with_invalid_metric_type[JACCARD]` — `KeyError: 'err_msg'` (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_with_invalid_metric_type[HAMMING]` — `KeyError: 'err_msg'` (3/3 configs)
- `test_search_with_output_fields_all@TestSearchV2Shared` — `AssertionError` (3/3 configs)

**Fixes applied:**
- **recall**: Add `metric_type: COSINE` + `nprobe: 64` to search params
- **exists expression**: Remove `json_field` and `float_array` from parametrize (top-level field `exists` expression is not valid for these types)
- **invalid metric type**: Add `err_msg` assertion (was only checking `err_code`, `KeyError` when test accessed missing key)
- **output_fields_all**: Add `new_added_field` to expected output fields list

### 7. `test_milvus_client_sparse_search.py` — update error message for invalid metric type

**Fixes:**
- `test_sparse_search_invalid_metric_type[L2]` — `got 65535 ... expected 1100` (3/3 configs)
- `test_sparse_search_invalid_metric_type[COSINE]` — same error (3/3 configs)

**Root cause:** Server error message changed from `"only IP is supported"` to `"metric type not match: invalid parameter[expected=IP][actual=...]"`.

**Fix:** Update `err_msg` assertion to match new format.

## Test plan

- [x] `test_upsert_preserves_element_filter` — verified PASSED locally
- [x] `test_insert_without_connection` — verified PASSED locally
- [ ] Full nightly CI run to validate all 16 fixes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: NicoYuan1986

title: fix: [cp2.6] force promote RPC hangs forever due to missing doAckCallback invocation
body:
Cherry-pick from master

pr: #48535
issue: #47352

## Summary

Cherry-picked from master PR #48535 (open - preemptive backport)

**Note**: Original PR is still open. This CP PR should be updated if the original PR changes.

Fixes two bugs in the force promote path of `UpdateReplicateConfiguration`:

1. **Force promote RPC hangs forever**: goroutine in `triggerAckCallback` called `doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts` but never `doAckCallback`, so the broadcast task's `done` channel was never closed and `BlockUntilDone()` hung forever. Fixed by chaining `doAckCallback(task, g)` after `doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts`.

2. **Missing caller-supplied config validation**: `validateForcePromoteConfiguration` was never called in `handleForcePromote`. Fixed by calling it after broadcaster acquisition. Renamed instance method to `buildForcePromoteConfiguration` to avoid confusion.

## Verification

- [x] File count matches original PR (3 files)
- [x] Code changes verified (+75/-36)
- [x] No conflict markers
author: bigsheeper

reviews.body:
## Risk: Double invocation of `doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts`

In `ack_callback_scheduler.go`, `doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts` is called **twice concurrently** for every force promote task:

1. **Before FastLock** (line 137–141, added in `81eb2e29da`): a fire-and-forget goroutine launches `doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts` unconditionally.
2. **After FastLock** (line 150–158, this PR): another goroutine calls `doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts` followed by `doAckCallback`.

Both goroutines will:
- `BlockUntilAllAck` — concurrent waits on the same task (harmless)
- `fixIncompleteBroadcastsForForcePromote` — reads incomplete tasks, calls `MarkIgnoreAndSave`, and calls `AppendMessages` to write to WAL — **potentially double-appending messages**

Additionally, if FastLock fails and the task is re-queued, the fire-and-forget goroutine (call site 1) fires again on every retry, causing multiple goroutines to pile up.

**Suggestion**: Verify that `fixIncompleteBroadcastsForForcePromote` is idempotent and safe under concurrent execution. If not, consider removing the fire-and-forget call at line 137–141, since the post-FastLock goroutine (call site 2) now handles the complete flow (fix + ack callback).

commits.message:
fix: [cp2.6] force promote RPC hangs forever due to missing doAckCallback invocation

Cherry-pick from master PR #48535

Cherry-picks two fixes in the force promote path of UpdateReplicateConfiguration:

1. Force promote RPC hangs forever: goroutine in triggerAckCallback called
   doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts but never doAckCallback, so the
   broadcast task's done channel was never closed and BlockUntilDone() hung.
   Fixed by chaining doAckCallback(task, g) after doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts.

2. Missing caller-supplied config validation: validateForcePromoteConfiguration
   was never called in handleForcePromote. Fixed by calling it after broadcaster
   acquisition. Renamed instance method to buildForcePromoteConfiguration to
   avoid confusion with the static validation function.

pr: #48535
issue: #47352

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Yihao Dai <yihao.dai@zilliz.com>

title: fix: replace hard-coded -1 manifest version with named constants
body:
Replace all hard-coded `-1` literal used as manifest version with the named constant `packed.ManifestEarliest` (value `0`) for new segment creation, making the intent explicit and consistent across the codebase.

Key changes:

title: fix: force promote RPC hangs forever due to missing doAckCallback invocation
body:
## What does this PR do?

Fixes two bugs in the force promote path of `UpdateReplicateConfiguration`.

### Bug 1: Force promote RPC hangs forever

**Root Cause**

In `ack_callback_scheduler.go`, `triggerAckCallback` handles force promote messages by launching a goroutine to call `doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts`. The goroutine had its own `defer g.Unlock()` but never called `doAckCallback`.

`doAckCallback` is the only function that invokes `MarkAckCallbackDone()`, which closes the broadcast task's `done` channel. `broadcastScheduler.AddTask` calls `task.BlockUntilDone()` on this channel, so without `doAckCallback` being called, `BlockUntilDone()` blocks forever — leaving the force promote RPC permanently hung.

**Fix**

Chain `doAckCallback(task, g)` after `doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts(task)` in the same goroutine. `doAckCallback` already handles `g.Unlock()` via its internal defer, so the separate unlock defer in the goroutine is also removed.

```go
// Before (buggy):
go func() {
    defer func() {
        s.rkLockerMu.Lock()
        g.Unlock()
        s.rkLockerMu.Unlock()
    }()
    s.doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts(task)
}()

// After (fixed):
go func() {
    s.doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts(task)
    s.doAckCallback(task, g)  // closes done channel, unblocks BlockUntilDone()
}()
```

### Bug 2: Missing caller-supplied config validation

**Root Cause**

In `assignment.go`, `validateForcePromoteConfiguration(config, currentClusterID)` existed but was never called in `handleForcePromote`. The caller-supplied config was passed through without any validation.

**Fix**

Call `validateForcePromoteConfiguration(config, currentClusterID)` after broadcaster acquisition to validate the caller-supplied config (must contain exactly the current cluster with no topology) before proceeding with the promotion.

Also rename the instance method `validateForcePromoteConfiguration(ctx)` to `buildForcePromoteConfiguration(ctx)` to avoid confusion with the static validation function.

## Tests

Verified with E2E tests covering the full force promote lifecycle:
- Force promote on primary cluster rejected
- Force promote with invalid config rejected
- Force promote succeeds on secondary with data integrity (1000 rows replicated + 500 new rows post-promote)
- Force promote on already-promoted cluster rejected

issue: #47352
author: bigsheeper

commits.message:
fix: force promote RPC hangs and missing config validation

Two bugs in the force promote path:

1. ack_callback_scheduler.go: triggerAckCallback launched a goroutine to call
   doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts but never called doAckCallback afterwards.
   doAckCallback is the only function that invokes MarkAckCallbackDone(), which closes
   the broadcast task's done channel. Without it, BlockUntilDone() blocks forever,
   leaving the force promote RPC permanently hung.

   Fix: chain doAckCallback(task, g) after doForcePromoteFixIncompleteBroadcasts(task)
   in the same goroutine. Remove the separate defer g.Unlock() since doAckCallback
   already handles unlocking internally.

2. assignment.go: validateForcePromoteConfiguration(config, currentClusterID) existed
   but was never called in handleForcePromote. Add the call after broadcaster acquisition
   to validate the caller-supplied config before proceeding.

   Also rename the instance method to buildForcePromoteConfiguration to distinguish
   it from the static validation function.

title: enhance: unify stats file path model with caller-provided basePath
body:
Move stats file (text_index, json_key_stats) path computation from C++ to Go. Go computes basePath based on storage version (V2: traditional top-level directories, V3: segment basePath/_stats/) and passes it to C++ via proto. C++ uses the basePath directly with no V2/V3 branching.

Key changes:
- Add stats_base_path to BuildIndexInfo proto (write path)
- Add base_path to LoadTextIndexInfo/LoadJsonKeyIndexInfo proto (read path)
- Add base_path to segcorepb TextIndexStats/JsonKeyStats (SegmentLoadInfo path)
- TextMatchIndex::Upload() returns relative paths (consistent with JsonKeyStats)
- JsonKeyStats::Load()/TextMatchIndex::Load() require basePath (Assert-checked)
- Extract BuildTextIndexPrefix()/BuildJsonKeyStatsPrefix() to metautil to eliminate 5 duplicated format strings
- ConvertToSegcoreSegmentLoadInfo() resolves V3 manifest stats with basePaths

issue: #48547

C++ via proto. C++ uses the basePath directly with no V2/V3 branching.

Key changes:
- Add stats_base_path to BuildIndexInfo proto (write path)
- Add base_path to LoadTextIndexInfo/LoadJsonKeyIndexInfo proto (read path)

title: enhance: [2.6] support switching of WAL implementation
author: tinswzy

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a significant and valuable feature: the ability to switch WAL implementations at runtime. The implementation is comprehensive, touching many parts of the system from the RESTful API down to the WAL adapters and data flushing pipeline. The use of a two-stage commit protocol (broadcast, flush, ack, then update config) for the switch is a robust approach. The changes also include necessary refactoring in the WAL opener to support dynamic selection of WAL implementations. The addition of new API endpoints for altering WAL and immutable configs is well-designed. Overall, this is a high-quality contribution that enhances the flexibility and maintainability of Milvus.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a significant enhancement to support runtime switching of the WAL implementation. This is achieved through a new RESTful API endpoint `/management/wal/alter`. The changes are extensive and span across multiple components including the coordinator, datanode, and streamingnode.

Key changes include:
- A new dynamic WAL opener that can handle different WAL implementations at runtime.
- A two-stage process for WAL switching: first flushing all data, then updating checkpoints to the new WAL's initial position.
- A new RESTful API endpoint to trigger the WAL switch.
- A new RESTful API endpoint to alter other immutable configurations.
- Persistence of immutable configurations like `mq.type` to etcd at startup.
- Propagation of the `AlterWAL` signal through the data processing pipeline to ensure all nodes are aware of the switch.

The implementation appears robust, with good use of exclusive locks for broadcasting changes and a solid two-stage commit process for the WAL switch. The code is well-structured. I have one suggestion for improvement to make a check more precise.

commits.message:
enhance: support switching of WAL implementation (#45286)

issue: #44726

Introduce an immutable option to prevent accidental modification of
critical configurations.
Support switching of WAL implementation.

first.

title: fix: resolve race in adaptive rate limit controller notify ordering
body:
## Summary
- Fix flaky `TestAdaptiveRateLimitController_EnterRejectMode` in `pkg/streaming/util/ratelimit`
- Root cause: `notify()` stored mode atomically BEFORE notifying observers. Tests checking `getMode()` via `Eventually` could see the mode change and call `AssertExpectations` before the backgroundLoop goroutine finished the observer notification
- Fix: move `mode.Store()` after observer notification in `notify()`, ensuring observers are notified before the mode change is externally visible

## Test plan
- [x] Reproduced: 4/100 failures with `-race` on unmodified code
- [x] Verified: 0/100 failures with `-race` after fix
- [x] All `streaming/util/ratelimit` tests pass with `-race`

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/39893

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: liliu-z

commits.message:
fix: resolve race in adaptive rate limit controller notify ordering

notify() stored mode atomically BEFORE notifying observers. Tests
checking getMode() via Eventually could observe the mode change and
call AssertExpectations before the backgroundLoop goroutine finished
the observer notification, causing flaky mock expectation failures.

Move mode.Store() after observer notification so that by the time
external callers see the new mode, observers have been notified.

Reproduced: 4/100 failures with -race on unmodified code.
Verified: 0/100 failures with -race after fix.

title: fix: [cp26]add generic LoadPlugin[T] to serialize plugin.Open() calls (#48420)
body:
Replace three independent plugin.Open() + Lookup() + type-assert sequences with a single centralized LoadPlugin[T](path, symbol) generic function that holds a mutex around plugin.Open(). This prevents the concurrent plugin.Open() panic ("plugin has empty pluginpath") and eliminates the footgun of forgetting to acquire the mutex at new call sites.

Migrated call sites:
- hookutil.initHook() (MilvusHook + MilvusExtension)
- hookutil.initCipher() (CipherPlugin) — the unprotected site
- QueryNode.initHook() (QueryNodePlugin)

The old LockHookInit/UnlockHookInit API is removed — the mutex is now an internal detail of LoadPlugin.

See also: #48335
pr: #48420
author: XuanYang-cn

commits.message:
fix: add generic LoadPlugin[T] to serialize plugin.Open() calls (#48420)

Replace three independent plugin.Open() + Lookup() + type-assert
sequences with a single centralized LoadPlugin[T](path, symbol) generic
function that holds a mutex around plugin.Open(). This prevents the
concurrent plugin.Open() panic ("plugin has empty pluginpath") and
eliminates the footgun of forgetting to acquire the mutex at new call
sites.

Migrated call sites:
- hookutil.initHook() (MilvusHook + MilvusExtension)
- hookutil.initCipher() (CipherPlugin) — the unprotected site
- QueryNode.initHook() (QueryNodePlugin)

The old LockHookInit/UnlockHookInit API is removed — the mutex is now an
internal detail of LoadPlugin.

See also: #48335
pr: #48420

title: enhance: [2.6] type-aware bidirectional in/== or expression rewriting
author: zhengbuqian

commits.message:
feat(parser): type-aware bidirectional in/== or expression rewriting with PK optimization

Introduce smart expression rewriting that chooses between TermExpr (IN)
and UnaryRangeExpr (== or) based on data type characteristics:
- Integer types: use IN when count >= 10
- Floating types: use IN when count >= 15
- String/other types: use IN when count >= 3
- Primary key fields: always use IN (PK is sorted, IN uses efficient
  two-pointer binary search, 60-117x faster than == or for 100 values)

The rewriting is bidirectional:
- combineOrEqualsToIn: merges == or chains into IN above threshold
- visitTermExpr: splits IN into == or below threshold
- Both directions respect the same type-aware thresholds and PK override

Signed-off-by: Buqian Zheng <zhengbuqian@gmail.com>

title: enhance: type-aware bidirectional in/== or expression rewriting
body:
Replace the fixed threshold (150) for merging == or into in[] with type-specific thresholds based on benchmark data:
- INT types: use in[] when N >= 10 (== or is faster below due to simpler execution path)
- FLOAT types: use in[] when N >= 15 (float hash is more expensive)
- Other types (varchar, bool): use in[] when N >= 3

The rewriting is now bidirectional:
- == or → in[]: when shouldUseInExpr returns true (existing direction)
- in[] → == or: when shouldUseInExpr returns false (new direction, via visitTermExpr)
- not in → != and: when shouldUseInExpr returns false (new, via visitUnaryExpr)

Both directions use the same shouldUseInExpr function to ensure consistency.

issue: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/issues/45525
author: zhengbuqian

commits.message:
feat(parser): type-aware bidirectional in/== or expression rewriting with PK optimization

Introduce smart expression rewriting that chooses between TermExpr (IN)
and UnaryRangeExpr (== or) based on data type characteristics:
- Integer types: use IN when count >= 10
- Floating types: use IN when count >= 15
- String/other types: use IN when count >= 3
- Primary key fields: always use IN (PK is sorted, IN uses efficient
  two-pointer binary search, 60-117x faster than == or for 100 values)

The rewriting is bidirectional:
- combineOrEqualsToIn: merges == or chains into IN above threshold
- visitTermExpr: splits IN into == or below threshold
- Both directions respect the same type-aware thresholds and PK override

title: feat: [ExternalTable Part3] Support manual refresh for external collections
body:
design doc: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus-design-docs/blob/main/design_docs/20260105-external_table.md

issue: #45881

This change introduces manual refresh capability for external
collections, allowing users to trigger on-demand data synchronization
from external sources. It replaces the legacy update mechanism with a
more robust job-task hierarchy and persistent state management.

Key changes:
- Add RefreshExternalCollection, GetRefreshExternalCollectionProgress,
  and ListRefreshExternalCollectionJobs APIs across Client, Proxy,
  and DataCoord
- Implement ExternalCollectionRefreshManager to manage refresh jobs
  with a 1:N Job-Task hierarchy
- Add ExternalCollectionRefreshMeta for persistent storage of jobs and
  tasks in the metastore
- Add ExternalCollectionRefreshChecker for task state management and
  worker assignment
- Implement ExternalCollectionRefreshInspector for periodic job
  cleanup
- Use WAL Broadcast mechanism for distributed consistency and
  idempotency
- Replace legacy external_collection_inspector and update tasks with
  the new refresh-based implementation
- Add comprehensive unit tests for refresh job lifecycle and state
  transitions
  
design doc: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus-design-docs/blob/main/design_docs/20260105-external_table.md
author: weiliu1031

title: fix: handle NaN vs None comparison in output field value check for nullable fields
body:
## Summary

- Fix `output_field_value_check` in `param_check.py` to handle NaN vs None comparison for nullable fields
- When nullable fields contain `None` values, `pandas.DataFrame` converts them to `NaN`. The check function then fails comparing `NaN` (from DataFrame) with `None` (from Milvus search results). This fix treats both as equivalent null values.
- Remove resolved issue reference comments (`#47065`)

**Related issue**: #47065

### Changes

**Modified Files:**
- `tests/python_client/check/param_check.py`: Add NaN/None equivalence check before asserting output field values
- `tests/python_client/milvus_client/test_add_field_feature.py`: Remove resolved `#47065` comment
- `tests/python_client/milvus_client/test_milvus_client_collection.py`: Remove resolved `#47065` comment

### Test Plan

- [x] `test_search_by_pk_with_output_fields_and_consistency_level[Strong/Session/Bounded/Eventually]` - 4 previously failing tests now PASS
- [x] All 98 search_by_pk tests: 93 passed + 4 fixed + 1 skipped
- [x] 13 null/nullable vector related tests all PASS

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: yanliang567

commits.message:
fix: handle NaN vs None comparison in output field value check for nullable fields

When nullable fields contain None values, pandas.DataFrame converts them
to NaN. The output_field_value_check function then fails because it
compares NaN (from DataFrame) with None (from Milvus search results).
This fix treats both as equivalent null values.

Also removes resolved issue reference comments (#47065).

Signed-off-by: yanliang567 <82361606+yanliang567@users.noreply.github.com>

title: test: fix snapshot checker row count mismatch in chaos test
body:
## Summary
- Split snapshot testing into two checkers to fix row count mismatch failures in chaos tests
  - **SnapshotChecker** (`Op.snapshot`): lightweight, shares collection with other checkers, only verifies snapshot create/restore operations succeed
  - **SnapshotRestoreChecker** (`Op.restore_snapshot`): uses independent collection with internal DML operations, verifies data correctness (row count + PK) after restore
- Removed `_snapshot_lock` from `Checker` class and all DML checkers (Insert/Upsert/Delete) to eliminate coupling

## Root Cause
The `SnapshotRestoreChecker` shared a collection with other concurrent checkers and relied on a global `_snapshot_lock` to prevent data modifications during snapshot creation. However, some code paths (e.g., `Checker.insert_data()` base method, `DeleteChecker.update_delete_expr()` refill logic) bypassed the lock, causing row count mismatches after restore. The delta was exactly 3000 (`DELTA_PER_INS`).

Failure log: `expected=158188, actual=155188` (delta=3000)

ref: https://qa-jenkins.milvus.io/blue/organizations/jenkins/chaos-test-cron/detail/chaos-test-cron/23943/pipeline

## Test plan
- [ ] Run chaos-test-cron pipeline and verify `Op.snapshot` (shared collection) passes
- [ ] Verify `Op.restore_snapshot` (independent collection) passes with no row count mismatch
- [ ] Confirm other checkers (insert/upsert/delete) are not impacted by lock removal
author: zhuwenxing

commits.message:
fix: decouple SnapshotRestoreChecker from concurrent checkers

Split snapshot testing into two checkers:
- SnapshotChecker: lightweight, shares collection, only verifies
  snapshot create/restore operations succeed
- SnapshotRestoreChecker: uses independent collection with internal
  DML operations, verifies data correctness after restore

Remove _snapshot_lock from Checker class and all DML checkers
(Insert/Upsert/Delete) to eliminate coupling that caused row count
mismatch failures (expected=158188 actual=155188, delta=3000=DELTA_PER_INS)

Signed-off-by: zhuwenxing <wenxing.zhu@zilliz.com>

title: fix: change queryPreExecute merge logic from row-based to column-based
body:
issue: #46953 
- GenNullableFieldData only supported scalar types, causing error when partial upsert after adding nullable vector fields

issue: #47160  
- pickFieldData used row index directly to access Contents array without converting to data index, causing index out of range panic

relate: #45993


Row-based processing had two problems:
- Nullable vector handling was tightly coupled with other field types, requiring complex special-case logic
- Update path was inefficient for nullable vectors: data had to be read first then updated in-place. For example, changing a valid vector to null requires shifting subsequent data since null values are not stored, which is very inefficient.

Column-based processing solves both:
- Each field type is processed independently, nullable vector logic is cleanly separated from other types
- Nullable vectors are appended directly without read-then-update, avoiding expensive data shifting operations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                - Iterate over fields instead of rows when merging upsert and existing data                                                                                                                                         

key changes:
- Remove nullable vector special handling: nullableVectorMergeContext, buildNullableVectorIdxMap, rebuildNullableVectorFieldData, etc.                                                                                                                                                   
- Use generic column utilities: AppendFieldDataByColumn, UpdateFieldDataByColumn                                                                                                                                    
- Add vector type support to GenNullableFieldData                                                                                                                                                                   
- Add unit test for nullable vector upsert scenarios
author: marcelo-cjl

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request refactors the upsert logic to be column-based, which is a good enhancement for performance. It introduces new utility functions `AppendFieldDataByColumn` and `UpdateFieldDataByColumn`. My review found some critical issues in the new implementation related to the handling of nullable vectors in full-format vs. compressed-format, which could lead to data corruption. There's also a potential issue in a new utility function that could lead to incorrect behavior if used improperly. I've detailed these issues in the comments below.

commits.message:
change queryPreExecute merge logic from row-based to column-based

Signed-off-by: marcelo-cjl <marcelo.chen@zilliz.com>

add checking existence for existPKToIndex

Signed-off-by: marcelo-cjl <marcelo.chen@zilliz.com>

title: fix: memory safety issues for nullable fields in growing and sealed segments
body:
issue: #47160 
issue: #47666 

relate: #45993 

commit 1
ThreadSafeValidData::get_data() previously returned a const reference  without holding the lock, causing two issues:
- Data race: concurrent read/write without synchronization.
- Use-after-free: even with a lock, returning a reference allows callers to hold it after the lock is released. Concurrent writers calling set_data_raw() may resize the underlying vector, invalidating the reference and causing memory access violations.

commit 2
For nullable columns, the offsets passed to BulkValueAt and similar  methods are physical offsets (indexing only valid/non-null rows), but ToChunkIdAndOffset maps them as logical offsets (indexing all rows including nulls). This mismatch causes incorrect chunk/offset resolution. Add ToChunkIdAndOffsetByPhysical() which uses cumulative valid row counts per chunk to correctly map physical offsets, and use it in BulkValueAt, BulkPrimitiveValueAt, and BulkVectorValueAt for both ChunkedColumn and ProxyChunkColumn when the column is nullable.

commit 3
- CachedSearchIterator used chunk_row_nums() (total logical rows) as chunk_size, but nullable vector chunks only store valid vectors. This caused out-of-bounds reads during brute force search. 
- SearchOnSealedColumn's iterator_v2 path was missing TransformOffset after NextBatch, returning physical offsets instead of logical offsets
author: marcelo-cjl

commits.message:
return valid_data by value with shared_lock to fix data race

Signed-off-by: marcelo-cjl <marcelo.chen@zilliz.com>

use physical offset mapping for nullable columns in bulk access

Signed-off-by: marcelo-cjl <marcelo.chen@zilliz.com>

fix heap-buffer-overflow in brute force search with nullable vectors

Signed-off-by: marcelo-cjl <marcelo.chen@zilliz.com>
